title: Find a Gateway (CatalogBuilderPanel) cannot be dismissed — no backdrop click, no Escape, × unreachable after results render
description:
Operator converged-build smoke walk (2026-06-08): 'The Find a Gateway window has no way to close it or click off of it after it's opened and any of the functions are used.' Operator rates this BEYOND a P0 — fatal UX = a functional crash. Second time a fresh feature has shipped a no-dismiss modal.

ROOT CAUSE (verified on origin/main, src/catalog/CatalogBuilderPanel.tsx):
- The overlay root <div className='catalog-builder-overlay' role='dialog'> has NO onClick={onClose} backdrop handler. So clicking off the panel does nothing ('no way to click off of it').
- There is NO Escape-key handler anywhere in the component.
- The only dismiss path is the × button (.catalog-builder__close, onClick={onClose}). Operator reports that after fetching stations / using any function, the × is unreachable — likely the panel/overlay grows past the viewport with no sticky header / no scroll containment, pushing the × off-screen (needs CSS confirmation: overlay+panel max-height, sticky header, z-index/pointer-events).

ASYMMETRY TO COPY: the SIBLING panel src/catalog/CatalogRequestPanel.tsx already does this correctly — overlay div has onClick={onClose} with an inner stopPropagation wrapper, plus a visible close button. CatalogBuilderPanel was built without that pattern.

FIX DIRECTION: (1) add onClick={onClose} to the overlay + onClick={e=>e.stopPropagation()} on the inner .catalog-builder (backdrop dismiss); (2) add an Escape-key handler (useEffect keydown → onClose); (3) make the close affordance always reachable — sticky header within a scroll-contained panel (panel max-height + overflow:auto on the body, header stays pinned). Browser-smoke the post-results state specifically (feedback_browser_smoke_before_ship).

PREVENTION: this is the 2nd no-dismiss-modal in a fresh feature — worth a testing-pitfalls entry (every inline-overlay must have backdrop-dismiss + Escape + an always-reachable close, asserted in a test). Source: PR #465 (Catalog Builder / Find a Gateway, tuxlink-a2gd).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-29zx-catalog-dismiss. Branch: bd-tuxlink-29zx/catalog-dismiss. Created 2026-06-09 02:10:28 UTC by delta-bog-cypress.
close_reason: Closed

title: Contacts grid collapses — ContactsPanel jammed into MessageList column, radio dock slides into detail area
description:
Operator converged-build smoke walk (2026-06-08): the Contacts feature is completely broken and unusable. The render grid 'collapses to the left' and 'drags the radio modem all the way into where the contacts list should be.'

ROOT CAUSE (verified on origin/main):
- src/shell/AppShell.css: .panes uses grid-template-columns: 200px 380px 1fr (and .panes--with-dock: 200px 380px 1fr 400px). The grid tracks are sized for the 3/4-child message layout (FolderSidebar | MessageList | reading-pane | [RadioPanel dock]).
- src/shell/AppShell.tsx (~line 929): when selectedFolder === 'contacts', a single <ContactsPanel/> is rendered as grid CHILD 2. A single grid child only occupies ONE track (the 380px MessageList column) unless told to span. So ContactsPanel is crushed into 380px on the left and the 1fr (+400px dock) tracks are wrong.
- With the radio dock on (panes--with-dock), RadioPanel becomes child 3 and lands in the 1fr track (the contacts-detail area) instead of the 400px dock track — i.e. the radio modem 'slides into where the contacts list should be.' Exactly the operator symptom.
- The mount comment claims the early-return 'wraps both the MessageList column AND the reading pane', but that is not true in CSS-grid terms for a single child.

FIX DIRECTION (for the implementing session): give the contacts state its own grid template — e.g. a .panes--contacts modifier collapsing to 200px 1fr (and accounting for the dock track), OR have the ContactsPanel wrapper span grid-column: 2 / -1 so it occupies the full MessageList+reading-pane(+dock) union. Verify with a real browser smoke per feedback_browser_smoke_before_ship (grid layout is exactly the class of bug static review misses).

Source: converged-build operator smoke walk; regression introduced by PR #472 (Contacts+Favorites, tuxlink-raez).

title: [P0] Fix Logging window usability, styling, and clear-history size behavior
description:
Smoke walk of the working converged build surfaced a P0 cluster in the new Logging window. This was not caused by the current Codex logging-boundary PR; it is a first-time smoke finding against the working converged build. Symptoms: (1) once opened, the Logging window does not render controls to close it, making it functionally sticky; (2) the window styling does not match the rest of Tuxlink despite operator concern during spec/design; (3) log history/export minimum size appears to be nearly 1 MB, which is large for alpha issue upload; (4) clicking Clear History increases the minimum size by roughly 2 KB per click and cannot reset to 0 without manually deleting log files from the filesystem. Use repo bug-hunter/systematic-debugging discipline: reproduce and trace root cause before proposing fixes; use TDD before production changes. After fixing, smoke walk a simulated CMS comms failure and evaluate the filed issue/logging evidence for alpha needs.

## Required Skills
investigate,systematic-debugging,test-driven-development
acceptance_criteria: Logging window has visible/usable close controls and remains accessible after open; styling matches the established Tuxlink shell/window conventions; Clear History is idempotent and resets operator-visible history/log archive size appropriately without filesystem manual deletion; repeated Clear History clicks do not grow persisted logs; simulated CMS comms failure can file/export an issue with useful diagnostic evidence and no excessive baseline log bloat; targeted tests and smoke evidence are recorded.
notes: Implemented in commit 9da09d6 by yew-sorrel-condor. Root causes verified: logging_window.rs is borderless but LoggingView lacked custom Tuxlink chrome/resize handles; logging_clear_history preserved the active JSONL and emitted a warning after clear, so disk usage could not reset and grew on repeated clicks. Fix: LoggingView now uses shared Tuxlink chrome/titlebar/resize handles/theme tokens; Clear History flushes pending writes, truncates the active JSONL, deletes closed JSONLs, preserves unrelated files, and returns retryable error if the active log path mutex is busy. Gates: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml logging::commands::tests -- --nocapture (17 passed); pnpm vitest run src/help/LoggingView.test.tsx src/help/LoggingExportSection.test.tsx (16 passed); pnpm vitest run src/help/ReportIssueModal.test.tsx src/shell/chrome/dispatchMenuAction.test.ts (48 passed; existing React act warning only); pnpm typecheck (passed after offline frozen pnpm install in worktree); pnpm build (passed, existing Vite dynamic-import/chunk-size warnings). Safe CMS smoke assessment: docs/live-cms-testing-policy.md bars agent-run live CMS/RF; verified Report Issue/dispatch tests and code path only. Operator manual smoke should use local/invalid CMS endpoint or explicit consent for any live CMS run.
close_reason: Implemented by yew-sorrel-condor in commit 9da09d6 and merged via PR #436 (merge commit 7f63878). All GitHub required checks passed: verify amd64/arm64 and release build amd64/arm64. Agent-side safe smoke covered Report Issue/dispatch tests and code-path review; remaining converged-build GUI/operator smoke is tracked separately as tuxlink-t8c0 because live CMS/RF and GUI smoke require operator control/consent.

These entries should not appear in the operator-facing connection log. Investigate via the local investigate workflow, prove the log routing root cause, then fix the boundary so diagnostic/runtime telemetry stays out of the connection log without suppressing legitimate diagnostics elsewhere.

## Required Skills

title: [P0 WLE parity] Prompt operator to select inbound messages to download (bandwidth/time budget)
description:
Smoke test 2026-06-05 against cms-z: tuxlink currently downloads ALL available inbound messages on connect without prompting. WLE prompts the operator with a list of pending messages and lets them select which to pull — critical for emcomm scenarios with limited bandwidth or time budgets where downloading everything would exhaust the link.

Critical WLE parity feature. P0 because emcomm users on bandwidth-constrained links (HF, ARDOP, packet) cannot trust tuxlink as a replacement until this exists.

Discovered during smoke test of latest converged build against cms-z.winlink.org (Cameron, 2026-06-05).
notes: SHIPPED to main: feature via PR #480 (merged by operator 2026-06-08T23:42Z); connect-path staleness bug found in the Task-9 cms-z smoke (no prompt — auto-download to Inbox) FIXED via PR #482 / tuxlink-u9z8 (merged 2026-06-09T02:02Z, commit 9dd1cc07). REMAINING: operator Task-9 cms-z dev-smoke on the warm main build — Hold/Delete semantics (BLOCKER), 45s socket-idle, abort, preference-OFF, multi-batch. Close after smoke passes. The fix makes the toggle take effect on the very next connect regardless of toggle timing, so the startup-window staleness that caused the no-prompt symptom will not recur.

title: HTML Forms alpha-candidate completion: P1 frontend + P2 native auto-fill + P3 catalog/custom-form polish

Forms must be EXCELLENT, not parity-checkbox. The bar is alpha-tester readiness.

title: [P0/perf] Infinite theme-broadcast loop pegs CPU when help window is open
description: Operator (2026-06-03): tuxlink WebKitWebProcess and Rust backend both pegged (>40% each) at idle when help window is open. Root cause: theme broadcast cycle. (1) Help window's useHelpTheme.ts:28 listens for 'color_scheme_changed'. (2) On event, it calls applyColorScheme(payload). (3) applyColorScheme in src/shell/colorScheme.ts:231-238 invokes 'theme_broadcast_scheme' — fire-and-forget. (4) Rust theme_broadcast_scheme in src-tauri/src/theme_state.rs:36 calls app.emit('color_scheme_changed', scheme) which broadcasts to ALL windows INCLUDING THE SENDER (Tauri default). (5) Help window's listener fires AGAIN with the same payload → goto 2. Infinite IPC + DOM-mutation loop. Each iteration: one Tauri command + DOM write + JS event callback. Pegs both processes. Fix: add a  option to applyColorScheme (default true to preserve menu-flip + startup broadcast behavior); useHelpTheme's two callsites pass {broadcast: false} since they're responding to a broadcast and must not echo.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-och6-theme-broadcast-loop. Branch: bd-tuxlink-och6/theme-broadcast-loop. Created 2026-06-03 23:08:04 UTC.
close_reason: PR #338 merged. Broke the cross-window theme-broadcast loop by giving applyColorScheme a {broadcast?: boolean} opt and having useHelpTheme's two callsites pass false.

title: [bug] help window crashes with 'No QueryClient set' — App.tsx help branch missing QueryClientProvider
description: Operator smoke 2026-06-03 of merged PR #312 (tuxlink-0gsy): help window opens but renders nothing; WebKit console shows 'Error: No QueryClient set, use QueryClientProvider to set one' followed by 'An error occurred in the <HelpView> component'. Root cause: App.tsx wraps QueryClientProvider only around <AppShell />; the isHelpWindow + isComposeWindow branches mount their components without it. HelpView's useHelpSearch hook calls useQuery (from @tanstack/react-query), which throws without a provider. Tests passed because HelpView.test.tsx + useHelpSearch.test.ts wrap their own QueryClient in the test render — classic dependency-injection-by-test that left the production mount path unsupported. Fix: lift QueryClientProvider up so all branches get it. Add a regression test that mounts App with window.location.pathname='/help' and asserts no error is thrown.

title: App white-screens on launch — dependabot PR #252 bumped react without react-dom
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED CRITICAL REGRESSION (2026-06-02).

Symptom: launching tuxlink (tauri dev OR production) shows a white screen with no content. React refuses to mount.

Root cause: PR #252 (dependabot) bumped 'react' from 19.2.6 to 19.2.7 + '@types/react' from 19.2.15 to 19.2.16, but did NOT bump 'react-dom' (still at 19.2.6). React 19 enforces strict version parity between 'react' and 'react-dom' — peer mismatch throws an uncaught runtime exception at mount time:

  Error: Incompatible React versions: The 'react' and 'react-dom' packages must have the exact same version. Instead got:
    - react:      19.2.7
    - react-dom:  19.2.6
  Learn more: https://react.dev/warnings/version-mismatch

Captured via headless chromium + CDP against http://localhost:1420 of the converged build.

Fix:

1. Bump react-dom from 19.2.6 to 19.2.7 in package.json
2. Bump @types/react-dom to match react-dom (current spec says ^18.3.0 — this is also stale; should be ^19.2.x)
3. Run 'pnpm install' to regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml
4. Verify: 'pnpm exec tsc --noEmit' clean + 'pnpm vitest run' clean
5. Verify: headless chromium loads localhost:1420 and #root has content after mount

Follow-up hardening (defer to P2 bd):
- Configure dependabot's 'groups' so react + react-dom + @types/react + @types/react-dom always bump together. This SHOULD prevent the same class of bug from recurring. Edit .github/dependabot.yml to add a 'react-monorepo' group:
    groups:
      react:
        patterns:
          - 'react'
          - 'react-dom'
          - '@types/react'
          - '@types/react-dom'

Discovered: 2026-06-02 operator-smoked the converged build after merge of #252 / release 0.17.0.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-ola6-react-dom-version-sync. Branch: bd-tuxlink-ola6/react-dom-version-sync. Created 2026-06-02 21:53:21 UTC by plover-willow-basalt.

title: Position UI/on-air locator conflation — ribbon shows config_grid not live GPS fix under LocalUiOnly
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED REGRESSION (post tuxlink-c79g merge).

Symptom: with config { position_source: Gps, gps_state: LocalUiOnly, identity.grid: 'DM33', position_precision: SixCharGrid } and a fresh GPS fix (verified: gpsd producing TPV mode=3 at ~1Hz, tuxlink connected, arbiter.has_fresh_fix()=true), the ribbon displays 'DM33' instead of the live precision-reduced fix grid 'DM33ww'.

Root cause: effective_broadcast_locator in src-tauri/src/position/mod.rs:18-49 conflates two distinct concerns:
  (a) ribbon DISPLAY = what to show the operator locally
  (b) on-air LOCATOR = what gets transmitted (privacy-gated by gps_state)

The Codex P1-B finding from the prior position-subsystem-restoration 5-round adrev (PR #215 / tuxlink-c79g) intentionally collapsed (a)+(b) into one helper, with rustdoc 'the ribbon always shows exactly what is/would be transmitted.' That collapse is the design error: under LocalUiOnly + source=Gps + fresh fix, the operator's literal intent ('see GPS locally, don't broadcast') is broken because the local display falls back to config_grid.

Fix (corrected Path A, validated by Codex consultation 2026-06-01):

Backend:
  - position/mod.rs: add effective_ui_locator(config, arbiter):
      source=Gps + fresh fix + gps_state in {LocalUiOnly, BroadcastAtPrecision} → arbiter.broadcast_grid() (live fix at precision)
      source=Gps + (no fresh fix OR gps_state=Off) → config_grid fallback
      source=Manual → config_grid
  - Keep effective_broadcast_locator unchanged (on-air gating preserved).
  - Update rustdoc on broadcast helper: it is single source of truth for on-air ONLY.
  - ui_commands.rs PositionStatusDto: add ui_grid: String. Populate via effective_ui_locator.

Frontend:
  - useStatus.ts: add ui_grid to PositionStatusDto type. liveGrid reads ui_grid (not broadcast_grid).
  - GridEdit.tsx: clean stale pjih-era comments.

Spec amendment (SAME PR, first commit per propagation contract):
  - docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-position-subsystem-restoration-design.md
  - Amend vocabulary, §2.5, §3.1, §3.2, §3.4, §6.1, §6.4
  - Replace 'ribbon equals on-air locator' with 'ribbon equals local display locator; on-air locator remains separately privacy-gated'

title: REGRESSION fix(position): chip stuck GPS-green + accepts manual input but doesn't switch — pjih over-removed operator agency
description:
Operator-reported 2026-06-01 post-PR #189 merge: 'GPS is now fully broken. It doesn't switch to manual entry with [the prior] gps available [affordance], it just says GPS green at all times and accepts/displays whatever input. That's major regression.'

ROOT CAUSE (suspected — needs investigation)
PR #189 (tuxlink-pjih) decoupled set_manual from source-pinning and removed the explicit Use-GPS button from GridEdit, on the assumption that effective_source derived live from arbiter state would be sufficient signal. That over-removed operator agency:
  - The chip now reads positionStatus.active_source (derived: Gps when fresh fix, Manual otherwise).
  - With gpsd publishing fixes, effective_source=Gps permanently → chip is permanently GPS-green.
  - The operator can no longer FORCE Manual mode for an explicit on-air override.
  - Typing in the grid updates config.identity.grid + arbiter.manual_grid; under gps_state != BroadcastAtPrecision the broadcast falls back to config.identity.grid, so the display 'accepts whatever input' even though chip says GPS.

REQUIRED FIX (shape — operator decides)
Restore explicit operator agency over source selection. Options:
  (a) Restore the use-gps button + reintroduce stored source as the chip's primary signal (closest to pre-pjih, but pjih's stuck-in-Manual bug returns).
  (b) Add an explicit Manual ↔ GPS toggle separate from grid-edit; chip shows operator's choice; arbiter respects it.
  (c) Hybrid: chip shows effective source by default; affordance to override (force Manual / force GPS) lives in the source chip itself or in Settings.

SEVERITY P0 — operator-visible major regression caused by my PR #189.

title: RADIO-1: packet connect is unsafe on-air — unbounded airtime, abort can't halt TX, pre-connect DISC
description: INCIDENT 2026-05-22: a packet dial over the new RFCOMM socket ran ~110s of repeated SABM keying with no reachable abort; operator had to power OFF the radio to stop it. This is a RADIO-1 violation. Do NOT dial packet (any transport) until ALL of the below land. Found via Codex adrev (dev/adversarial/2026-05-22-ax25-socket-rx-runaway-codex.md, gitignored). FIX BUNDLE: (1) Bounded airtime — split the connect SABM-retry policy from data N2; one SABM + at most one retry + a HARD total-elapsed cap (~20-30s) regardless of n2_retries. (2) Abort before every transmit — AbortableByteLink::write must check the flag (currently only read() does, link.rs:80); connect() must check abort before push_kiss_params and before EACH SABM (currently TXes at top of each attempt); never key after Cancel. (3) No pre-connect DISC — connect() builds Ax25Stream{closed:false} before the link is up, so a failed dial (DM/timeout/abort) drops the stream -> Drop -> disconnect() -> sends DISC + waits T1 (datalink.rs:714). Only arm teardown after a UA is received. (4) REVERT the T1 floor (config.rs into_params MIN_RF_T1_MS, added this session) — it tripled worst-case airtime (3s->10s per retransmit) and was the wrong lever. (5) Build/test on task-amd-main-ui (has the abort + resize UI); main lacks them. Blocks tuxlink-uhc and tuxlink-nx2.
close_reason: Landed on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate (PR pending): ka7+p5u via live-config refresh on config_set_*; efo via lenient packet.link deserialize + TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR; 2y4 RADIO-1 bundle (bounded airtime + abort-before-write + no pre-connect DISC + T1-floor revert + KISS nibble), Codex-cleared (4 invariants), TOCTOU refinement tracked in tuxlink-0ja. 338 lib + 453 vitest green.

title: Find a Gateway dialog stacks below app chrome — header/× unreachable when tall (follow-up to tuxlink-29zx)
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-29zx (PR #484, merged): the backdrop/Escape dismiss landed and made the dialog non-fatal, but the underlying layout bug remained. Operator smoke: when Find a Gateway is tall (station results loaded) its header + × land 'outside the top of the main window' and cannot be used (the dialog can now be dismissed by clicking off it, but the controls are still unreachable).

ROOT CAUSE: src/catalog/CatalogBuilderPanel.css overlay shipped at z-index 50 — BELOW the app chrome: menubar z-index 90, dropdown 100, resize handles 200 (src/shell/chrome/chrome.css). A tall panel (max-height 88vh, centered → top edge ~6vh) puts its header band inside the titlebar/menubar region; the higher-z chrome paints over it. The sibling CatalogRequestPanel (dismisses fine) uses z-index 1000; this file's comment claims it 'matches CatalogRequestPanel.css' but the z-index drifted to 50.

FIX: raise the overlay to z-index 1000 (above all chrome; matches sibling + Grib + userFolders modals) + a regression assertion that the overlay z-index exceeds every app-chrome z-index. Sibling defect InboundSelectionPanel (z-index 50) tracked separately as tuxlink-76zy.

title: Find a Gateway is buried in the Message menu + bundles non-gateway features — relocate to / invoke from the radio modem
description:
Operator converged-build smoke walk (2026-06-08): 'Message -> Find a Gateway is very unintuitive. This was the catalog parser but it got filed under the Message menu which doesn't make sense to a user and includes features in it which are solidly not Find a Gateway. Why is that buried in a sub-menu at all instead of built into the radio modem or invocable from there, the place where people need to find gateways?'

CURRENT STATE (origin/main):
- src/shell/chrome/menuModel.ts: { id: 'menu:message:catalog_builder', label: 'Find a Gateway…' } — lives under the Message menu (tuxlink-a2gd). Opens CatalogBuilderPanel as an inline overlay.
- Information architecture mismatch: 'Find a Gateway' is a MODEM-context action (you find a gateway in order to connect a radio session), but it sits in the Message (compose/mailbox) menu.
- SCOPE BLEED: CatalogBuilderPanel mixes true gateway-finding (location + station modes + radius → distance-sorted station list) WITH an 'Also request (by message)' section that queues INQUIRY messages for Area weather / Propagation / Winlink info — items the operator says are 'solidly not Find a Gateway.' Those overlap conceptually with the separate CatalogRequestPanel (Message → Catalog Request…).

RECOMMENDED DIRECTION (confirm with operator — this is an IA/placement change, candidate for a short brainstorm before rebuild):
1. Surface gateway-finding FROM the radio modem panel (a button/affordance in the ARDOP/Packet/VARA panels where the operator is about to dial), not (only) buried in the Message menu. The need arises at connect time.
2. Split scope: keep the station-finder (location/mode/radius → results) as 'Find a Gateway'; move the by-message info-category requests (area weather / propagation / winlink info) out of it — fold them into the existing Catalog Request feature (menu:message:catalog_request) where in-band INQUIRY requests already live. One coherent 'request info by message' surface; one coherent 'find a station to dial' surface.

This needs a placement/IA decision (and likely a brief brainstorm per the project's UI-before-build discipline) before implementation. NOT a pure code fix. Touches menuModel.ts (+menuModel.test.ts EXPECTED_IDS), CatalogBuilderPanel, the radio panels, and CatalogRequestPanel. Source: PR #465 (tuxlink-a2gd).

title: Favorites/Recent added to all modem modes indiscriminately — Telnet CMS shouldn't have it; active-tab style is generic/inconsistent
description:
Operator converged-build smoke walk (2026-06-08): 'We added Favorites and Recent to all radio modem modes indiscriminately. That's wrong. Telnet Winlink CMS doesn't need this.' Plus: 'the button style highlight used is Claude-default and not consistent with the rest of the project.'

PART A — wrong modes (src/favorites/FavoritesTabs.tsx, design M7):
- Current M7 design: ardop-hf / packet / telnet → Favorites|Recent|Manual tabs; vara-hf / vara-fm → Manual only (isVara() guard).
- Operator correction: Telnet Winlink CMS should NOT get Favorites/Recent either. Telnet connects to a fixed CMS host — there is no 'pick a nearby station to dial' affordance to favorite, unlike ARDOP/Packet (the genuine pre-dial modes). Principle: Favorites/Recent belongs only where the operator chooses among many dial targets (ARDOP/Packet). Telnet + VARA = Manual only.
- FIX: extend the manual-only exclusion to cover telnet (e.g. broaden the isVara guard to an isManualOnly(mode) covering vara-hf, vara-fm, telnet), and drop the Favorites/Recent mount from TelnetRadioPanel. Update the M7 design note + the FavoritesTabs / panel tests (menuModel/contract tests may assert the tab set).

PART B — tab styling (src/favorites/FavoritesTabs.css):
- .favorites-tab-trigger[data-state='active'] uses color: var(--accent) + border-bottom-color: var(--accent) (a plain underline on the generic accent). Operator reads the active-tab highlight as 'Claude-default' / generic and inconsistent with the rest of the project chrome.
- FIX: align the active-tab treatment with the project's existing tab/segmented-control styling elsewhere in the app (match the established design tokens + active-state visual language; not a bare accent underline). Design-review the active state against sibling controls.

Source: PR #472 (Contacts+Favorites, tuxlink-raez/egmp).

title: fix: inbound-review prompt never fires (gated on stale live_config, not fresh disk pref) — PR #480 follow-up
description:
Found during the tuxlink-bsiy Task-9 cms-z dev-smoke (operator, 2026-06-08): with the 'Review pending messages before downloading' preference enabled, a real CMS connect downloads everything straight to Inbox with no selection prompt.

Root cause: native_connect gated the selecting decider on self.live_config().review_inbound_before_download — the backend's in-memory config snapshot, seeded from disk at startup and refreshed only by set_config, which config_set_review_inbound skips when the backend is not yet installed (if let Some(backend) = state.current()). Enabling the brand-new preference before the backend finished installing (common on a fresh build) writes true to disk but leaves live_config false, so the connect takes the accept-all arm. cms_connect reads the correct fresh disk value but discarded it.

Fix (verified green + clippy --all-targets clean in the bsiy worktree, captured as a patch): cms_connect builds the CmsSelectionContext only when the freshly-read cfg.review_inbound_before_download is on; native_connect selects the prompting decider on the context's presence alone. Existing loopback integration test flipped to pin the regression (live_config false + context present must still prompt).

PR #480 (tuxlink-bsiy) was merged to main by the operator before the smoke completed, so the fix lands as a fresh PR off main per ADR 0017 (bsiy branch is merged-dead).
notes: PR #482 open off main (https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/482). Fix committed de32878. Verified: regression test passes + clippy --all-targets clean on identical code in the bsiy worktree before patch-transfer to main. Awaiting CI both arches, then merge + smoke Task-9 gates on warm main.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #482 (merge 9dd1cc07, both arches green), merged to main by operator 2026-06-09T02:02Z. Connect-path staleness fix: cms_connect gates the selection context on the fresh disk preference; native_connect selects the decider on context presence, not stale live_config. Operator Task-9 cms-z smoke now runnable on warm main.

title: Remediate public-OSM ingestion: strip OSM TileLayer from compose PositionMapWidget + revert OSM CSP allowlist, use bundled static fallback
description: Operator-mandated 2026-06-08: 'fix anything else which is ingesting public OSM tiles and not shipping static assets we download one time, like a low view static worldmap fallback layer.' The shipped src/compose/PositionMapWidget.tsx loads https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png directly in the webview (PR #392), and src-tauri/tauri.conf.json CSP whitelists tile.openstreetmap.org in img-src + connect-src (PR #420 / closed tuxlink-bt2q). This VIOLATES the locked never-public-OSM posture and the operator's item-11c WLE-ban concern. Remediation: remove the OSM TileLayer + the CSP allowlist; replace with the bundled static fallback layer from the foundation issue (same offline-first source chain). Effectively reverts tuxlink-bt2q. DEPENDS-ON the foundation issue (needs the bundled static asset + gatekeeper to exist as the replacement). No RF path.

Build the OFFLINE-FIRST, NEVER-PUBLIC-OSM map subsystem the locked spec mandates: (a) reuse EXISTING Maidenhead converter (src-tauri/src/position/maidenhead.rs + src/forms/position/maidenhead.ts) — do NOT rebuild; (b) ship a bundled static low-zoom world-map asset (one-time download, served from 'self', CSP stays locked) as the required offline fallback layer; (c) Rust backend tile-gatekeeper — webview never fetches tiles directly; backend fetches only from an operator-configured permitted server (opt-in) and serves to the webview; public OSM is NOT an option in the chain; (d) GridMapPicker React component — pin mode (item 18) + GRIB-box drag mode (item 21), toggleable Maidenhead grid overlay, honors 4-char broadcast-precision default. title: FZ-M1 compact shell polish: overlay drawer + grid-expand bug + rail affordances + ribbon alignment
description:
Operator browser-smoke of merged PR #464 (tuxlink-h7q7) found 4 defects requiring a polish pass before the compact shell is alpha-quality:
1. Radio drawer PUSHES (shrinks reader to ~452px) instead of OVERLAYING. Operator decision: overlay, not push — the tiny reading pane is operationally useless. Must coexist with the form-viewer child Tauri webview (occlusion).
2. Grid implodes when the left rail expands: .sidebar.is-expanded{position:absolute} drops the sidebar from grid auto-flow, shifting MessageList->col1 (squished), reader->col2, leaving col3 a void.
3. Collapsed-rail controls contextless: 4 of 5 folders share the same '▢' icon (indistinct); bare '+' add-folder + unlabeled connection rows aren't discoverable.
4. Ribbon GridEdit (GPS/grid source switcher) + folder '+' button touch/overlap neighbors; poor alignment from the 44px-min-height bump in the tight flex ribbon.

description:
Audit wf_2b5b6659 (2026-06-07, basalt-mesa-dahlia) found 5 Codex fixes that landed on origin/main but are PARTIAL under adversarial review — each fixes the common case but misses a production sub-path the finding covered. This umbrella tracks the 4 code-fixable gaps (item 28 is design-gated, tracked separately):

- Item 39 (tuxlink-35v6, CLOSED): Compose From reads cfg.callsign only; renders BLANK for offline-path operators. Fix: resolve via callsign||identifier (reuse formatCallsign from src/shell/useStatus.ts). cfg.identifier already on config_read DTO.
- Item 38 (tuxlink-479c, CLOSED): ribbon connection chip special-cases packet; ARDOP/VARA revert to generic 'Idle · <configTransport>' label in idle-after-close. Fix: derive ribbon label from activeConnection for ardop-hf/vara-hf/vara-fm (DashboardRibbon.tsx + AppShell.tsx).
- Item 24 (tuxlink-088s, IN_PROGRESS): decode_b2f_body_text all-or-nothing from_utf8 gate can re-introduce mojibake on mixed-encoding bodies (one stray byte flips whole body to Latin-1). Fix: byte-wise decode keeping valid UTF-8 runs, CP-1252-map only invalid bytes. Add valid-UTF-8 + mixed-encoding tests.
- Item 4 (tuxlink-wiwb, IN_PROGRESS): emit_exchange_result_progress not threaded into Telnet P2P (ui_commands.rs telnet_p2p_connect ~5474) — operator can't see per-message detail. Fix: build outbound_log + call emit_exchange_result_progress; add test.

description: Operator feedback on 2026-06-06: Codex work was moving faster than the Claude-established workflow. Direct commits carried Agent trailers, but GitHub-generated merge commits did not; Codex did not have Claude skills such as build-robust-features / bug-hunter installed and failed to explicitly surface/substitute a formal equivalent; AGENTS.md did not provide enough load-bearing non-Claude workflow parity.\n\nScope: codify a Codex primary-agent parity checklist, make missing-skill substitution explicit, add harness-independent Agent trailer enforcement for local commits, update AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md pointers, and audit open Codex-authored PRs before resuming product work.

- AGENTS.md explicitly points non-Claude agents at the parity workflow.
- CLAUDE.md contains the canonical rule/pointer so AGENTS.md is not the only source.
- A repo-local parity checklist covers build-robust-features and bug-hunter substitutes when actual skills are unavailable.
- Local git hooks enforce Agent trailers for any locally-created commit, independent of Claude PreToolUse hooks.
- Existing open Codex/delegated PRs are audited for monikers and validation gaps before more product work continues.

title: [bug] PR #401 regression — B2F messages still show binary garbage; placeholder never triggers
description:
Operator smoke 2026-06-05 (post PR #401 merge): the binary-content placeholder added in PR #401 never triggers on real CMS-Z Catalog image responses. The body still renders as a wall of REPLACEMENT-CHARACTER glyphs interleaved with surviving ASCII.

Root cause: PR #401 fixed `find_text_plain_body`'s fallback for when mail_parser returns `PartType::Binary`. But real Winlink inbound messages aren't MIME — they're **B2F wire format**:

  Mid: 9YMNP4GB4UOD
  Body: 580                  ← decimal byte count of text body
  File: 26999 600x600.jpg    ← byte count + filename per attached file
  From: SERVICE
  Subject: INQUIRY ...

  <580 bytes of text body>
  <26999 bytes of binary JPEG>

When mail_parser ingests this:
- B2F headers look syntactically like RFC 5322 headers
- Default Content-Type implicit text/plain  
- Everything after the blank line (text + binary) becomes one big text body
- msg.body_text(0) returns Some(lossy-utf8-of-text-plus-binary)
- PR #401's Binary-fallback never triggers

The project already has a B2F parser at `src-tauri/src/winlink/message.rs::Message::from_bytes` that correctly handles the `Body: N` byte-count + per-attachment `File: N filename` structure. The fix is to detect B2F (presence of Mid: header, or attempt B2F parse first) and use `Message::body()` to get just the declared body bytes.

Scope:
- Modify find_text_plain_body in ui_commands.rs to try B2F parsing first (when raw bytes are available)
- Update the call site to pass raw bytes
- Add regression test using a real-shape B2F message with text + binary attachment
- Verify the existing PartType::Binary fallback (from PR #401) still works for true single-part MIME

Closes after fix.

title: GPS pkexec helper binary + PolicyKit policy + 'Fix it for me' wiring (slice 2)
description:
Activates the "Fix it for me" buttons shipped disabled in tuxlink-9xy1 (bd-1). Ships Dave's magic-button experience — the "screenshot for the club Slack" milestone.

Depends on: tuxlink-9xy1 (bd-1).

## Scope

### Helper binary `/usr/libexec/tuxlink-gps-fix`

Small Rust binary (~50 LOC) with a fixed action enum:

- `add-dialout` → `usermod -aG dialout $REAL_USER` (resolves via pkexec's $PKEXEC_UID environment variable).
- `mask-modemmanager` → `systemctl mask ModemManager`.
- `unmask-modemmanager` → `systemctl unmask ModemManager` (reversibility commitment).

Helper rejects unknown actions and refuses to execute without $PKEXEC_UID set. No arbitrary command execution. Source is auditable.

### PolicyKit policy

Ship at `/usr/share/polkit-1/actions/com.tuxlink.app.policy`:

```xml
<action id="com.tuxlink.app.gps-fix">
  <description>Tuxlink wants to adjust GPS-related system configuration</description>
  <message>Tuxlink would like to fix a GPS configuration issue. This will require entering your password. You can reverse the change from Settings → Location → Troubleshoot.</message>
  <icon_name>com.tuxlink.app</icon_name>
  <defaults>
    <allow_active>auth_admin</allow_active>
  </defaults>
  <annotate key="org.freedesktop.policykit.exec.path">/usr/libexec/tuxlink-gps-fix</annotate>
</action>
```

### Bundle wiring

`linux.deb.files` in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json ships both the helper binary and the policy file. AppImage gracefully degrades — no PolicyKit policy can be registered with the system from inside an AppImage, so the "Fix it for me" buttons hide on AppImage installs with the explanation "PolicyKit not available — use Show command".

### Tauri-side spawner

Rust command that:
- Invokes `pkexec /usr/libexec/tuxlink-gps-fix <action>`.
- Captures exit code + stderr.
- Emits a Tauri event with the result (success / auth-dismissed / action-failed / pkexec-not-installed).
- Re-runs the relevant detection probe so the UI auto-refreshes.

### Post-fix UX for dialout

- Action completes → UI shows: "Done. You need to log out and back in for this to take effect — that's a Linux rule we can't bypass. Want me to log you out now?"
- "Log out now" invokes `loginctl terminate-session` (preferred) or `gnome-session-quit --logout` as fallback. Both are user-session operations, no sudo required.
- If user defers logout, dashboard pill stays amber until next login confirms group membership (bd-1 detects on startup).

## Tests

- Helper binary stdout contract (`action: ok` / `action: failed: <reason>`).
- PolicyKit policy syntax validation (`pkaction --action-id com.tuxlink.app.gps-fix --verbose` should parse cleanly).
- Rust-side error handling for pkexec exit codes:
  - 0 → action succeeded.
  - 1 → action failed (read helper stderr for reason).
  - 126 → auth dialog dismissed.
  - 127 → pkexec not installed.

## Acceptance criteria

- Dave clicks "Fix it for me" on the dialout card → PolicyKit auth dialog renders with the tuxlink icon + policy message → password entered → command runs → "log out and back in" notice appears.
- ModemManager mask works identically; the reverse (`Unmask ModemManager`) is reachable from the Settings → Location → Troubleshoot tab even after the fix succeeded.
- pkexec absent / disabled → "Fix it for me" buttons hidden with explanation.
- Helper binary refuses execution without $PKEXEC_UID set.

## Effort

3-4 days eng time.

title: GPS source-picker React component + Settings → Location wiring (slice 1)
description:
First slice of the GPS setup UX redesign (design doc: docs/design/2026-06-05-gps-setup-ux-design.md). Ships Bob (gpsd-pre-configured), Mike (manual-grid fixed-station), and the diagnostic half of Dave's path. Sue gets manual source-switching but not auto-detected NMEA yet.

## Scope

Build the shared React component (e.g. `GpsSourcePicker`) that renders in two chromes:
- Wizard Step 4 (Welcome / Callsign / Winlink account / **Location** / Test send / Done).
- Settings → Location panel with four tabs: Troubleshoot / Source picker / Manual grid / Privacy.

The component runs detection probes in parallel and renders source-cards (working sources) or triage-cards (blocked sources). Manual grid is always available as a card.

## Detection probes (all unprivileged Rust → Tauri commands)

- `gps_probe_gpsd()` — TCP connect to 127.0.0.1:2947 with a 200 ms timeout for wizard probe (existing src-tauri/src/position/gpsd.rs:run_gpsd_client refactored).
- `gps_probe_serial_devices()` — readdir `/dev/ttyACM*` + `/dev/ttyUSB*`; for each, `udevadm info -q property <path>` to extract ID_VENDOR_FROM_DATABASE, ID_MODEL_FROM_DATABASE, ID_VENDOR_ID, ID_MODEL_ID.
- `gps_probe_dialout_membership()` — `id -Gn` against `getent group dialout`, or `nix::unistd::Group::from_name("dialout")` + `getgroups()`.
- `gps_probe_modemmanager_status()` — D-Bus session-bus query for `org.freedesktop.ModemManager1` presence; fall back to `systemctl is-active ModemManager` via `zbus` if D-Bus probe inconclusive.
- (Optional bd-1 stretch) `gps_probe_bluetooth_nmea()` — BlueZ device enumeration. OK to defer to bd-3 if scope balloons.

## UI surfaces

- **Wizard Step 4 chrome:** Back / Continue buttons; "Use this" pre-selected when a green source-card exists.
- **Settings → Location chrome:** four tabs (Troubleshoot / Source picker / Manual grid / Privacy); Settings sidebar shows Profile (Callsign / Winlink account / Location) + App (Theme / Privacy) only — radio modem config stays in the modem pane per operator architecture call.
- **Triage cards** render "Show command" buttons that expand the exact sudo command + Copy button.
- **"Fix it for me" buttons present but disabled** with tooltip "Coming in next release" — they activate in bd-2.
- **Manual grid editing** with 4-char / 6-char precision-reduction radio (per `[[gps-precision-reduction]]` memory).
- **Source switching** without app restart — PositionArbiter accepts source changes at runtime.

## Out of scope

- The pkexec helper binary (bd-2).
- Native NMEA reader (bd-3) — gpsd-only sources for now.
- Live detection monitoring (bd-4) — probes run on wizard step entry + Settings open + Rescan click only.

## Acceptance criteria

- Bob's wizard path: gpsd detected, "Use this" pre-selected, one Enter completes Step 4.
- Mike's wizard path: "Skip — I'll enter my grid manually" available as a first-class option from any state, no GPS timeout required.
- Dave's wizard path: triage cards render with dialout / ModemManager / no-device diagnoses. "Show command" reveals copy-pasteable sudo command. "Fix it for me" disabled with "Coming soon" tooltip.
- Settings → Location panel: same picker, same triage, same source-meta rendering. Three tabs (Troubleshoot / Source picker / Manual grid) + Privacy as a fourth.
- Detection probes run in <500 ms p95 (parallel; gpsd probe bounded to 200 ms).
- Manual grid validation reuses src-tauri/src/position/maidenhead.rs.
- All unit tests for probe functions pass; component-level tests cover each persona's path.

## Mockup

docs/design/mockups/2026-06-04-gps-setup-mocks.html — States A/B/C/D in the wizard, plus Settings → Location → Troubleshoot and Settings → Location → Source picker.

## Effort

~1 week eng time for a careful pass.

title: Intent-filtered Outbox drain: MessageMeta needs routing_flag storage (Codex P1#3)
description:
Codex Phase 3-4 boundary review found P1 routing gap that allows wrong-session message leakage.

Location: src-tauri/src/winlink_backend.rs:2165

Mechanism: B2F engine drains every Outbox item regardless of ExchangeConfig.intent. routing_flag() exists on SessionIntent but is only covered by tests/comments — it's not actually read by the drain. In a P2P or radio-only VARA/ARDOP exchange, CMS/R-pool/unflagged messages can be offered over the wrong RF session, contrary to spec §3 capability matrix.

This is operationally dangerous: a CMS message could ride out over a radio-only peer link (or vice versa), with off-spec semantics + potential operator confusion + compliance/forensics issues.

Proposed fix (full): add MessageMeta.routing_flag: Option<RoutingFlag> storage. Tag at write-path: compose-form picks based on intent (Cms→C, P2p→none, RadioOnly→R, etc.); inbound dispatch tags based on the routing flag the inbound session carried. Read-path filter in the B2F drain: only propose messages matching intent.routing_flag().

Proposed fix (temporary safety gate, if full refactor is multi-session): block non-CMS exchange paths with an error until routing flags exist — prevents the leakage at the cost of disabling P2p + RadioOnly outbound until the proper fix lands.

This was independently flagged by Task 3.4's implementer subagent before Codex; both confirm the gap.

Codex transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-phase3-4-boundary-codex.md (gitignored)
Spec ref: §3 capability matrix (Cms→C, P2p→none, RadioOnly→R routing flags).
notes:
Pattern B (safety gate) chosen, NOT Pattern A. See commit 98a0164 +
its commit message for full rationale.

Why Pattern B:
- Pattern A (MessageMeta.routing_flag schema + tag at compose/inbound +
  filter at drain) would cascade into 2 sibling MessageMetaDto types
  (ui_commands + search), the search index extractor + likely the
  SQLite schema, an on-disk RFC5322 header convention, and 4+
  inbound-store sites that need to mint the flag from session intent.
- Cascade exceeded what one dispatch can land cleanly under the
  alpha-polish push.

What landed:
- `build_outbound_proposals(mailbox, intent)` — new intent arg.
  Non-CMS intents (P2p / RadioOnly / PostOffice / Mesh) return
  BackendError::MessageRejected with a diagnostic that self-identifies
  as a safety gate + names this bd issue.
- 5 drain call sites updated: run_ardop_b2f_exchange (DIAL —
  propagates), run_vara_b2f_exchange (DIAL — propagates),
  run_ardop_b2f_answer (ANSWER — degrades to empty), run_vara_b2f_answer
  (ANSWER — degrades to empty), telnet_listen::run_b2f_answerer
  (ANSWER — degrades, existing pattern), telnet_p2p_connect (DIAL —
  propagates). Also: inline drain loop in run_ardop_b2f_exchange
  replaced with the shared helper (had been duplicating the loop).
- 5 new unit tests assert the gate fires for each non-CMS intent +
  one positive test pins CMS as unchanged.
- 1049 lib tests passing (was 1038 baseline; +11).

Gate-lift path (when this bd issue ships):
- Delete the early-return safety-gate block at the top of
  build_outbound_proposals.
- Change the body from "drain all" to
  "drain only meta.routing_flag == intent.routing_flag()".
- All call sites already pass intent — no call-site changes needed.

DO NOT close this bd issue. Parent (Codex review) will close after
confirming the safety gate is adequate for the alpha cut.

Coordination note: shared worktree with sibling tuxlink-pdnw dispatch.
Sibling's commits 0f96291 + 341b858 + 49f81ff + ecfc177 landed during
this session in modem_status / modem_commands / vara_commands /
ui_commands (listener consumer task funcs). Zero file-line overlap;
the one shared file (ui_commands.rs) had me at line ~4028 and the
sibling at lines ~2580/2739/3231/3375 — clean split.

title: modem_vara_b2f_exchange returns session to Closed instead of Open (Codex P1#2)
description:
Codex Phase 3-4 boundary review found P1 lifecycle violation in VARA b2f.

Location: src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/commands.rs:1355-1357

Mechanism: a successful VARA Send/Receive currently always drops the transport and calls vara_stop_session_inner, which clears active_intent/active_transport_kind and returns the panel to Closed. Spec §2 says 'outbound dial is within-session' — B2F should return to open/idle or listener-armed, not stop the session.

This breaks redial/listener continuity: the operator can't retry without re-opening the session.

Proposed fix: refactor modem_vara_b2f_exchange to mirror Task 3.6's ARDOP pattern. Take_transport → connect (already in place) → exchange → install_transport back to keep VaraState::Open. The session's active_intent/active_transport_kind stays populated; the listener (if armed) can re-arm.

Note: P1#1 (close-vs-consumer race) may interact with this fix — implement the close-generation flag before this so the install_transport-back path can detect close-already-won.

Codex transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-phase3-4-boundary-codex.md (gitignored)
Spec ref: §2 'Outbound dial is within-session' + §5 state machine.

title: Close-vs-armed-consumer race in ARDOP + VARA lifecycle (Codex P1#1+#4+#5)
description:
Codex Phase 3-4 boundary review (2026-06-04) found 3 P1 race conditions in the close-vs-consumer path on both ARDOP + VARA. Same mechanism class — fix together.

P1#1 (ARDOP, modem_commands.rs:1000): if Close Session is clicked while modem_ardop_b2f_exchange owns the transport after take_transport(), ardop_close_session_inner aborts and reset_to_stopped() clears the session, but the unconditional install_transport reinstall runs when the aborted exchange unwinds. Leaves a live transport in a session the operator just closed.

P1#4 (VARA, vara/commands.rs:1167-1169): when listener is armed, vara_close_session_inner's disarm only sets the consumer's shutdown flag, then immediately proceeds to abort/stop. Consumer still owns the transport; its normal drain path later calls return_transport, restoring VaraState::Open after Close has returned.

P1#5 (ARDOP, modem_commands.rs:638): same race as P1#4, ARDOP side. ardop_set_listen_inner(false) only signals; modem_ardop_disconnect_inner resets the session while the consumer still owns the transport. Consumer's shutdown path unconditionally install_transports it back. Close Session can leave ardopcf running with a transport reattached after status reads Stopped.

Proposed fix: introduce a 'closing' generation counter or atomic flag on both ModemSession + VaraSession. Consumer's drain path checks the flag before reinstalling; on 'closing' it drops/shutdowns the transport instead of returning it. close_session sets the flag before disarm/abort, then waits for consumer to drain (bounded timeout) before completing.

Codex transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-phase3-4-boundary-codex.md (gitignored)
Spec ref: §5 state machine — closed state must be terminal until next open_session.

title: Allowlist OSM tile host in tauri.conf.json CSP for PositionFormV2 map
description:
The PositionFormV2 map widget (PR #392, src/compose/PositionMapWidget.tsx) loads tiles from https://*.tile.openstreetmap.org. Production tauri.conf.json's CSP restricts img-src to 'self' data:, so remote OSM tiles are blocked. The map renders with the existing graceful fallback (blank canvas + Maidenhead grid overlay via Leaflet tileerror event handler).

Surfaced by 2026-06-04 Codex full-diff adrev P2 finding.

Operator-decided UX (atoll-basin-crag session 2026-06-04): 'Leaflet + offline tiles strategy. Tile source = OSM online + graceful blank-canvas + Maidenhead grid-square overlay fallback when offline.' The OSM-online half of that decision can't take effect under the current CSP.

Two paths:
(a) Add 'https://*.tile.openstreetmap.org' to the img-src directive in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json. Cleanest from UX perspective. Needs privacy review (the request reveals the operator's map view to OpenStreetMap).
(b) Ship local tile pack and skip the remote tile load entirely. Filed separately as a P3 (richer offline UX). Until then, only the grid-square overlay shows.

Operator decision needed before merge of PR #392 or follow-up. Refs: tuxlink-hnkn.

close_reason: Functional CSP allowlist was already present on main via commits 0918948/3542e77; PR #420 adds a regression test asserting OSM tile hosts remain allowed in img-src while preserving self/data image sources. Verified with positionMapCsp vitest, typecheck, and git diff --check.

title: Decide WLE schema alignment for CheckInForm + re-enable registration
description:
The Task 3 CheckInForm implementation (PR #392, commit 80c5ff5) chose a simplified 7-field schema (tactical_call, op_name, group_net, status, comments, grid, initials) that does NOT match the bundled WLE Winlink_Check_In_Initial.html template's ~18-field schema (msgto, newsubject, organization, datetime, msgsender, contactname, assigned, status, service, band, session, location, maplat, maplon, mgrs, grid, locationsource, comments). Semantic names diverge — there is no tactical_call field in WLE; the closest equivalent is MsgSender.

Surfaced by 2026-06-04 Codex full-diff adrev P1 finding (dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-p2-full-diff-codex.md). Per the Task 1 wire-format-mismatch lesson, a misaligned form silently mis-renders on the receive side AND is not recognized as a standard Winlink Check-In by CMS / other clients.

Defensive disposition in PR #392 (commit b22c533): CheckInForm registration commented out in src/forms/index.ts. Picker falls through to existing WLE webview Check-In form (P1 behavior, unchanged from main). CheckInForm code stays in the repo (src/compose/CheckInForm.tsx + 15 tests + view + registry entry) for operator review. The CATALOG_ID_ALIASES alias for Winlink_Check_In_Initial → Winlink_Check-In is pre-installed so re-enable is a one-line toggle.

Three resolutions on the table:
(a) Full WLE schema alignment — rebuild FIELDS array + form fields + tests against the WLE template. Larger form (~18 fields). Most faithful.
(b) Keep simplified 7-field UI but map to WLE payload at submit time. CheckInForm UX stays the same, onSubmit transforms fields to WLE shape. Easier wins but loses 11 of the 18 WLE fields.
(c) Ship the simplified subset and have CMS / other Winlink clients treat the form as a generic message instead of a Check-In. Lowest fidelity. Probably not acceptable.

Need operator decision before re-enabling. Refs: tuxlink-hnkn.

title: tuxmodem-tx --watchdog: spawn tux-rig-watchdog for SIGKILL-safe TX (Phase 1.5 slice 2)
description:
Integrate the tux-rig-watchdog binary (PR #378 / tuxlink-23ps, now on main) with tuxmodem-tx as a new --watchdog flag for the full-TX mode. Makes the on-air TX path SIGKILL-safe end-to-end without operator-side coordination.

Scope:
- Add Args.watchdog: bool field + --watchdog parser branch to tuxmodem-tx
- When set, before opening LinuxTty/RtsPtt directly, spawn tux-rig-watchdog as a child process:
  - Pass --device <ptt_path> --max-seconds <ceil(airtime_budget * 2)> args
  - Pipe stdin to the child so OS closes it when tuxmodem-tx exits
  - Wait for child to assert PTT (block briefly until it logs 'asserting PTT' on stderr OR a short timeout elapses)
- The audio path proceeds as normal (no PTT toggle from tuxmodem-tx; the watchdog holds it)
- On normal exit: tuxmodem-tx finishes → drops stdin pipe → child sees EOF → releases PTT
- On SIGKILL of tuxmodem-tx: OS closes the pipe → child sees EOF → releases PTT (the whole point)
- Validation: child's return code reported to operator after wait

Test coverage (hardware-free via process-spawn mocks):
- --watchdog flag parses + propagates to TxOptions
- Spawn logic accepts child path override for tests
- Wait-for-ready times out if child fails to assert within budget
- Drop semantics: tuxmodem-tx's exit drops the stdin pipe

Out of scope:
- Multi-rig watchdog coordination (one watchdog per call site)
- PR_SET_PDEATHSIG variant (could fold in later as parallel safety)
- Heartbeat protocol on stdin (EOF-based is sufficient for SIGKILL case)

Depends on: PR #378 (watchdog binary) MERGED, PR #377 (--frame-mode multi-sync) MERGED. Both confirmed merged 2026-06-04.

Safety: this is exactly the SIGKILL-safe upgrade RADIO-1 + the bd-i3bz description called for. Library-tested + smoke-tested locally without real device; operator runs against real radio under the existing per-invocation consent gate.

title: tuxmodem-tx + tuxmodem-rx: --frame-mode multi-sync (Phase 10 slice 3)
description:
Wire the multi-symbol + preamble composition (PR #375 / tuxlink-k2xv — transmit_multi_with_preamble + receive_multi_with_sync) into both CLIs as a new FrameMode::MultiSync variant. Unlocks arbitrary-length payloads (up to u16::MAX bytes) through the fully agent-runnable CLI loopback.

Scope:
- Both tuxmodem-tx and tuxmodem-rx FrameMode gains a third variant: MultiSync.
  Existing: Raw (no preamble, single symbol), Sync (preamble + single symbol).
  New: MultiSync (preamble + N symbols with 2-byte length-prefix header).
- --frame-mode multi-sync accepted by both binaries (alongside existing raw and sync).
- tuxmodem-tx::encode_payload routes MultiSync through transmit_multi_with_preamble.
- tuxmodem-rx::decode_one_symbol routes MultiSync through receive_multi_with_sync.
- Backwards compat: raw and sync semantics unchanged.

Acceptance criterion: CLI loopback with 100-byte payload in multi-sync mode produces CLEAN MATCH:

  tuxmodem-tx --write-wav /tmp/x.wav --payload '<100-byte text>' \
              --mode wide-floor --frame-mode multi-sync
  tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav /tmp/x.wav --expected '<same 100-byte text>' \
              --frame-mode multi-sync

Test coverage:
- frame_mode_parse accepts 'multi-sync'
- encode_payload multi-sync output length = preamble + N symbols
- decode multi-sync roundtrip recovers payload (1 byte / 9 byte / 100 byte / 1000 byte)
- backwards compat: raw and sync still work
- end-to-end WAV roundtrip via write_wav/read_wav

Depends on: PR #371 + PR #373 + PR #374 + PR #375 (all merged into main).

Out of scope:
- New on-air verification (operator-driven, RADIO-1)
- BER vs SNR sweeps (PHY Phase 11)

title: tux-rig-watchdog: SIGKILL-safe PTT release via stdin-EOF detection (Phase 1.5 slice 1)
description:
New binary tux-rig-watchdog in the tux-rig-rts crate that provides SIGKILL-safe PTT release for the tuxmodem program.

The problem: tux-rig-rts's Drop impl releases PTT cleanly on SIGINT/SIGTERM/panic-unwind, but SIGKILL skips Drop. Without external safety, a SIGKILL of tuxmodem-tx mid-transmission can leave the radio keyed until power-cycled.

The solution: a separate process that holds the PTT line. When the parent dies (any reason including SIGKILL), the OS closes the pipe → watchdog detects stdin EOF → releases PTT and exits. Kernel-mediated, works through SIGKILL because the OS closes the pipe automatically.

Scope (slice 1, lib-only/standalone):
- New binary tux-rig-watchdog --device <PATH> [--max-seconds N]
- Opens PTT, asserts immediately, holds for up to N seconds (default 30, hard cap 60 mirroring tux-rig-rts)
- Releases PTT on: max-seconds elapsed, SIGINT/SIGTERM received, OR stdin EOF detected
- Logs state transitions to stderr (assert at start, release with reason at end)
- The watchdog runtime exposes a testable trait-parametrized loop so we can unit-test the orchestration without real /dev/ttyUSB or real signals

Integration with tuxmodem-tx (spawn watchdog + pipe heartbeats / EOF) happens in slice 2 — out of scope here.

Test coverage (hardware-free via MockTtyWriter):
- watchdog asserts PTT on startup
- watchdog releases PTT when stdin-EOF flag is set (simulated)
- watchdog releases PTT when signal flag is set
- watchdog releases PTT when max-duration elapses (clock injected)
- the loop pattern is deterministic given mocked inputs (signal flag, stdin flag, clock)
- Drop still backstops if all else fails (existing tux-rig-rts test pattern)

Out of scope (slice 2+):
- Integration with tuxmodem-tx (spawn + pipe coordination)
- PR_SET_PDEATHSIG variant (Linux-only safety upgrade)
- Multi-rig watchdog (one watchdog per rig)
- Unix-socket protocol (overkill for slice 1)

Depends on: nothing (tux-rig-rts is on main and stable).

Safety profile: this binary does not transmit; it only toggles the RTS line. The radio keys when RTS asserts (per existing tux-rig-rts behavior). Operator owns the per-invocation consent gate per RADIO-1. Agent codes + commits + operator runs against real device.

title: tuxmodem-phy multi-symbol + preamble composition (Phase 10 slice 2)
description:
Compose Phase 10 slice 1 (transmit_multi/receive_multi, tuxlink-cwjp / PR #374) with Phase 12 slice 1 (preamble round-trip, tuxlink-iyl9 / PR #371) so multi-symbol payloads can travel through the over-the-air frame format.

Scope:
- WidebandLowDensityFloor::transmit_multi_with_preamble(&[u8]) -> Result<Vec<f32>, PhyError>
  Output: [preamble (192 samples)][multi-symbol body (N × symbol_size_samples)]
  Trivially: preamble samples + transmit_multi(payload) samples.
- WidebandLowDensityFloor::receive_multi_with_sync(&[f32]) -> Result<(usize, Vec<u8>), PhyError>
  Scans for preamble (via existing PreambleDetector), then calls receive_multi on the post-preamble samples. Returns (preamble_start, payload).

Tests:
- preamble + multi roundtrip for various payload sizes (1, 9, 100, 1000 bytes)
- leading-silence offset (preamble at sample 1000)
- pure silence rejects with FrameDetect
- truncated input after preamble rejects with FrameDetect
- empty payload roundtrip (1 symbol, len=0)

Library-only. CLI integration in slice 3.

Depends on: PR #371 (preamble round-trip) merged + PR #374 (multi-symbol primitive) merged or stacked.

title: tuxmodem-phy multi-symbol framing primitive (Phase 10 slice 1)
description:
Add multi-symbol framing to WidebandLowDensityFloor so payloads can exceed the current ~9-byte single-symbol capacity. Library-only slice; CLI integration (--frame-mode multi-sync or similar) follows in a separate PR.

Scope:
- New WidebandLowDensityFloor::transmit_multi(&[u8]) -> Result<Vec<f32>, PhyError>
  Splits payload into chunks of (data_capacity_per_symbol - HEADER_LEN) bytes after prepending a 2-byte big-endian u16 length header. Each chunk becomes one OFDM symbol; output is the concatenation. Maximum payload: u16::MAX (65535 bytes).

- New WidebandLowDensityFloor::receive_multi(samples: &[f32]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, PhyError>
  Decodes the first symbol, reads the length header, determines how many additional symbols to decode, then decodes the remainder and trims to declared length.

- Both APIs assume aligned input (no preamble integration in this slice). Phase 10 slice 2 will compose with PR #371's preamble round-trip for transmit_multi_with_preamble/receive_multi_with_sync.

Header format:
- Bytes 0-1: u16 big-endian payload length (excludes header)
- Bytes 2..: payload bytes

Sample-count math:
- HEADER_LEN = 2 bytes
- For Wide mode at BPSK: data_indices.len() / 8 ≈ 9 bytes per symbol (matches single-symbol cap)
- payload_bytes_per_symbol = 9 - HEADER_LEN_IN_FIRST_SYMBOL = 7 (first symbol)
- subsequent symbols carry full 9 bytes each
- symbols_needed = 1 + ceil((payload.len() - 7) / 9) when payload > 7 bytes, else 1
- Result length = symbols_needed * symbol_size_samples

Test coverage:
- 1-byte roundtrip (1 symbol)
- 5-byte (1 symbol, fits with header)
- 7-byte (boundary: exactly first-symbol capacity)
- 8-byte (boundary: 2 symbols, last has 1 byte)
- 10-byte (2 symbols)
- 100-byte (~12 symbols)
- 1000-byte (~111 symbols)
- Empty payload roundtrip (1 symbol with len=0)
- Payload exceeds u16::MAX rejects cleanly
- Decoder rejects truncated input

Out of scope:
- Preamble integration (slice 2)
- CRC / FEC (Phase 8 wires in FecCodec from subsystem #4)
- CLI changes (slice 3 follow-up)
- Multi-symbol mixed-mode (different OFDM mode per symbol — Phase 7+)

Spec inputs:
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-clean-sheet-modem-3-phy-waveform.md
- PR #371 (tuxlink-iyl9) precedent for additive lib-only slices

Depends on: nothing (tuxmodem-phy main is current).

title: tuxmodem-tx + tuxmodem-rx: --frame-mode raw|sync CLI wiring (Phase 12 slice 2)
description:
Wire the preamble round-trip primitive (PR #371 / tuxlink-iyl9 — WidebandLowDensityFloor::transmit_with_preamble + receive_with_sync) into the tuxmodem-tx and tuxmodem-rx binaries via a new --frame-mode raw|sync flag.

Default to raw for backwards compatibility with the v0.0.1 wire format (one OFDM symbol, no preamble). sync mode adds the 192-sample Zadoff-Chu preamble on the TX side and uses receive_with_sync to find the symbol in arbitrary-length captures on the RX side. Removes tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav's 'first symbol_size samples only' caveat (operator no longer needs to manually trim).

Scope:
- tuxmodem-tx: new Args.frame_mode field; --frame-mode raw|sync arg. Default raw. When sync, encode_payload routes through transmit_with_preamble. Airtime budget extends by 192 / 48000 ≈ 4 ms.
- tuxmodem-rx: same flag. When sync, decode_one_symbol routes through receive_with_sync; the returned start_sample is reported alongside the decoded bytes for operator diagnostics.
- Both CLIs print 'frame-mode: sync' in their output banner when in sync mode.
- USAGE help text updated.

Test coverage:
- Arg parsing: --frame-mode raw / --frame-mode sync / default (raw) / unknown name reject
- tuxmodem-tx sync mode encodes preamble + symbol (sample count = 192 + symbol_size)
- tuxmodem-rx sync mode decodes preamble + symbol correctly (roundtrip)
- tuxmodem-rx sync mode handles leading-silence WAV (operator captures more than just the symbol)
- Backwards compat: --frame-mode raw on both CLIs == pre-Phase-12 behavior

CLI smoke (agent-safe):
- tuxmodem-tx --write-wav /tmp/sync.wav --payload 'TEST' --mode wide-floor --frame-mode sync
- tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav /tmp/sync.wav --expected 'TEST' --frame-mode sync
- Expected: CLEAN MATCH

Out of scope (still tuxlink-9ggl umbrella):
- Multi-symbol framing (PHY Phase 10)
- Mixed-mode raw + sync interop (sync receivers reading raw WAVs and vice versa — error gracefully but no auto-detect)
- AGC / CFO refinements (PHY Phase 4)

Depends on: PR #371 (tuxlink-iyl9) MERGED. Confirmed merged at 2026-06-04 11:11 UTC.

title: tuxmodem-phy preamble round-trip primitive (Phase 12 slice 1)
description:
Wire the existing Zadoff-Chu PreambleGenerator + PreambleDetector (tuxmodem-phy::sync::preamble) into a new round-trip primitive on WidebandLowDensityFloor. Library-only slice; no CLI changes (those follow in a subsequent PR once tuxlink-xvrb + tuxlink-4dv9 merge).

Scope:
- Add WidebandLowDensityFloor::transmit_with_preamble(&[u8]) -> Result<Vec<f32>, PhyError> that produces [preamble samples (192)][OFDM symbol samples (CP + body)] for a single-symbol payload.
- Add WidebandLowDensityFloor::receive_with_sync(&[f32]) -> Result<(usize, Vec<u8>), PhyError> that scans for the preamble, slices the symbol that follows, demods, and returns (preamble_start_sample, payload).
- Existing transmit / receive APIs unchanged — backwards compatible.

Why: today tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav (PR #367) requires the operator to manually trim the WAV to exactly one symbol's worth of samples. With preamble-based sync, the receiver can find the symbol in an arbitrary-length capture, removing the manual-trim step.

Limitation: still single-symbol only — multi-symbol framing is PHY Phase 10 (separate slice). This slice closes ONE half of the 'realistic on-air workflow' gap: preamble alignment. Multi-symbol payloads remain capped at ~9 bytes.

Test coverage (hardware-free):
- transmit_with_preamble + receive_with_sync roundtrip recovers payload (no offset)
- receive_with_sync handles a leading-silence offset (preamble appears at sample 1000, not 0)
- receive_with_sync handles a trailing-silence tail (preamble at sample 0, then noise after the symbol)
- receive_with_sync returns Err / detection-failed on a buffer with no preamble (pure noise)
- transmit_with_preamble produces a sample count of PREAMBLE_LEN (192) + symbol_size (FFT + CP)

Out of scope:
- CLI wiring in tuxmodem-tx / tuxmodem-rx (follow-up PR after this lands + PRs #367 + #369 merge).
- Multi-symbol framing (PHY Phase 10, separate slice).
- AGC / CFO estimation refinements (already have separate stubs in sync/).
- BER vs SNR sweeps (Phase 11).

Spec inputs:
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-clean-sheet-modem-3-phy-waveform.md §3 forcing function 3 (shared sync infra)
- tuxmodem/crates/tuxmodem-phy/src/sync/preamble.rs (existing primitive)
- tuxmodem/crates/tuxmodem-phy/src/robustness_floor/wideband_lowdensity.rs (where new methods land)

title: tuxmodem-rx: capture + demod + BER CLI (Phase 4 of tuxmodem hardware bring-up)
description:
Phase 4 of the tuxmodem hardware bring-up umbrella (tuxlink-9ggl). The receive complement to Phase 3's tuxmodem-tx (tuxlink-i3bz). Captures audio from a CPAL input device, demodulates one OFDM symbol via WidebandLowDensityFloor::receive, prints the decoded payload + optionally a BER vs an expected reference.

Scope:
- New binary tuxmodem-rx (probably its own crate to mirror tuxmodem-tx).
- Pipeline: AudioInput captures N samples at 48 kHz f32 mono → WidebandLowDensityFloor::receive(samples) → bytes → print/compare.
- Modes: --listen (capture until SIGINT or N seconds), --decode-wav <PATH> (read .wav and decode without device), --expected <text|@file> for BER comparison.

API surface:
- tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav <PATH> [--expected <ARG>] — fully agent-testable
- tuxmodem-rx --listen --device <NAME> [--max-secs <N>] [--expected <ARG>] — captures from real device

Required PHY extension:
- AudioInput sibling to AudioOutput in tuxmodem-phy::audio_device:
  - list_input_devices() (mirror list_output_devices)
  - AudioInput::open(Option<&str>) (mirror AudioOutput::open)
  - AudioInput::record_blocking_with_abort(N samples, &AtomicBool) -> Result<AudioBuffer, PhyError>

Out of scope:
- Continuous decoding loop with frame-sync — that's PHY Phase 12+.
- Multi-symbol framing — PHY Phase 10.
- AGC / channel-quality reporting beyond what RxFrame already carries.

Safety: 
- Pure capture. No PTT, no audio output. Agent can run --decode-wav end-to-end. Agent CAN run --listen on the operator's bench rig as long as no RF transmission is happening simultaneously (operator may run the rig in receive-only mode for this purpose).

Spec inputs:
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-clean-sheet-modem-3-phy-waveform.md
- tuxlink-i3bz (Phase 3 TX) — mirror its lib/bin layout + airtime budget pattern
- existing PHY: WidebandLowDensityFloor::receive at tuxmodem/crates/tuxmodem-phy/src/robustness_floor/wideband_lowdensity.rs:73

Why now: complements Phase 3; pure-receive class avoids the on-air-verification trap for RF-path abort changes (tuxlink-0ja lesson); unblocks operator's TX↔RX loopback validation (Pi audio out → loopback cable → Pi audio in → decode) which is the next-best thing to real off-air SDR observation.

title: tuxmodem-tx: payload → PHY → PTT + audio CLI (Phase 3 — the plug-into-radio milestone)
description:
Phase 3 of the tuxmodem hardware bring-up umbrella (tuxlink-9ggl). The "plug it into a radio" milestone — composes the Phase 1 PTT (tux-rig-rts) + Phase 2 audio (tuxmodem-phy::audio_device) + the existing PHY waveform into a single CLI that transmits real modulated tuxmodem data through a real radio.

Scope:
- New binary `tuxmodem-tx` (probably in tuxmodem-phy under a new feature `tx-cli` that depends on `audio-device`, OR as its own crate `tuxmodem-tx` — decide at start of session).
- Pipeline: payload bytes → tuxmodem-phy encoder → AudioBuffer (mono 48kHz f32) → tux-rig-rts PTT assert → audio_device::play_blocking → PTT release.
- PTT lead-in: assert PTT, then sleep ~100ms BEFORE the first sample reaches the soundcard. Without lead-in the first 50-100ms of the waveform goes out before the radio's TX chain is fully keyed; the radio chops the preamble.
- Bounded total airtime: cap derived from PHY mode + payload size; reject configurations that would exceed (default 30s; max ever 60s).
- SIGINT/SIGTERM during TX: trigger early-release (release PTT first, then exit; DON'T let the audio buffer finish playing).
- Mode selection: probably --mode wide-floor (the noise-floor robustness mode) for first slice; richer modes follow.

API surface:
- `tuxmodem-tx --payload <text-or-file> --mode <name> --device <audio-device-name> --ptt-device <tty-path>` — full transmission
- `tuxmodem-tx --dry-run ...` — encodes + reports duration but DOES NOT assert PTT or play audio (validates the pipeline without RF risk)

Operator validation (G90 + Digirig):
1. `tuxmodem-tx --dry-run --payload "TEST" --mode wide-floor` — verifies encode pipeline, reports waveform duration.
2. `tuxmodem-tx --payload "TEST" --mode wide-floor --device 'USB Audio Device' --ptt-device /dev/digirig` — actual on-air TX. Operator's SDR observes the waveform off-air on the G90's TX frequency.

Out of scope (separate phases):
- Phase 4: tuxmodem-rx CLI — receive + demod + BER (next after Phase 3).
- Subsystems #5/#6/#7: link/MAC/ARQ/adaptation for TWO-way modem-to-modem.
- The watchdog daemon (Phase 1.5) — separate process owning the PTT fd. Phase 3 ships with the in-process SIGTERM handler from tux-rig-rts; the daemon is the SIGKILL-safe upgrade.

Critical safety:
- This binary issues real on-air transmissions when invoked with the full arg set. Per RADIO-1 + project_g90_vara_standard_works_firsthand: operator is the licensee; agent codes + commits + operator runs the test. Agent MUST NOT run the binary against the real device.
- The PHY's wide-floor mode is the FIRST mode to test — designed for robustness; failure mode is "operator hears noise but signal carries" rather than "radio spends 30s pumping out garbage."
- Bounded airtime + bounded payload size + working abort + SDR observe before the on-air test — all per feedback_radio1_bounded_airtime_abort.

Spec inputs:
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-clean-sheet-modem-3-phy-waveform.md
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-clean-sheet-modem-overview.md
- docs/hardware/modem-test-rig.md (the FM rig spec — illustrative; HF is via Digirig, not DRA-100)
- ADR 0014 (clean-sheet bright line)

title: Plan revision: address Codex Round 1 P1 + P2 findings (tuxlink-0ye6)
description:
Codex Round 1 adrev (2026-06-04, dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-round1-design-coherence-codex.md) surfaced 6 P1 + 5 P2 findings against the plan + spec. Plan-fix bundle (no operator decisions; apply when operator unblocks):

P1-Codex-1: Plan needs to clarify ARDOP Connect-step location — spec says connect happens at Open Session OR at Connect; plan Task 3.5 says NO connect_arq at open. Existing modem_ardop_b2f_exchange assumes link is up. Fix: ARDOP Connect button does connect_arq + B2F + disconnect; ardop_open_session spawns ardopcf but does NOT connect_arq.

P1-Codex-4: Plan Task 4.1 wires VARA abort as DISCONNECT\r — wrong. VARA's command codec has separate ABORT and DISCONNECT. ABORT is hard teardown; DISCONNECT is graceful. Spec needs ABORT. Fix: send ABORT\r first, optionally DISCONNECT\r second; test captures ABORT as first cmd.

P1-Codex-5: Auto-arm + transport ownership conflict — current listener consumers take ownership of the transport for the armed window. Plan's outbound-with-listener-armed model has no transport arbiter. Fix: design backend session arbiter OR explicit disarm-during-outbound-rearm-after transition BEFORE shared-panel work.

P1-Codex-6: SessionIntent ALREADY EXISTS in src-tauri/src/winlink/session.rs with Cms/RadioOnly/PostOffice/Mesh/P2p variants. Plan Task 3.1 creates a duplicate. Fix: extend existing enum, don't create new file. Add serde derives if missing.

P2-Codex-7: Spec auto-arm WLE-parity framing is over-broad — repo's WLE-parity correction shows different rules per transport. Fix: rewrite spec § disparity by transport/intent; label as tuxlink design choice where decompiled evidence doesn't confirm WLE parity for the exact session type.

P2-Codex-9: Plan stores lifecycle in React local state. Doesn't recover from hot reload / ardopcf crash / VARA socket drop / rapid Open-Close. Fix: subscribe to backend status events; lifecycle derived from backend, not useState.

P2-Codex-10: varaHfAdapter and varaFmAdapter share command names; backend TransportKind::VaraHf vs VaraFm distinguishes. VARA FM sessions will be logged + gated as VARA HF. Fix: adapter sends transportKind + intent; tests for both HF and FM.

P2-Codex-11: Phase 1 ships broken interim commits (consent removed before replacement; tests red across phase boundaries). Fix: reorder so safeguard-strip lands LAST after Open/Close + ABORT + shared panel are green.

After operator unblocks decisions tuxlink-<consent-gate> / tuxlink-<airtime-bound> / tuxlink-<radio-only-scope>, revise spec + plan; then run Codex rounds 2-5; then subagent-driven impl.

title: Operator decision: airtime bound replacement (120s cap vs 600s wedge guard vs no cap)
description: Codex Round 1 adrev (2026-06-04) P1 finding: plan Task 1.5 replaces CONNECT_DEADLINE=120s with a 600s TCP-wedge guard. Codex notes ARQTIMEOUT=30 is link-idle, not connect-bound, so 600s is real airtime. Memory radio1-bounded-airtime-abort says airtime must be bounded. Memory no-tuxlink-added-safeguards says drop tuxlink-added caps. Operator shape call: which bound is correct? Options: (a) keep 120s, (b) 600s, (c) no cap + ABORT-only, (d) modem-native only. See dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-round1-design-coherence-codex.md.
notes: Operator decision 2026-06-04 (cypress-glade-peregrine): MODEM-NATIVE ONLY. Rely on ARQTIMEOUT (~30s link-idle) + working ABORT button as the only bound. No tuxlink-added 120s/600s cap. Aligns with feedback_no_tuxlink_added_safeguards + feedback_radio1_bounded_airtime_abort (abort halts TX = the bound). Plan Task 1.5: REMOVE CONNECT_DEADLINE entirely; do NOT introduce a 600s wedge guard. Ensures Codex P1 #4 ABORT-cmd fix carries the bound.
close_reason: Closed

title: Operator decision: keep backend consent gate after dropping ConsentModal?

Memory no-tuxlink-added-safeguards mandates drop; Codex argues keep a backend one-shot operator-click gate even without the modal. Operator shape call needed before plan Phase 1 can proceed. See dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-round1-design-coherence-codex.md.
notes: Operator decision 2026-06-04 (cypress-glade-peregrine): DROP backend consume_consent_token atomic. Rationale: WLE has no equivalent backend token; the operator click is the consent (feedback_radio1_governs_tx_not_ui); aligns with feedback_no_tuxlink_added_safeguards. Busy-guard handles overlap-prevention separately. Plan Tasks 1.1-1.4 proceed as currently written (full removal of consume_consent_token).

title: fix: surgical mermaid CSS revert — invisible-diagram regression from PR #352
description:
The Mermaid CSS shipped in PR #352 (commit ebbef4c) rendered diagrams entirely invisible against the dark theme. Operator confirmed via grim screenshot: empty space where the flowchart should render.

Root cause: '.label-container { fill: var(--surface) }' + similar broad rect-fill rules. SVG fill is inherited; setting it on wrapper groups propagated into every descendant rect/path/text, painting them all in var(--surface) which is one hex digit away from var(--bg).

Fix shape: revert to the surgical minimum that addresses ONLY the original black-on-dark-blue regression. Keep theming for node shapes / clusters / edge strokes, add SVG text fill with !important to defeat Mermaid's inline fill='black', keep targeted .label / .label foreignObject > * / foreignObject div for HTML labels. Drop the broader sequence-diagram, .label-container, .edgeLabel rect, foreignObject div/span/p triplet that may have lost specificity fights.

Patch ready at /tmp/mermaid-surgical-revert.patch (137 lines).
