title: Set Wayland/X11 app_id from Rust at startup; collapse to one .desktop file (supersedes tuxlink-xcay workaround)
description:
Proper resolution of the dual-.desktop workaround shipped in tuxlink-xcay. The deferred-by-design follow-up explicitly mentioned in xcay's notes: "Cleaner long-term fix: explicitly set the Wayland app_id in Rust at WindowBuilder time via tao's wmclass() / set_app_id() API."

## Why this matters now

Operator empirically verified the duplicate menu entry on the v0.25.0 .deb install (2026-06-04). The dual-install workaround that fixed the original tuxlink-mj7i icon bug is now visibly wrong in production: end users see two "Tuxlink" entries under Hamradio. The bundle is unpredictable for downstream packagers (a Debian or Fedora packager wouldn't tolerate the duplicate).

Operator-confirmed direction (2026-06-04): collapse to one .desktop file via the deferred Rust-side fix, do NOT diagnose-and-pick on the operator's host (that's host-specific reliance, not architectural fix).

## Scope (probably 1-2 focused sessions, not "half a day")

### Investigation phase

1. Read tauri-runtime-wry source in Tauri 2.11.2 to determine current GApplication.application_id behavior. The xcay bd from 2026-06-02 found Tauri was setting app_id to the binary name (tuxlink), not the bundle identifier (com.tuxlink.app). Tauri version drift since then may have changed this; need source-level confirmation, not host probing.

2. Survey the override mechanism candidates and pick one:
   - Tauri .setup() hook + gtk_window.set_wmclass() — works on X11; deprecated in GTK 3 but still functional; Wayland behavior unclear (gtk3-on-Wayland may or may not propagate to xdg_toplevel.app_id)
   - Force g_application_set_application_id() before Tauri inits GApplication — requires figuring out timing; may need an upstream hook
   - Submit a Tauri upstream PR adding a clean tauri.conf.json field like `tauri.bundle.linux.appId` — cleanest long-term but weeks of elapsed time for review + release cadence

### Implementation phase

3. Wire the chosen override in src-tauri/. Force Wayland/X11 app_id to com.tuxlink.app (matching tauri.conf.json's identifier and the project's intent in scripts/com.tuxlink.app.desktop).

4. Drop tuxlink.desktop from bundle.linux.deb.files in tauri.conf.json. Drop tuxlink.png installation from the dev script + .deb (keep com.tuxlink.app.png only). Single .desktop file, single icon name.

5. Update scripts/install-desktop-entry.sh to install only com.tuxlink.app.desktop + com.tuxlink.app.png. Drop the dual-install logic.

### Verification phase

6. Build the .deb. Install on a clean target. Confirm:
   - Exactly one "Tuxlink" entry under Hamradio in the menu
   - The entry's icon is the Tuxlink penguin (not a generic fallback)
   - Clicking the menu entry launches the app
   - The running window's panel icon matches the menu entry's icon
   - `wlrctl toplevel list` (or compositor equivalent) reports app_id = com.tuxlink.app

7. Verification matrix across compositors:
   - labwc + wf-panel-pi (Raspberry Pi OS default, the original tuxlink-mj7i reproducer)
   - sway (canonical wlroots reference)
   - X11 + a panel that respects WM_CLASS (xfce4-panel, mate-panel)
   - GNOME (gnome-shell on Wayland)
   - At least 2 of these need to pass before merge; others can be follow-up bds if regressions appear

### Documentation phase

8. Close tuxlink-xcay (workaround was the right interim, now superseded).
9. Update the linux-desktop-integration-validation auto-memory entry. The "three-layer validation" rule still holds; the "dual-install fallback" guidance is retired.
10. If we submitted an upstream Tauri PR, link it in CONTRIBUTING.md so future maintainers don't try to re-discover the rationale.

## NOT in scope

- macOS bundle behavior (CFBundleIdentifier is already correct via Tauri's macOS bundler)
- Windows AppUserModelID (already correct via Tauri's MSI bundler)
- Pi-side host diagnosis (operator-confirmed not the right path; this is architectural)

## Acceptance criteria

- A fresh .deb install on a representative compositor shows exactly one Tuxlink menu entry with the correct icon
- bundle.linux.deb.files installs exactly one .desktop file and one icon-name set
- tuxlink-xcay is closed with reference to the merged PR
- linux-desktop-integration-validation memory entry is updated

## Reference

- tuxlink-xcay (the dual-install workaround that this supersedes)
- tuxlink-mj7i (the original icon-missing bug)
- tuxlink-5e2d (the Exec=/usr/bin/env fix that's still load-bearing — preserve)
- Memory entry linux-desktop-integration-validation
notes:
2026-06-04 magpie-isthmus-gorge — architectural reframing after source-read + Codex consult.

ROOT CAUSE (refines the bd issue body's premise): The duplicate Hamradio menu entry is NOT a Tauri runtime defect requiring Rust-side app_id management. It is OUR `linux.deb.files` overlay in `tauri.conf.json` adding `com.tuxlink.app.desktop` ON TOP OF the bundler's auto-generated `tuxlink.desktop` (which is internally coherent with the default GApplication.application_id=tuxlink derived from g_get_prgname).

EVIDENCE:
- Read tauri-2.11.2/src/app.rs:2272-2286 — enableGTKAppId default false → passes None as app_id
- Read tauri-runtime-wry-2.11.2/src/lib.rs:2908-2918 — only calls with_app_id() when Some
- Read tao-0.35.2/src/platform_impl/linux/event_loop.rs:200-230 — None → gtk::Application::new(None,...) → GTK falls back to g_get_prgname (=binary name "tuxlink")
- dpkg-deb -c on v0.25.0 arm64 .deb shipped from cameronzucker/tuxlink Releases shows BOTH /usr/share/applications/tuxlink.desktop AND com.tuxlink.app.desktop, each with Name=Tuxlink Categories=Network;HamRadio; → both show in menu

ARCHITECTURAL CONSEQUENCE: The Rust-side path (enableGTKAppId=true + reverse-DNS naming) is incoherent without fighting the bundler because Tauri's bundler hardcodes the .desktop filename to productName-derived binary name (=tuxlink) with NO `desktopFileName` knob. Reverse-DNS naming would require: (a) custom desktop_template, (b) brittle postinst rm of bundler's auto-gen, (c) manual icon overlay. R3 is the least risky fix.

CODEX CONSULT (2026-06-04, gitignored at dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-mpds-architectural-consult-codex.md, 2580 lines): "Your hypothesis is correct. The duplicate Hamradio menu entry is caused by the project overlay adding a second desktop entry, not by Tauri runtime app-id behavior. R3 is the clean architectural fix." Codex Q5 flagged "would update/comment the bd issue with this consult and ask the operator to amend 'Rust-side app_id override' to 'remove reverse-DNS overlay; keep Tauri default app id.'" — this NOTES entry IS that amendment.

PROPOSED FIX (R3):
1. Drop `/usr/share/applications/com.tuxlink.app.desktop: ../scripts/com.tuxlink.app.desktop` entry from tauri.conf.json `linux.deb.files`
2. KEEP `/usr/share/applications/tuxlink.desktop: ../scripts/tuxlink.desktop` overlay (preserves the /usr/bin/env GIO trick from linux-desktop-integration-validation memory)
3. KEEP `enableGTKAppId` unset (default false) — runtime app_id stays "tuxlink", matches bundler-installed filenames
4. Delete scripts/com.tuxlink.app.desktop (no longer referenced)
5. Simplify scripts/install-desktop-entry.sh to install only the tuxlink-named variants (drop dual-install logic)
6. Update memory entry linux-desktop-integration-validation: retire "dual-install fallback" guidance

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA STATUS: All criteria from the bd issue body still satisfied (exactly one Tuxlink menu entry, one .desktop file shipped by overlay, one icon-name set in hicolor, xcay closed). The change in shape: no Rust changes; reverse-DNS app_id NOT pursued — operator can redirect at PR review if reverse-DNS is desired for non-stated reasons.

If operator prefers reverse-DNS path: file follow-up bd for the more invasive approach (desktop_template + postinst + icon overlay).
close_reason: Closed

title: VARA listener disarm: add ABORT side-channel to interrupt active B2F
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-9ls2 (PR pending). The ARDOP equivalent (PR #344 Codex P1#3, tuxlink-61yg) shipped an abort_in_flight() side-channel on the cmd socket so vara_set_listen(false) — i.e., operator clicks Disarm — could interrupt an active B2F session and stop the modem keying within ~1s.

The current VARA disarm path (ui_commands.rs vara_set_listen, ~line 3017) sets a shutdown flag that the consumer task observes only between loop iterations. Inside an active run_vara_b2f_answer call, the consumer is blocked for the full B2F duration. Disarm clicked → shutdown flag set → consumer doesn't see it until B2F completes naturally → modem keeps transmitting B2F responses for tens of seconds (worst case: until the 2s data-socket read_timeout chains stop the exchange).

Why this didn't ship with tuxlink-9ls2: the architecture is "consumer takes transport via take_transport()" — by design, the disarm path has no handle on the cmd socket. Fix needs one of:
(a) VaraSession::take_transport returns transport + cloned cmd_writer; the clone goes into VaraListenHandle; vara_set_listen(false) uses the clone to send ABORT\r;
(b) restructure the consumer to use a shorter per-poll budget inside B2F (less robust);
(c) operator workaround: shut VARA process down externally to break the data socket (current state).

Watched failure mode: Part 97 — disarm = "stop transmitting now"; current behavior is "stop transmitting eventually." Not a license violation since the consumer DOES eventually stop, but it doesn't meet the operator's intuition for the button.

Acceptance criteria:
- After vara_set_listen(false), the modem stops transmitting B2F responses within ~1s
- Add an integration test (or operator smoke step) that exercises this
- Mirror the wire form: VARA OutboundCommand::Abort renders "ABORT" (per command.rs:65)
close_reason: Closed

title: release.yml does not fire on tag push from release-please (GITHUB_TOKEN anti-recursion); needs PAT
description:
Verified gap in the release pipeline. v0.24.1, v0.24.2, v0.24.3 all tagged today, all with ZERO binary artifacts. Verified via `gh run list --workflow=release.yml --event=push` (empty).

## Root cause

googleapis/release-please-action@v5 uses the default GITHUB_TOKEN to push tags + create Release objects. Per GitHub's documented anti-recursion policy (https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication#using-the-github_token-in-a-workflow), workflow runs triggered by events created with GITHUB_TOKEN do NOT trigger downstream workflows. The tag push from release-please.yml therefore does NOT fire release.yml.

This is a deliberate GitHub guard against infinite-loop scenarios (workflow A triggers workflow B triggers workflow A...). It also breaks the common pipeline pattern where release-please's tag triggers a release-build workflow.

## Fix (operator-only — requires repo secrets)

Standard recipe: replace the default GITHUB_TOKEN in release-please.yml with a Personal Access Token (PAT). Events created by a PAT DO trigger downstream workflows.

1. Generate a fine-scoped PAT at https://github.com/settings/tokens?type=beta
   - Repository access: Only this repo (cameronzucker/tuxlink)
   - Repository permissions: Contents: Read and write; Pull requests: Read and write
   - Expiration: 1 year (max for fine-grained); calendar a renewal
2. Add as repo secret: gh secret set RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN --repo cameronzucker/tuxlink --body PAT_VALUE
3. Update .github/workflows/release-please.yml: add token: secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN to the googleapis/release-please-action@v5 step (`token: ${{ secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN }}` in YAML syntax)
4. Verify on next release: after release-please's Release PR is merged, watch gh run list --workflow=release.yml --event=push for a new run

## Alternative (more robust, more setup)

GitHub App approach using actions/create-github-app-token@v1 — token minted at workflow time, no expiration management burden. More setup (create + install GitHub App) but better long-term.

## Verification post-fix

The first tagged release after the fix should produce two workflow artifacts (linux-bundles-amd64 + linux-bundles-arm64) attached to the GitHub Release. v0.24.1/2/3 are missing artifacts and will remain missing — release.yml has no backfill trigger for past tags; if you want artifacts on those, run gh workflow run release.yml --ref v0.24.3 (workflow_dispatch fires on any ref) once and it'll attach to the existing Release object.

## Reference

https://github.com/googleapis/release-please-action/blob/main/docs/permissions.md is the canonical setup doc for both PAT and GitHub App paths.

title: ARDOP listener: route inbound CONNECTED through gate + run B2F answerer on Accept
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-7vea (the bundled UI + ARDOP-LISTEN-flag-flip PR). The current implementation of ardop_listen sends LISTEN TRUE to a running modem, which makes the modem accept inbound ARQ at the modem layer. But there is no application-layer router that catches the CONNECTED event from ardopcf, runs the listener-arms gate (gate_inbound_peer_now), and hands the modem data stream to run_exchange_with_role(Answer) on Accept.

What this means today:
- Operator arms the listener → LISTEN TRUE sent to modem → modem accepts inbound ARQ
- Peer connects → ardopcf emits CONNECTED on the cmd socket
- The existing status broadcaster catches CONNECTED into the modem status display ("Connected to N7CPZ")
- BUT no B2F handshake runs at the application layer — there is no path from the CONNECTED event to run_exchange_with_role(Answer)
- So the peer sees a successful ARQ-level connect but cannot exchange mail via tuxlink as listener

What needs to land:
1. A way for ardop_listen to install an "inbound CONNECTED handler" that runs while the listener is armed
2. The handler builds PeerId from the CONNECTED event's peer_call, calls gate_inbound_peer_now against the operator's allowlist + arms record
3. On Accept: hand the transport's data_stream to run_exchange_with_role(ExchangeRole::Answer, ..) for B2F (mirroring native_packet_exchange's pattern)
4. On Reject: arq_disconnect + append reject event to forensics log
5. Mailbox Inbox-persist + Outbox-drain symmetry on the answerer side (mirrors tuxlink-k3ru for Telnet)

Architectural concern: while the listener handler is running B2F on Accept, the modem-status broadcaster (which uses drain_status_events on the same transport) may compete for cmd-socket events. Resolution likely involves having the listener handler take ownership of the transport during the inbound exchange and putting it back when done, with the broadcaster going silent in the interim. See spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-listener-ui-design.md §1.2 for sketch.

This is the ARDOP analogue of the Telnet inbound-mail symmetry tracked at tuxlink-k3ru. Both deserve their own focused PRs.

Depends on: tuxlink-7vea landing.

Closes: tuxlink-95g8 was filed in the prior dhbl PR as the umbrella for ARDOP live-modem wiring. The LISTEN-flag-flip portion landed in tuxlink-7vea; this issue replaces tuxlink-95g8 as the focused successor for the CONNECTED + B2F routing piece.

title: CI: ci.yml workflow — tests/lint/typecheck/build on amd64+arm64 matrix
description:
Codex-consulted plan to add a verification CI workflow. Motivation: 4-6 parallel agents on the Pi 5 thrash local CPU running cargo test + clippy + typecheck + vitest + tauri build before push; move that to GitHub Actions' free Linux runners (amd64 + arm64 both available since 2024). Codex consult at dev/adversarial/2026-06-03-ci-workflow-consult-codex.md.

Decisions (operator-confirmed):
- Triggers: pull_request + push to main + push to feat/v0.0.1
- Matrix: amd64 (ubuntu-latest) + arm64 (ubuntu-24.04-arm), both required. ARM is the primary target hardware; skipping it negates the gate.
- CONTRIBUTING.md Local verification rewrite in same PR

Implementation:
- New .github/workflows/ci.yml
- New package.json typecheck script (the documented pnpm typecheck doesn't actually exist today — Codex caught this)
- Cache namespace tuxlink-ci-* distinct from release.yml's tuxlink-* (cargo target/debug vs target/release isolation already handled by Cargo; separate cache keys keep eviction economics sensible)

title: CI: add arm64 matrix job to Linux packaging pipeline
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-qybc (PR #325 merged). Current pipeline runs on ubuntu-latest only, emitting amd64 artifacts. Cameron flagged that compiling on the Pi pegs the CPU for hours and blocks the converge-build test workflow; CI-built arm64 artifacts are the cleaner path.

Wrap the existing job in a strategy.matrix with [amd64 -> ubuntu-latest, arm64 -> ubuntu-24.04-arm] (GitHub Actions' free ARM runners added in 2024 — no cross-compile complexity). Adjust:
- runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
- cargo cache key adds matrix.arch (target dirs are arch-incompatible)
- upload-artifact name is unique per matrix run (linux-bundles-amd64 / linux-bundles-arm64)
- gh-release upload on tag attaches both arches to the same release

Doubles CI minutes per run but produces both architecture sets. Per-release artifact total ~256MB (12M deb x2, 12M rpm x2, 104M AppImage x2).

title: ARDOP listener: wire LISTEN TRUE + CONNECTED event routing + B2F answerer handoff
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-dhbl PR (ARDOP listener-arms wiring). The Codex post-subagent adrev round 2026-06-03 flagged that the listener-arms gate is correctly shipped + unit-tested but the live-modem wiring is NOT in place:

- `ardop_listen()` records the arms event but does NOT send `LISTEN TRUE` to a live modem
- The async event loop does NOT consume `CONNECTED <peer>` events and route them through `serve_inbound_one`
- On Accept the connected stream is NOT handed to `ExchangeRole::Answer` B2F driver
- `ardop_set_listen(enabled)` is a no-op stub that returns Err in the Codex-fix commit

The full architectural change requires plumbing the live `ModemSession` (or its transport command channel) into the `ardop_listen` Tauri command. The subagent flagged this as deferred and noted the foundation lacks a `with_transport_cmd_mut` accessor.

Scope:
- Wire `ardop_listen()` to: claim the ModemSession, send `LISTEN TRUE`, install a CONNECTED-event router that builds PeerId, calls listener_decide, hands accepted stream to `run_exchange_with_role(Answer, ..)` 
- `ardop_set_listen(false)` sends `LISTEN FALSE` + clears the operator state
- Mailbox Inbox-persist + Outbox-drain on Accept (parallel to tuxlink-k3ru's Telnet symmetry)
- BackendStatus::Listening { transport: "ARDOP" } when armed; back to Disconnected on reject

Depends-on: tuxlink-dhbl (ARDOP listener-gate PR landing)

title: [perf] Memoize useStatusData + React.memo DashboardRibbon + StatusBar
description: Codex flagged useStatusData polls (config 5s + backend/position 2s) as a top render-storm source. The hook returns a fresh object every call so any re-render of AppShell creates new props for DashboardRibbon + StatusBar — even when the underlying poll data hasn't changed. Fix: useMemo the hook's return value keyed on the actual query data refs; React.memo on DashboardRibbon + StatusBar so they bail when statusData identity is stable. Required prereq: AppShell must already useCallback its props passed to those components (verified during PR #305).

title: RMS-Relay foundation: SessionIntent enum + B2 RoutingFlag tagging + banner parser
description:
Foundation slice for the §2.13 RMS-Relay client umbrella. Backend-only, no UI changes — mirrors the tuxlink-3o2o listener-arms-foundation pattern that landed before xehu/inde/dhbl/xnoy.

Scope:
1. New SessionIntent enum: Cms | RadioOnly | PostOffice | Mesh | P2p. Add to src-tauri/src/winlink/session.rs (alongside ExchangeRole).
2. Extend ExchangeConfig with intent: SessionIntent. Default = Cms for back-compat.
3. Per-message RoutingFlag (C/R/L) tagging in B2F message compose. Per the deep-dive §3.1, the flag goes in the outgoing FB header. Inbound: parse and stash for the mailbox layer.
4. Banner-phrase parser: new src-tauri/src/winlink/relay_banner.rs module. Implement the state machine from B2Protocol.cs:1879-1883 + L2050-2079 with the exact byte-sensitive phrases ('THIS IS A RADIO-ONLY HUB. MESSAGES WILL NOT BE SENT' / 'NETWORK POST OFFICE.  MESSAGES WILL BE STORED LOCALLY' [note: two spaces] / 'CMS>' / 'CMS via <X>' / etc.). Returns a B2RelayState enum: NotRelay | LocalDatabase | RadioNetwork | RadioNetworkAndInternet | NoCMSConnectionAvailable.
5. Tests for all of the above. Banner-parser tests with the exact phrases (case-sensitive). RoutingFlag round-trip tests.

OUT OF SCOPE for this slice:
- UI wiring (Radio-only / Post Office / Network PO sidebar entries) — separate per-path slices.
- Persistent state-indicator strip — UI follow-up.
- Dial-host plumbing (Telnet/Packet/VARA/ARDOP relay-endpoint config) — per-transport follow-ups.

Spec input:
- dev/scratch/winlink-re/findings/client-of-rms-relay.md §2.2, §3, §10 (especially §10.4 — FIX the WLE typo 'will by stored' → 'will be stored' in tuxlink's modal text; do NOT carry it forward).
notes: Replaced PR #321 with PR #322 (rebased onto main to pick up the PR #318/#319/#320 listener-foundation work which added 7 new ExchangeConfig sites). Worktree branch is now bd-tuxlink-kld3/rms-relay-foundation-v2.

title: UMBRELLA: Implement §2.13 RMS-Relay client (paths 1A/1B/1C + foundation)
description:
Umbrella for the implementation pass on the WLE-parity §2.13 row (Client-of-RMS-Relay). The deep-dive (tuxlink-svsb) is complete at dev/scratch/winlink-re/findings/client-of-rms-relay.md — this umbrella tracks the implementation slices that consume that spec.

Implementation decomposes into a FOUNDATION pass + three per-path slices, mirroring the listener-arms-foundation pattern (tuxlink-3o2o → xehu/inde/dhbl/xnoy).

CHILDREN (filed separately):
- Foundation: SessionIntent enum + RoutingFlag tagging + banner parser (no UI). Mirror of how 3o2o shipped before the per-transport listeners.
- Path 1B: Telnet Radio-only (sidebar entry + dial flow + state indicator).
- Path 1C: Network Post Office / MESH (sidebar entry + dial flow).
- Path 1A: "Use RMS Relay" checkbox on existing Telnet-CMS path.

CROSS-CUTTING follow-ups (already-filed bd issues that pair with this):
- tuxlink-k6wk — Saved peers / relay endpoints favorites manager (§9.7).
- tuxlink-oxs2 — ConfirmConnection inline strip (§10.7).

Spec inputs:

- docs/design/2026-05-22-session-type-selector-design.md (UI structure for sidebar accordion).
- docs/design/2026-06-02-wle-client-parity-closure-plan.md §2.13 disposition (🟢 ship, P2).

Out of scope:
- tuxlink-as-RMS-Relay-hub. The deep-dive is explicit: tuxlink is a CLIENT of relays, not an implementation of one.
- AREDN MESH telnet to CMS (§2.12, tuxlink-esy7) — sibling row but a different deep-dive.
- Cross-transport RadioOnly variants (Packet/VARA/ARDOP) — same flag, different transports; follow-on issues per transport.

title: Telnet listener inbound-mail symmetry: persist received + drain outbox on inbound exchange
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-xehu PR (Telnet-P2P listener). The Codex post-subagent adrev round 2026-06-03 flagged that the listener's run_b2f_answerer Vec<Answer> decide callback returns accept-all (P1 fix applied in the same PR) but the listener does NOT:

1. Drain the operator's Outbox into the inbound exchange (so an inbound peer can never pull pending mail from us).
2. Persist `result.received` to the mailbox Inbox (so accepted-by-B2F messages disappear).

The Packet listener leverages `native_packet_exchange` in `winlink_backend.rs` which does both (see line ~1273 `run_exchange_with_role(...)` call site + ~1289 inbox/sent persistence). Telnet has its own `run_b2f_answerer` in `telnet_listen.rs` that doesn't.

Right architectural fix: either (a) factor `native_packet_exchange`'s outbox-drain + inbox-persist into a transport-agnostic helper that Telnet calls too, or (b) plumb the mailbox through the Tauri `telnet_listen` command → run_accept_loop → handle_one_session and persist there. (a) is cleaner.

Scope:
- Plumb Arc<Mailbox> into telnet_listen's accept loop
- On `Accept` post-allowlist+password, load operator Outbox into the B2F exchange's `outbound` vec (mirror native_packet_exchange ~line 1240)
- After run_exchange_with_role returns: store result.received to Inbox; move result.sent MIDs from Outbox to Sent
- Surface result.rejected as a session-log warning
- Tests: inbound exchange end-to-end: peer dials in, offers 1 message, sees it stored; operator's Outbox drains to peer; peer's ACK moves MIDs to Sent

Depends-on: tuxlink-xehu (Telnet listener PR with Codex-fix commit landing)

title: Linux packaging pipeline: native Tauri bundlers (deb + rpm + AppImage), supersedes cs7

Supersedes [tuxlink-cs7] which was scoped to AppImage with bundled-Pat content (stale per the 2026-05-30 Pat strip directive).

Scope: ship the three Tauri-native Linux bundle formats via the existing release pipeline. Tauri 2.11.2's bundler () emits all three from one command — confirmed by inspecting  which lists deb, rpm, appimage as the supported Linux targets.

Deliverables:

  2. Verify libsecret-1-0 (the runtime keyring dep) appears in the auto-generated Depends:/Requires: fields, or add it explicitly via tauri.conf.json
  3. CI integration: extend .github/workflows/release.yml from cargo build --release to pnpm tauri build --bundles appimage,deb,rpm; upload artifacts to GitHub Releases on v* tags; emit SHA256 sums
  4. Documentation: move deb/rpm/AppImage from README Not-yet-shipped to Install-options; add install commands to docs/install.md

Out of scope:

title: Wire menu:message:print (Ctrl+P) — accelerator exists, handler missing
description:
The Ctrl+P accelerator is in src/shell/chrome/menuModel.ts:148 mapped to menu:message:print. The menu item is rendered as disabled+badged ("soon"). dispatchMenuAction has no case for the id, so it falls through to the safe no-op default. Net effect: Ctrl+P silently does nothing — a UX bug, not just an unwired feature.

Tauri 2 webviews expose window.print(). Wiring is:

1. Add a `print` handler to MenuHandlers in AppShell.tsx that fires window.print() (gated on having an open message selected, so the print dialog renders the current reading-pane content).
2. Add a case in dispatchMenuAction.ts dispatching menu:message:print to h.print().
3. Remove `disabled: true` from menuModel.ts:51 Print entry.
4. Tests: dispatchMenuAction.test.ts gets a parallel "menu:message:print fires handlers.print" assertion; useAccelerators.test.ts confirms Ctrl+P fires the action id.

Out of scope:
- Multi-message print (print whole folder) — not in Express either.
- Print-specific styling (@media print rules) — defer until operator finds the default rendering insufficient.

title: README: drop kernel AX.25 mention from Pat characterization (correctness)
description: Operator correctness note on PR #302 (merged): the README's What-it-is section describes Pat as supporting AX.25 'via Direwolf, the Linux kernel stack, or a serial TNC' — but the Linux kernel AX.25 stack has been deprecation-tracked out of modern releases. The module remains shipped on some older builds (e.g. Ubuntu 22.04 LTS, this dev Pi's Raspberry Pi OS) but cannot be characterized as a supported path for new operators picking a Pat install in 2026. Pat's CLI still exposes 'ax25+linux' as a backend option per pat help connect, but operator note is authoritative for the operational reality.\n\nFix: drop 'the Linux kernel stack' from the parenthetical. New text: 'AX.25 (via Direwolf or a serial TNC)' — preserves the multi-backend angle without misleading on a deprecated path.

title: Wire up P2P-VARA HF/FM in the sidebar — flip cms-only scope-bound from PR #221
description:
PR #221 (tuxlink-dfmf) shipped Phase 2 VARA UI for the CMS intent only. The VaraRadioPanel + useVaraConfig + radioPanelVisibility router all already support `intent: 'p2p'`; the only thing keeping P2P-VARA disabled is the `built: false` flag pair in `src/connections/sessionTypes.ts` for p2p.vara-hf + p2p.vara-fm.

Per the rationale comment on PR #221's sessionTypes.test.ts: "Only flipping CMS in Phase 2 keeps the operator-facing scope bounded to what the implementation actually covers." That's a scope-bounding choice, not a technical constraint — the Phase 2 surface (TCP open/close, no transmit) is equally RADIO-1-safe for P2P. There is no peer-dial path in Phase 2, so the same diagnostic-only TCP-config surface ships to both intents.

Deliverables:
1. Flip p2p.vara-hf + p2p.vara-fm to built: true in src/connections/sessionTypes.ts
2. Update src/connections/sessionTypes.test.ts: the "P2P VARA HF/FM stay unbuilt" test inverts to "P2P VARA HF/FM are built", with comment updated.
3. Verify AppShell.radioPanel.test.tsx VARA-HF/FM tests still pass under p2p intent (likely no change needed — panelTitle's intent suffix already supports it).
4. Ship as one PR.

Out of scope (Phase 3 — tuxlink-fzl7):
- Consent-gated peer-dial CONNECT flow
- VARA session state machine (DISCONNECTED → PENDING → CONNECTED)
- B2F-over-VARA

title: Main client device-target support: Panasonic FZ-M1 (7" 1280x800, touch)
description: The main Tuxlink client must explicitly support the Panasonic FZ-M1 as a primary deployment device. FZ-M1 is a 7" rugged tablet with 1280x800 WXGA at ~216 PPI, capacitive touch, commonly used in emergency response / field deployments — direct fit for tuxlink's emcomm audience. Implies: (a) effective usable area ~1280x760 after OS chrome; (b) high pixel density means chrome-sized 12-14px text is illegibly small without device-level scaling; (c) touch targets must be >=44x44 px (Apple/Material guidelines); (d) layouts must reflow at narrow widths (sidebars collapse, dashboards stack); (e) automated/visual tests should run at FZ-M1's effective viewport. Scope: audit every primary UI surface (AppShell, ribbon, mailbox, radio panel, compose, settings, wizard) for fit at FZ-M1 dimensions + touch usability; remediate where broken. Parallel issue tuxlink-0gsy carries the help-window-specific support — this issue covers the rest of the client.
notes: PR #464 OPEN (ready, not draft): https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/464 — taiga-raven-wren 2026-06-07. Full FZ-M1 compact mode shipped across all surfaces + Rust compose clamp. 181 frontend + 12 Rust tests green, typecheck clean. Two adrev passes (Codex R1 13 findings + Claude 4-lens 13 findings) + Codex post-impl review (1 gap fixed). Awaiting operator browser-smoke before merge. Follow-up tuxlink-jwgi (collapsed-drawer abort) + wizard Register anchor flagged for design.

title: RADIO-1 framing for listener mode: bounded armed-window + abort-on-disarm UX
description:
UX framing for listener-mode operator consent per docs/design/2026-06-03-multi-transport-listener-architecture.md §2.4 + memory radio1-bounded-airtime-abort.

Listener-mode operator consent = the click that ARMS the listener. UI must make this explicit: armed=TX-authorized for incoming sessions for TTL window (default 1h, operator-configurable). Abort-on-disarm halts any in-flight inbound session.

Deliverables:
- UI affordance: armed/disarmed visual state + TTL countdown + 'disarm now' button
- Backend: ListenerArmsRecord TTL enforcement (auto-disarm on expiry)
- Abort path: in-flight inbound session terminates cleanly on disarm
- Documentation entry in docs/pitfalls/implementation-pitfalls.md citing the listener-as-consent semantics

Per memory radio1-governs-tx-not-ui: this is UX wrapping consent semantics, NOT a Part 97 hook-layer enforcement.

notes: AUDIT CORRECTION (Phase 2 deep-dive 2026-06-02): The 2026-05-29 row 9.7 label 'Add Telnet RMS to known-stations' is materially misleading. DialogAddTelnetStation does NOT add custom CMS endpoints; it adds Telnet-P2P partners OR PostOffice/Relay endpoints to favorites files (Telnet P2P Favorites.dat, Telnet PostOffice Favorites.dat) — pipe-delimited plaintext with plaintext passwords. Only path to non-default CMS endpoint is Use RMS Relay checkbox (covered in client-of-rms-relay.md / tuxlink-svsb). Tuxlink proposal: replicate favorites concept; diverge on credential storage (keyring not plaintext); relabel UI 'Saved peers / relay endpoints'. See dev/scratch/winlink-re/findings/add-telnet-rms-known-stations.md + docs/design/2026-06-02-wle-client-parity-closure-plan.md §1.3.

title: [GRIB] Saildocs request — parameter form, composer to query@saildocs.com, save-attachment flow
description:
Per operator B1 default (PR #283 merged 'resume as you were'). WLE parity = Saildocs only; no Winlink-CMS GRIB; no in-app viewer.

Spec:
1. Form: src/grib/GribRequestPanel.tsx (inline overlay) with controls for:
   - Region: 4 fields (LAT0, LAT1, LON0, LON1) — whole degrees + N/S/E/W per Saildocs format
   - Grid spacing: dlat, dlon (default 2,2)
   - Valid times: comma-separated or range (e.g. '6,12..96'; default '24,48,72')
   - Parameters: multi-select checkboxes from {PRMSL, WIND, HGT, SEATMP, AIRTMP, WAVES} (default PRESS,WIND)
   - Mode: send (one-shot) vs sub (recurring with days= and time= fields)
   - Model: gfs (only one supported for now; Saildocs has more but defer)
2. Composer: builds:
   - To: query@saildocs.com
   - Subject: brief label (operator-editable; default 'GRIB request')
   - Body: 'send gfs:LAT0,LAT1,LON0,LON1|dlat,dlon|VTs|Params' (or 'sub' variant)
   - Route through existing outgoing rails
3. Receipt: response arrives as Private message with GRIB-1 binary attachment. Existing MessageView attachment list renders it; 'Save As...' to disk. NO in-app GRIB viewer for v0.x (operator opens in zyGrib/OpenCPN externally — matches WLE).
4. Menu wire: 'Message → GRIB File Request' (per WLE menu naming).

Tests:
- Form validates region bounds + grid spacing + times syntax
- Composer golden test: known parameter set → expected Saildocs body string
- 'send' vs 'sub' switch composes correctly

Spec reference: https://saildocs.com/gribinfo (canonical syntax). Recap in docs/design/2026-06-02-cms-request-protocol-grounding.md §'GRIB request via Saildocs'.
notes: PR #288 rebased onto current origin/main (4af72bf → e5f049a) after ddiq/PR #286 merge created 6 keep-both-sides conflicts in the menu/dispatcher/state surface. Resolved + verified 992/992 vitest pass, tsc clean. PR is mergeable=MERGEABLE, mergeStateStatus=CLEAN. Awaiting operator merge.

title: WLE CMS-request parity sprint (catalog, stations, bulletins, GRIB)
description:
Operator (2026-06-02): 'I'd like us to be able to do all of that — it's 100% required for legacy client parity.' Refers to WLE's Request menu — sending specially-formatted messages to CMS service endpoints and receiving back catalog/data as body text or attachment.

Mechanically, requests are just specially-formatted outgoing messages — no new transport. We already have outgoing-with-attachments on native rails (tuxlink-l55l shipped 2026-06-02). This sprint adds the UI + body templates + result rendering on top.

Sub-issues filed per slice:
- tuxlink-XXX (catalog request) — first, smallest, AND its response is ground truth for what other request types the operator's CMS actually supports.
- tuxlink-XXX (station list) — text response, table renderer.
- tuxlink-XXX (bulletins) — subscribe + fetch.
- tuxlink-XXX (GRIB files) — parameter-picker form + binary attachment passthrough. Largest surface; ships last.

Plus tuxlink-XXX (P0): protocol grounding via Pat (la5nta/pat clone from github) — ships first, before any code. Per feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability + feedback_winlink_re_authoritative_sources, no request vocabulary gets invented from agent memory.

Execution: bluff-birch-cove session via /loop dynamic mode. Operator unsupervised. One PR per slice off origin/main. CMS-telnet testing against cms-z.winlink.org authorized per feedback_cms_telnet_testing_authorized; no on-air per RADIO-1. Pause + surface on operator-decision-needed or PR-queue saturation (>3 open from this sprint).
notes: Sprint shipping-done. Both sub-issues have PRs open: ddiq=#286 (catalog framework), vrpk=#288 (GRIB Saildocs). Awaiting review+merge.

All 4 sub-issues merged: tuxlink-v8ee (PR #282 protocol grounding), tuxlink-tkdc (PR #283 empirical update), tuxlink-ddiq (PR #286 catalog framework), tuxlink-vrpk (PR #288 GRIB Saildocs). WLE CMS-request parity for outbound: catalog inquiries to INQUIRY@winlink.org + GRIB requests to query@saildocs.com both functional. Executed via /loop dynamic mode (bluff-birch-cove).

title: Add dependabot ignore rule for vitest semver-major bumps until ztuv coordinated upgrade lands
description:
Short-term mitigation for tuxlink-ztuv. Closing PR #274 (vitest 2 → 4) doesn't prevent dependabot from re-proposing it every weekly scan. Adding an ignore rule for vitest semver-major updates stops the recurring noise while the deliberate Vite 5 → 6 + vitest 2 → 4 migration (tuxlink-ztuv) is planned and executed.

4-line addition to the npm ecosystem block in .github/dependabot.yml:

  ignore:
    - dependency-name: 'vitest'
      update-types: ['version-update:semver-major']
    - dependency-name: '@vitest/*'
      update-types: ['version-update:semver-major']

Effect:
- Suppresses 3.x and 4.x vitest proposals
- DOES allow vitest 2.x.y minor/patch updates to continue (e.g. 2.1.10, 2.2.0)
- Trivially reverted when ztuv lands (delete the 4 lines)

title: Coordinated upgrade: Vite 5 → 6 + vitest 2 → 4 + @vitest/coverage-v8 2 → 4 (block dependabot from auto-bumping vitest before this lands)
description:
tuxlink-xcay session triage of dependabot PRs closed PR #274 (vitest 2.1.9 → 4.1.8) because vitest 4 requires Vite 6. Our project is on Vite 5.4.21.

Error reproduced locally:
  Error [ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED]: Package subpath './module-runner' is not defined by 'exports' in vite/package.json
  ... imported from vitest/dist/chunks/cli-api.BfdDOPPI.js

vitest 4 imports vite/module-runner internally; that subpath was added in Vite 6.

This task: coordinate the upgrade as one PR:
1. Bump vite ^5.3.0 → ^6.x in package.json
2. Bump vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8 → ^4.x (the dependabot bump)
3. Check vite 6 migration guide for breaking changes affecting our build (rollup version, plugin API, default behaviors)
4. Check vitest 4 migration guide (https://vitest.dev/guide/migration) — 9+ documented breaking changes; verify our config (vitest.config.ts) and test files don't use any removed APIs (basic reporter, ErrorWithDiff, getSourceMap, UserConfig from vitest's main entry)
5. Run pnpm exec tsc --noEmit + pnpm vitest run to confirm nothing breaks
6. Visual smoke after merge

While this is open, dependabot will keep re-proposing the vitest 4 bump every weekly scan. Options:
A) Add dependabot ignore rule for vitest majors in .github/dependabot.yml (and remove it as part of this upgrade PR)
B) Just close re-proposed PRs each week until this lands
C) Land this fix quickly enough that the noise is brief

Discovered: 2026-06-02 dependabot PR #274 triage.

title: Configure dependabot groups so react + react-dom + @types/react bump together (prevent tuxlink-ola6 recurrence)
description:
tuxlink-ola6 root cause: dependabot's react PR (#252) bumped 'react' from 19.2.6 → 19.2.7 + '@types/react' 19.2.15 → 19.2.16 WITHOUT bumping 'react-dom' (still 19.2.6). React 19 enforces strict version parity between react + react-dom; the mismatch threw an uncaught runtime exception at mount time, white-screening the entire app.

dependabot supports 'groups' config in .github/dependabot.yml that bundles related updates into a single PR. This task adds a 'react' group so the four packages always bump together.

Fix: edit .github/dependabot.yml, add to the existing npm config:

  groups:
    react:
      patterns:
        - 'react'
        - 'react-dom'
        - '@types/react'
        - '@types/react-dom'

Optionally add other peer-pair groups:
- '@radix-ui/*' (related radix packages tend to bump together)
- '@tauri-apps/*' (api + cli + plugin-shell typically pair)
- '@testing-library/*' (jest-dom + react)
- 'vitest + @vitest/*'

Verification: after merging, the next dependabot react PR should include all four package bumps in one PR instead of bumping piecemeal.

Discovered: 2026-06-02 after operator-reported white-screen regression (tuxlink-ola6 / PR #252).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-642f-dependabot-groups-peer-pairs. Branch: bd-tuxlink-642f/dependabot-groups-peer-pairs. Created 2026-06-02 21:59:43 UTC by plover-willow-basalt.

title: Pin SearchBar width so it doesn't reflow the dashboard ribbon
description: Operator (2026-06-02): 'the search box dynamically resizes right now and pushes things around on the status bar. I'd really rather it not do that.' Current: src/shell/AppShell.css .layout-b .search-zone is flex: 1 1 auto; min-width: 360px; max-width: 720px — Gmail-style grow-with-content. As the window resizes (or as the SearchBar's intrinsic content changes — saved-search badge, meta text), the dashboard ribbon items shift left/right. Fix: pin .search-zone to a fixed flex-basis (e.g. flex: 0 0 560px) so the dashboard layout stays stable, and let the dashboard itself fill remaining space (its .dashboard already has flex: 1 1 auto).

(2) Native <select> renders with OS chrome on WebKitGTK that reads as 'disabled' against the dark theme. (3) Wants a more elegant pattern than the dropdown. Resolution: split SortMode into (SortKey, SortDirection); add 'size' + 'recipient' keys; replace native <select> with Radix DropdownMenu opened by an icon-button trigger; two radio groups (key + direction) with direction labels that adapt to the active key (Newest/Oldest for date, A→Z for sender/recipient/subject, Largest/Smallest for size). Full theme-token control top-to-bottom; no OS chrome leak.

SortMode → SortKey×SortDirection split, added Size+Recipient, swapped native select for @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu (icon trigger + two radio groups). localStorage key migrated tuxlink.messageList.sortMode → sortState; legacy ignored. 913 vitest pass.

title: [bug] ARDOP Radio section ALSA/PTT defaults are placeholder ghosts, not auto-fill: empty fields silently fail at ardopcf spawn
description: ArdopRadioPanel's Capture/Playback/PTT inputs render placeholder text (plughw:1,0 / /dev/ttyUSB0) that LOOKS auto-filled but is HTML placeholder ghosting only. The actual value is empty until the operator types something. commitCapture/commitPlayback persist empty strings; the ardopcf spawn then runs without -c/-p flags and either refuses to start or attaches to the system default device (rarely the radio). The PTT field maps empty -> null = VOX, which is a real choice but a poor default for HF where most operators want CAT-PTT. The WebGUI port input is the only field that TRULY auto-derives (cmd_port - 1 via resolveWebguiPort). Restore symmetry with the AX.25 picker (tuxlink-mqu3) by adding real device-enumeration backends: ardop_list_audio_devices (shells to arecord -L / aplay -L, parses the ALSA device list) + reuse packet_list_serial_devices for PTT. Frontend replaces the text-input ghosts with dropdown + Refresh + manual fallback, same pattern as ModemLinkSection.

title: VARA: send MYCALL after TCP open + emit session log lines on Start/Stop
description:
Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt session) after PR #238 made Start clickable:
- VARA reports 'WARNING: VARA is not connected to any App via TCP Port 8300' because tuxlink opens the TCP socket but never sends MYCALL. VARA's host protocol expects MYCALL immediately as the identity handshake; without it, VARA considers the App as not properly connected.
- Radio modem pane's session log doesn't update on VARA Start/Stop because vara_start_session/vara_stop_session don't append to SessionLogState or emit session_log:line events. ARDOP/CMS backends use the emit_session_line helper pattern in ui_commands.rs; VARA needs equivalent.

Fix (one PR):
1. vara_start_session_inner: take callsign: Option<&str>; after TCP open, send MYCALL if Some. (Pre-MYCALL ordering: MYCALL is identity, BW is bandwidth, so MYCALL first.)
2. vara_start_session Tauri wrapper: read Config.identity.callsign, pass to _inner. Take AppHandle + SessionLogState state args; emit a session log line on Start (Info: 'VARA: opening transport...' on entry; Info: 'transport open' on success; Error: 'start failed — <msg>' on error).
3. vara_stop_session: same emit pattern (Info: 'VARA: transport closed').
4. Tests: existing _inner tests pass None for callsign; add a unit test verifying inner accepts callsign without panicking. Byte-on-wire MYCALL verification deferred to operator smoke (requires mock TCP server).

Pre-wizard (no callsign): skip MYCALL — VARA will continue to warn, but that's a wizard-completion problem, not a VARA bug.

bd: separate follow-up tuxlink-? filed for dashboard ribbon VARA integration (bigger surface).

title: Source segmented control: roving-tabindex + arrow-key keyboard navigation (WAI-ARIA APG radiogroup polish)
description:
tuxlink-z5pz segmented control review (commit 3598693 code-quality review) flagged that the radiogroup implementation does NOT follow the WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) for radiogroup keyboard navigation:

Current implementation:
- Both segments have role='radio' + aria-checked={...}
- Both segments are real <button> elements, so Tab navigates between them
- Space/Enter activates a segment

WAI-ARIA APG radiogroup pattern (deferred polish):
- Only the currently-checked radio is in the tab order (tabIndex=0); unchecked radios are tabIndex=-1
- Left/Right (or Up/Down) arrow keys move focus between radios in the group AND change selection
- Tab moves focus OUT of the radiogroup to the next focusable element

Spec §4.4 explicitly allows deferring this as P2 polish: 'Arrow keys could optionally implement radio-group keyboard navigation per WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices (left/right move focus between radios; activating with Space changes selection) — flag this as a P2 polish item if not implemented in this PR.'

This task implements the roving-tabindex + arrow-key handling. Scope:
- GridEdit.tsx: add tabIndex prop to each radio button (0 if checked, -1 otherwise)
- GridEdit.tsx: add onKeyDown handler to the radiogroup that intercepts ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/ArrowUp/ArrowDown and switches selection (firing onUseGps or onUseManual as appropriate)
- Tests: 4 new tests asserting (a) tab moves OUT of radiogroup, (b) ArrowRight on GPS when source=Manual fires onUseGps + focuses MANUAL, (c) ArrowLeft on MANUAL when source=Gps fires onUseManual + focuses GPS, (d) keyboard activation parity with click activation

This is a usability improvement for screen-reader + keyboard-only operators; not a correctness fix for any operator-reported defect.

title: MessageList sort UI — Phase 2 of mailbox-sort (operator-selectable sort with persistence)
description:
Fast-follow to tuxlink-mjc8 (PR #201 — backend-only deterministic ordering). Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt session): 'Asking for a sort feature with no front end wiring/element would entirely defeat the point.' The backend sort gives a sensible default but the operator needs an interactive control.

Design: dropdown control rendered above the virtualized message list (MessageList rows are 3-line grids, not tabular — clickable column headers don't fit). Options:
- Newest first (default; matches the backend's date desc baseline)
- Oldest first
- Sender A→Z
- Sender Z→A
- Subject A→Z
- Subject Z→A

Sort applies client-side (sort the MessageMeta[] before passing to Virtuoso) so the backend's mailbox_list signature stays unchanged. Operator's choice persists in localStorage (global default; per-folder defaults can layer on later).

Implementation:
1. src/mailbox/messageSort.ts (new) — pure SortMode enum + compareMessages helper + tests
2. src/mailbox/MessageListSortControl.tsx (new) — small dropdown + tests
3. src/mailbox/MessageList.tsx — accept sortMode + onSortModeChange props, render control in a header row, sort array before Virtuoso
4. src/shell/AppShell.tsx — owns sortMode state + localStorage hydration/persistence
5. Test updates for any tests that pin pre-sort behavior

Phase 3 ideas (out of scope here): per-folder sort defaults, multi-key sort, unread-first.

title: VARA panel — ungate form fields on aarch64; tuxlink can connect to remote VARA over TCP
description:
Fast-follow to PR #221 (tuxlink-dfmf). Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt session) found that on the Pi (aarch64), all form inputs (host/cmd_port/data_port/bandwidth) AND the Start button are disabled, with a red banner saying 'VARA requires an x86 / x86_64 host'.

That gating matches the original tuxlink-xfo spec ('show a disabled state with the explanation banner') but the spec was wrong. Pi can't RUN VARA locally (no Wine on aarch64), but Pi can absolutely CONNECT to a remote VARA over TCP — that's exactly what the 100.83.168.37:8300/8301 smoke target is for.

Fix:
1. Remove platformBlocked from the disabled={...} prop on host/cmd_port/data_port/bandwidth inputs and the Start button. loading || isOpen still apply.
2. Reword the banner from 'Controls below are disabled' → 'VARA cannot run *locally* on this host. Point host at a remote VARA instance to use it from here.'
3. Update VaraRadioPanel.test.tsx — the 'renders the Pi-availability banner on ARM and disables Start' test should now assert banner-presence + form-editability instead of Start-disabled.

Supersedes the inflexible gating intent in tuxlink-xfo (closable once this lands).

title: ARDOP panel elements jumbled / pushed off the right edge at 400px panel width
description: Operator-reported: ARDOP UI elements push past the right side of the radio panel even with the window maximized. Root cause is the textbook 1fr/flex:1 without min-width:0 companion pattern. SignalSection's Quality+SNR grid (104px 1fr) and RadioPanel's input rows (72px 1fr) resolve 1fr as minmax(auto, 1fr), so long content (ALSA device paths, PTT serial-by-id, etc.) pushes the track past the panel content box. FrameRibbon's flex:1 cells have the same defensive gap. Fix: swap to minmax(0, 1fr) and add min-width:0 to the shrinkable children. ModemLinkSection's repeat(3, 1fr) segmented picker has the same bug class — fix in the same PR.

title: VARA Phase 3 — session state machine + RF connect with RADIO-1 consent gate
description:
Phase 2 (tuxlink-dfmf) wired the minimal VARA TCP transport UI: open/close cmd+data sockets, edit host/ports/bandwidth, no RF transmission. Phase 3 adds:

1. Full VaraSession state machine — model VARA's INFO line + state events (CONNECTED <mycall> <target> [bw], PENDING, DISCONNECTED, PTT ON/OFF, BUFFER <n>, LINK REGISTERED, IAMALIVE, OFFLINE, error variants) as a session state mirror of ARDOP's ModemSession.

2. RF CONNECT path with RADIO-1 consent token (mirror modem_ardop_connect's mint→consume pattern):
   - vara_mint_consent (BACKEND mints, frontend never)
   - vara_connect_to_peer (consumes token atomically, sends CONNECT <mycall> <target>, manages the state transitions)
   - vara_disconnect_peer (sends DISCONNECT or ABORT depending on policy)

3. B2F-over-VARA integration — vara_b2f_exchange equivalent of modem_ardop_b2f_exchange. Reuse the b2f helper from ARDOP if possible (Phase 1's hblz issue raised the open question of whether to factor out a shared b2f.rs).

4. UI updates in VaraRadioPanel — Connect/Disconnect buttons + consent modal + peer-callsign input + connected-state display (peer + bw + buffer).

5. Bounded-airtime + abort discipline per feedback_radio1_bounded_airtime_abort.

Cross-refs:
- Phase 1 PR #199 (codec + transport)
- Phase 2 (this issue, tuxlink-dfmf) — to be merged before Phase 3 starts
- hblz issue: Phase 2 'awaits operator direction' design questions (Connect to peer state machine; b2f integration)
- v05-modem-design-posture memory: clean-room replacement is the long-term goal, but interim VARA support is the Phase 3 deliverable
close_reason: Subsumed by tuxlink-0ye6 (alpha-polish work). Task 3.4 shipped modem_vara_b2f_exchange as the VARA dial path's B2F entry point; the remaining 'consent token mint/consume' pieces of the original fzl7 scope were removed by tuxlink-0ye6's spec §2 (No tuxlink-added safeguards) — see Task 1.1 + 1.2 commits removing the ARDOP consent gate. Arbiter wire-in is tracked at tuxlink-17u9.

title: Position subsystem CSS polish: .dimmed, .dash-set-manually, .dash-gps-no-fix-status, .dash-gps-ready-status
description:
Track A position-subsystem-restoration (tuxlink-c79g) referenced four CSS classes in JSX but didn't add CSS for them:

- .dash-gps-ready-status (T11 — passive GPS-ready hint span, State 2)
- .dimmed (T13 — dimmed chip modifier when source=Gps && !gpsReady, spec §2.4 row 4/5)
- .dash-set-manually (T13 — 'Set manually' button, State 4/5 affordance)
- .dash-gps-no-fix-status (T13 — GPS no fix status text)

T11/T13/T15 reviewers flagged this as deferred to a styling pass. Functionality + DOM contracts + tests are intact (tests assert by className substring + data-testid, not visual state). But the classes render without visual styling until CSS lands.

This task adds the CSS to AppShell.css (or wherever the dash- prefixed classes live) to give these elements their intended visual appearance per spec §2.4 / §4.4. Includes:
- .dimmed: reduced opacity for State 4/5 chip (e.g. opacity: 0.6, color shift to neutral)
- .dash-set-manually: link-like button styling consistent with other inline actions
- .dash-gps-ready-status: subtle status text styling (color + size matching other status indicators)
- .dash-gps-no-fix-status: warning-tinted status text

Operator smoke after CSS lands: walk through all 5 states (Manual+grid, Manual+nogrid, Gps+fix, Gps+nofix+grid, Gps+nofix+nogrid) in the running app and confirm each state's visual hierarchy is clear.

Discovered: 2026-06-02 plover-willow-basalt while landing Track A T11/T13/T15.

title: Convert useStatusData to react-query so T14 invalidateQueries actually triggers refetch
description:
Track A T14 added queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['config_read'] }) calls in DashboardRibbon after onCommit + onUseGps. However, useStatus.ts polls config_read via raw useState + useEffect + setInterval(load, 5000) — NOT via useQuery({ queryKey: ['config_read'], ... }). So the invalidation has no production refetch target; the chip + grid display still lag up to 5s after a user action.

To deliver the UX improvement the plan promised (chip flips within one render cycle), useStatusData needs to be refactored to use react-query with the ['config_read'] key. The T14 invalidation hook will then actually trigger refetch.

Scope:
- Refactor useStatusData in src/shell/useStatus.ts to use useQuery({ queryKey: ['config_read'], queryFn: () => invoke('config_read'), refetchInterval: 5000 }) and remove the setInterval pattern.
- Same for position_status if applicable.
- Verify no consumer regression (DashboardRibbon, GridEdit, status.test.ts).
- Re-run T14 tests — they should still pass (the spy on invalidateQueries still triggers).

Discovered: 2026-06-02 plover-willow-basalt while landing tuxlink-c79g T14.

title: [convergence-discipline] Add core.hooksPath bypass to destructive-git deny list
description: Per Codex 2026-06-01 P1 #3 (state-machine-hooks PR review): the ADR 0017 escape hatch is loud and audited. But an agent could trivially bypass the lifecycle hooks via git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null commit ... (silent and unaudited). Add to .claude/hooks/block-destructive-git.sh deny patterns: -c core.hooksPath= ; --config core.hooksPath= ; GIT_HOOKS_PATH= when in front of a git command. Same class as the existing skips-hooks pattern. The destructive-git hook is the right enforcement layer; ADR 0017's lifecycle hooks are not Claude-Code-aware and cannot police their own bypass surface. Parent: tuxlink-edvb (umbrella).

title: [convergence-discipline] converge-build v1 P2 polish: jq-safe audit JSON + gh-degradation provenance
description:
P2 follow-ups from the converge-build v1 Codex review (transcript at dev/adversarial/2026-06-01-converge-build-v1-codex.md on bd-tuxlink-qepd/converge-build). Both are robustness/forensics polish; the v1 PR addresses all P1 and P3 findings inline, defers these P2s.

Codex P2 #5 — Audit JSON not safe enough for failure forensics:
- audit() interpolates JSON strings directly via printf — could break if paths contain quotes/newlines.
- Untracked-collision logs flatten path arrays via IFS=',' (loses per-file SHAs).
- Process logs keep only PIDs, not cmdlines/cwds.
Change: Build audit lines with `jq -n --arg/--argjson` for path-bearing fields; include per-collision local+upstream SHAs; capture killed-process cmdline/cwd from /proc/<pid>/cmdline + cwd before sending SIGTERM.

Codex P2 #6 — Branch classification silently degrades if gh is unavailable/unauthenticated:
- classify_branch() falls through to category "bd-issue-active" with no warning if gh fails.
- A merged-dead branch on a host with no gh auth gets misclassified as active.

title: TCP P2P Telnet (Winlink) — design + implement client+listener
description:
Implement raw-TCP B2F P2P-Telnet to allow tuxlink to exchange mail with WLE on operator's Windows machine (configured as Telnet P2P listener or Post Office), using WLE's registered SID as the CMS-facing proxy. Addresses production-CMS SID-rejection blocker and enables real e2e smoke for the native Winlink client.

Scope:
- Client-dial transport (telnet_p2p.rs sibling to telnet.rs)
- P2P-listen transport (port bind + accept loop)
- Allowed-stations enforcement (callsigns + IPs, * wildcards)
- Optional station-password layer (keyring-stored, not INI-plaintext like WLE)
- UI surface: fills the unbuilt p2p+telnet cell in session-type-selector matrix (per docs/plans/2026-05-22-session-type-selector.md)

Two divergences from WLE parity (both improvements):
1. Allowed-stations default ENFORCED (vs WLE's permissive Allow All = TRUE default)
2. Station password in OS keyring (vs WLE's plaintext INI under callsign)

Design verified against WLE decompile findings at dev/scratch/winlink-re/findings/p2p-telnet.md (session larch-clover-delta, 2026-06-01). Design doc lands at docs/design/2026-06-01-tcp-p2p-telnet-design.md.

title: [P2] Fix nested {FormFolder} resolution in forms::http_server
description: Codex 2026-06-01 P1 round finding: for templates in nested folders, {FormFolder} substitution percent-encodes the slash (e.g., Cat1%2FSub1) but axum decodes wildcard captures before reaching the handler's splitn. So a request for Cat1%2FSub1/file.css becomes Cat1/Sub1/file.css in rest, splitn(2, '/') gives folder_segment='Cat1' and file_path='Sub1/file.css', and the server looks under <template_folder>/Sub1/file.css instead of <template_folder>/file.css. The template_folder already pins to the right folder so the lookup is wrong twice over. Surface: src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs:289-291. Fix sketch: drop the folder-segment parse entirely (the session is per-form, so the template folder is implicit) and treat the full wildcard as file_path relative to template_folder. Add a test fixture with nested folder.
notes:
Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-gheo-forms-http-server-hardening. Branch: bd-tuxlink-gheo/forms-http-server-hardening. Created 2026-06-01 17:58:40 UTC by bison-condor-grouse.
Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-gheo-forms-p2-hardening. Branch: bd-tuxlink-gheo/forms-p2-hardening. Created 2026-06-05 04:44:28 UTC by basalt-osprey-willow.

title: [P2] Add asset-size cap on forms::http_server /folder/<path>
description: Codex 2026-06-01 P1 round finding: /folder/<path> requests in forms::http_server read the entire file into memory via std::fs::read (line ~311). A custom-form directory with a large image lets any local client allocate the entire file with no cap, exhausting memory on repeated requests. Fix sketch: (a) reject if file metadata.len() > 8 MB before read; OR (b) stream the file via tokio::fs::File + axum::body::Body::from_stream so memory pressure stays bounded. Option (b) is the more general fix but adds dep weight (axum-extra or tower-http streaming). Option (a) is simpler and fits the WLE-asset use case (form-adjacent images are small).

title: [P2] Bound the forms::http_server submission channel
description: Codex 2026-06-01 P1 round on tuxlink-ytya/p1-webview-infra finding: while a session is open, any local client (with origin spoofed past the new origin check, or pre-fix) can send many valid POSTs and each parsed body is queued into an unbounded mpsc channel. The 1 MB cap limits one request but not total queued memory or duplicate submissions. A bounded/one-shot channel with backpressure would prevent a local flood from growing memory until the compose receiver catches up or closes. Surface: src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs:122 (the mpsc::unbounded_channel() call in FormSession::open). Fix sketch: switch to mpsc::channel(1) (one in-flight submission per session — multiple submits in a single session is anomalous anyway, since the form closes itself after submit per the WLE convention). Update submit_handler to await send().await with a timeout; on full, drop with a 503 response.

title: Wire up VARA TCP transport (HF/VHF) — mirror ARDOP ModemTransport pattern
description: Implement a VARA TCP transport in src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/ as a sibling to the existing ardop/ module, implementing the same ModemTransport trait. VARA TCP exposes two sockets: command (default 8300) ASCII \r-terminated, and data (default 8301) raw byte stream. Common commands: MYCALL <call>, BW <bw>, LISTEN ON/OFF, CONNECT <mycall> <target>, DISCONNECT, ABORT, BUFFER. Responses: CONNECTED/DISCONNECTED/PTT/PENDING/BUFFER/IAMALIVE. Smoke target: 100.83.168.37:8300 (cmd) / 8301 (data) — operator-hosted test instance with no radio attached, so no RADIO-1 transmission concerns. Mirror ardop's file layout: command.rs (commands), wire.rs (parse/emit), session.rs (high-level types), transport.rs (TCP I/O), mod.rs (config + ModemTransport impl). Operator-requested overnight task — bookmark for review next session.
notes:
Phase 1 status (2026-06-01, larch-clover-delta session):

✅ Smoke gate met (probe round-trips against 100.83.168.37 cleanly)
✅ Codec extended with BusyDetected(bool), CommandAck, Registered variants (folded into PR #192 since smoke was PR-scope)
✅ tuxlink-efw2 filed + closed in same commit
✅ PR #192 marked ready for review (was draft)
✅ 23 vara module tests pass; clippy clean on lib + vara_tcp_probe bin
✅ Smoke probe re-run shows zero Unknown(verbatim) events — all banner+ack+status lines now typed

Phase 2 awaits operator direction:
- Pattern reference: ardop/transport.rs (2101 lines) + b2f.rs (460 lines) on main
- Open design questions before Phase 2 code starts:
  1. Mirror ARDOP state machine exactly, or adapt for VARA's different banner/handshake?
  2. How to integrate VARA's per-command OK ack pattern into the ModemTransport trait (which has no "wait for ack" primitive)?
  3. B2F integration: clone ARDOP's b2f.rs or factor out a shared helper?
  4. Per [[v05-modem-design-posture]] (technical merit over compatibility), is there an opportunity for cleaner abstractions vs. ARDOP-clone?

Phase 1 scope (PR #192) is the natural v0.1 codec ship. Phase 2 = subsequent PR.

title: Compose chrome label collisions with form-body labels: To/From/Subject appear in both surfaces
description:
UX issue surfaced during operator browser smoke 2026-06-01: the compose window's chrome has top-level 'To', 'From', 'Subject' inputs (which address the email envelope — recipient callsign(s), originator callsign, message subject). The ICS-213 (and likely future) form components also have 'To (Name and Position)', 'From (Name and Position)', and 'Subject' fields inside the form body (which are operator names + a form-internal subject distinct from the message subject). Operators new to the flow fill the chrome's To and assume the form's To is duplicate; the form's Send button stays disabled because to_name/fm_name/subjectline are unfilled with no clear cause.

Proposed brainstorm:
- Option A: rename form labels in React to disambiguate (e.g., 'To: addressee name', 'From: originator name', 'Form subject (distinct from message subject)'). Wire-format field IDs (to_name, fm_name, subjectline) stay unchanged for WLE/Pat compat.
- Option B: visually group the chrome inputs vs the form inputs more clearly (section borders + headings like 'Envelope' vs 'ICS-213 form').
- Option C: pre-populate form fields from chrome where logical (form's 'From' could pre-fill from chrome's callsign, etc.)

Operator-decision needed before implementation.

title: Floor rate-1/4 LDPC needs PEG (or column-swap-pivot) construction for rank-full H
description: PR for tuxlink-bbin (subsystem #4 FEC) Phase 4 lands a working systematic LDPC encoder for the WiFi-family codes (n=648, n=1296 at rates 1/2-5/6) via seed-iteration on rank-deficiency. The floor rate-1/4 (n=2048, k=512, m=1536) configuration-model construction does not produce rank-full H matrices reliably — 64 seed iterations exhaust without finding a rank-full instance. This is a known property of short-block random regular (3,4) LDPC where the m × m right-half submatrix has substantial rank-deficiency probability under structured row/column weight constraints. The fix is one of: (a) PEG (progressive-edge-growth, Hu-Eleftheriou-Arnold 2005) construction that guarantees full-rank generator; (b) column-swap pivoting in the encoder (record a column permutation, encode under permuted H, present systematic-form codeword on the external API). The Phase 4 encoder_handles_floor_rate14 test is currently #[ignore]'d. Un-ignore once one of (a) or (b) lands. This is the gate for #3's wideband_floor_vs_ardop_target test as well.

title: Author missing CSS for per-form authoring + view components

- .ics309-log-section, .ics309-log-entry, .ics309-log-table

VIEW (read-side, inbound message detail):

SHARED:

WHY THIS WASN'T CAUGHT EARLIER: vitest + jsdom don't load CSS, so the
component tests pass green regardless. The fragility was hidden until
operator browser-smoke. The FormPicker.css fix is the visible-blocker
piece; the rest of these only become visible once the operator clicks

  (--tux-surface, --tux-border, --tux-accent, etc.; see App.css:75-100

  component, or one shared src/forms/forms.css side-effect-imported from
  src/forms/index.ts.

VERIFICATION: Browser-smoke each form (compose-side AND inbound render
via DEV-3 fixtures) before declaring done. Vitest cannot certify visual
correctness here.

title: ARDOP: parse PINGACK + PING events for structured sn_db (deferred from PR #162)
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) found that PR #162 deferred sn_db because we assumed STATUS-string parsing was the only source. Wrong: ardopcf has structured PINGACK and PING events that carry S/N + quality as explicit numeric fields.

ardopcf events (Host_Interface_Commands.md):
- PINGACK SNdB Quality — reply to an outbound PING
- PING caller>target SNdB Quality — incoming ping

Fix (~50 LOC):
1. Add PingAck { sn_db: i32, quality: u32 } and possibly PingReply { caller, target, sn_db, quality } variants to Command enum (command.rs).
2. Extend Command::parse to handle them.
3. In apply_event_to_accumulators_inline, on PingAck → status.sn_db = Some(sn as f32).
4. For the operator to GET a PingAck, they fire an explicit PING command. Options:
   (a) New 'Ping' button in dock that sends PING <target>.
   (b) Automatic PING right before Connect (single shot to populate S/N before handshake).
5. Add Tauri command modem_ardop_ping(target) wrapping cmd.send_line('PING <target>').
6. Tests: stub transport emits PINGACK event; verify status.sn_db updates.

Unblocks the deferred numeric meter from PR #162.
close_reason: Closed

title: ARDOP: expose ARQBW bandwidth selection (200/500/1000/2000 Hz) in Settings + InitConfig
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) identified bandwidth selection as a P1 operator gap. ardopcf supports the ARQBW host command with values 200/500/1000/2000 Hz; the value can be FORCED by the client or negotiated with the server.

Today: ARQBW is already in our setter whitelist (command.rs:is_setter_echo_back) but never wired through. ardopcf uses whatever default is. Operators on a marginal HF path can't drop to 500 Hz; operators with clean conditions can't push to 2000 Hz.

Fix (~30 LOC):
1. Add bandwidth_hz: Option<u32> to ArdopUiConfig (Settings panel field).
2. Add bandwidth_hz: Option<u32> to InitConfig (session.rs).
3. In init_tnc, after LISTEN FALSE, send ARQBW <hz> FORCED if config set.
4. Surface as a Settings dropdown: Auto (default) / 200 / 500 / 1000 / 2000 Hz.

Validates against AE8Q operator walkthrough (https://ae8q.com/2022/03/winlink-hf-ardop-howto/) which says 'Width: 2000, 1000, 500' is operator-selectable. Smoke-blocker if a first connect fails on a marginal path.

title: ARDOP HF UI: frontend Send/Receive button wires modem_ardop_b2f_exchange (post #159)
description:
PR #159 shipped the backend B2F-over-ARDOP path (modem_ardop_b2f_exchange Tauri command) but no UI button calls it. Today the operator must invoke from the WebView console:

  const tok = await __TAURI__.invoke('modem_mint_consent');
  await __TAURI__.invoke('modem_ardop_b2f_exchange', { target: 'W7RMS-10', consentToken: tok });

For the dial to actually deliver mail in the dock UX, add a 'Send/Receive' (or similar) button to ArdopDock that:

1. Reads the configured CMS target from connect config (or accepts a target field — likely the same target the operator dialed in the Connect form).
2. Mints a fresh consent token via modem_mint_consent.
3. Invokes modem_ardop_b2f_exchange.
4. Surfaces progress/errors in the dock (the backend's ProgressSink already emits Transport-tagged session log lines; the dock might also show a simple 'B2F exchange in progress…' state).
5. On completion, refreshes the mailbox view so any received mail appears.

Probably small (~2-4 hours): mostly wiring + a few tests. The backend is done; this is the UX bridge.

Closes the 'ARDOP HF as a real feature' gap noted in PR #159's body.

title: ARDOP HF: extend ModemStatusBroadcaster to poll ardopcf cmd-socket for live meters
description:
The Task 3.4 ModemStatusBroadcaster (introduced by PR #153) emits modem:status every 250ms but only rebroadcasts the cached snapshot — there's no live polling of ardopcf's cmd-socket. Result: the dock renders the meter UI (S/N, VU input, Throughput, bytes RX/TX, uptime, ARQ state grid) but the values never update with real signal-quality data; they stay at whatever modem_ardop_connect set at connect time.

Plan noted this deferral explicitly: 'For v1 the broadcaster just emits the cached snapshot; the richer flow (poll the ardopcf cmd-socket for live S/N, throughput, etc.) is filed as a follow-up bd issue once the dock ships.' I forgot to file the follow-up at session end (2026-05-30 kite-larch-kingfisher); operator surfaced the gap during PR #153 review.

Work:
1. In ModemStatusBroadcaster::spawn (modem_status.rs), don't just snapshot — also drain pending events from the ardopcf CmdSocket via session.try_recv_event() (or similar non-blocking accessor on the installed transport).
2. Map ardopcf events to ModemStatus fields:
   - STATE → ModemState transitions (Connecting/ConnectedIrs/ConnectedIss/Disconnecting/Idle)
   - BUFFER → bytes_rx / bytes_tx + throughput_bps (rolling avg)
   - MYCALL / TARGET → peer
   - PINGREP / PING → sn_db
   - DRIVELEVEL / INPUTPEAKS → vu_dbfs (per ardopcf docs)
   - ARQ_BUSY / ARQ_RX / ARQ_TX → arq_flags
3. Write the updated ModemStatus back via session.set_status(...) (single lock acq) — the existing broadcaster snapshot loop already picks up the change on its next tick.
4. Test plan:
   - Unit: stub a CmdSocket that emits canned events; assert ModemStatus transitions.
   - Operator smoke: connect to a gateway, watch the dock's meters animate during a B2F exchange.

Blocks: 'ARDOP HF as a real feature' depends on this. The dock is useless to the operator if meters never update.

Adjacent: tuxlink-ytg (ARDOP data path + B2F wiring) is parallel — that's the mail-flow side; this is the visibility side. Both needed for a real ARDOP HF v1.
