title: ARDOP data path: coordinate cmd connection-state with DataSocket (EOF-on-DISCONNECT + gate ARQ-before-connect) — wire with B2F
description: Codex adversarial review of tuxlink-6aj (commit 582bbe1) found two related B2F-integration issues in the ARDOP data path that only bite once the sync B2F run_exchange is actually wired to DataSocket (deferred; out of MVP scope): (1) DataSocket::read returns EOF only when the data TCP socket closes, but ardopcf keeps the data socket OPEN and reports link drop via CMD-socket events (DISCONNECTED / NEWSTATE DISC). A sync B2F read_until on the data stream can therefore HANG forever after an on-air disconnect. (2) The data socket is opened at init (before ARQ connect) and DataSocket::read accepts EVERY ARQ-tagged frame; wl2k-go drops ARQ frames while not connected because ARDOPc can emit monitored / non-session data as ARQ — so stale RF data could be fed into the first B2F handshake read. Both need the SAME mechanism: the data path must observe the cmd-socket connection state. FIX when wiring B2F: share a connected/disconnected signal (an AtomicBool or a socket shutdown handle) from the CmdSocket control loop to the DataSocket so (a) a DISCONNECT unblocks / EOFs the data read, and (b) inbound ARQ frames are ignored until connected. Also consider the conn.go outbound flow-control refinement (Write waits for a BUFFER update / retries on CRCFault) for throughput. Reference: wl2k-go conn.go (Read pulls from a dataIn channel the control loop CLOSES on disconnect; tnc.go eof()). Blocks the B2F-over-ARDOP wiring (future phase).
notes: PR #157 opened. Backend wiring complete: ArqState shared atomic between cmd+data sockets, b2f.rs exchange driver, modem_ardop_b2f_exchange Tauri command, EOF-on-DISC + pre-connect frame drop + ARQ-before-write gate all implemented. 422 lib tests green, clippy clean. Frontend Send/Receive button wiring is the follow-up PR.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #159 (merge 5df177d) — run_ardop_b2f_exchange + modem_ardop_b2f_exchange Tauri command + Codex P1 fixes (FAULT→ArqState, DataSocket::drain_pending). Dep tuxlink-6aj shipped as PR #138 in v0.2; closing past the stale dep edge.

title: Add ARDOP HF transport (managed-spawn; generic external-TCP-modem client)
description:
LOCKED 2026-05-24 (operator). Add ARDOP HF support so operators already using ARDOP are not cut off (tuxlink is a tool, not a vanity project).

ARCHITECTURE LOCKED:
- tuxlink LAUNCHES + OWNS the modem lifecycle (managed-spawn): tuxlink is the single arbiter of the one-sound-card conflict — spins Dire Wolf down / ardopcf up on a VHF<->HF switch; SIGINT clean-stop (ardopcf ignores SIGHUP); confirm the audio device is RELEASED before the swap.
- The ARDOP transport is a GENERIC external-TCP-modem client, NOT ARDOP-special-cased: ardopcf / Dire Wolf / VARA / (future) first-party tuxmodem are all instances of ONE transport abstraction (drive a modem over its TCP host protocol + manage its process).

Target modem: ardopcf (LaRue fork). NOT in apt -> bundle as a Tauri sidecar (src-tauri/sidecars/ exists; confirm per-arch triples + license). Host interface: TCP 8515 cmd + 8516 data (+ optional 8514 webgui for level tuning). Target ARDOP v1/ardopcf (v2 abandoned). Audio: ardopcf needs 12 kHz -> ALSA plughw: (resampling) DIRECT to the radio USB codec; avoid PipeWire/Pulse routing for ARQ (Pi bookworm defaults to PipeWire — keep it off the device).

STILL OPEN: PTT/frequency sequencing — MVP (ardopcf -p RTS PTT, G90-proven; operator sets HF dial freq manually; NO rig-control dependency) vs FULL single-pane (tuxlink owns Hamlib CAT+PTT via the tuxlink-5jb rig-control crate). MVP can ship first; FULL depends on tuxlink-5jb.

PRE-PLAN: research locked, NOT an implementation plan — do not start coding before a plan / modem ADR. Full findings: docs/design/ardop-deployment-findings.md.
notes: MVP MERGED to main as PR #138 (c08bd8c base at merge time). Code complete: ADR 0015, winlink::modem::ardop wire/session/data/transport, ModemTransport, ManagedModem (SIGINT/SIGKILL+lsof), examples/ardop_connect CLI. Codex P0 (outbound framing) + 3 P1/P2 fixed before merge; 366 lib tests green. RADIO-1: on-air NOT validated — operator on-site via examples/ardop_connect. Follow-ups still tracked: tuxlink-ig0 (bind-wait passive check), tuxlink-ytg (data-path cmd-state for B2F), tuxlink-5jb (rig-control crate). KEEP OPEN until on-air bring-up + B2F wiring complete.

title: Re-evaluate AX.25 Bluetooth transport: revert nx2 socket pivot to proven serialport-TTY (keep 2y4 safety) — needs on-site on-air test
description:
Operator (RF authority) flagged that tearing down the working Bluetooth packet implementation to chase an unconfirmed double-TX may have been premature. Remote investigation (agent marten-finch-gorge, 2026-05-24) confirms the concern and pinpoints the real culprit.

WHAT THE TEARDOWN ACTUALLY WAS — two conflated changes:
(A) nx2 transport pivot (commits a511c69 + e123da9): swapped the PROVEN-RX serialport-TTY Bluetooth path (/dev/rfcommN, the transport that got the on-air W7MOT-6 DM) for an UNPROVEN-RX raw AF_BLUETOOTH/BTPROTO_RFCOMM socket, on a termios-teardown theory the author admits was never proven (raven-salamander-tamarack Honest reframe). Suspected root of tuxlink-4ef (TX works, RX broken). THIS is the premature teardown.
(B) 2y4 safety bundle (commit ea8ac94): NOT the questionable teardown. Five separable fixes; four are zero-cost to keep (abort-before-TX in write(), no pre-connect DISC, KISS decoder accepts any-port data frame, and the revert of the wrong-lever uhc T1 floor). The fifth is a tunable max-2-SABM + 25 s connect airtime cap. 2y4 ALREADY reverted the uhc T1 floor that was the actual double-TX fix the operator objected to.

KEY MECHANISM FINDING: the baseline connect() (OTA-reply state, a0838a5) keyed a SABM every T1 up to n2_retries+1 = 11 times (for _attempt in 0..=params.n2_retries). At default T1=3 s against a gateway that answered the DM at ~7 s, that fires SABMs at 0/3/6 s = three keyings in fast succession before the reply = the most likely mechanism of the double-key the operator observed on-air. So the double-key was REAL (operator-observed), but the RESPONSE to it (uhc T1 floor 10 s -> ~110 s runaway observed on a wrong-worktree build -> transport pivot) was the over-reaction. The 2y4 bounded-airtime fix (max 2 SABMs then listen) is proportionate and keys FEWER times than the baseline.

RECOMMENDED ACTION (when physically present): on a branch off main/consolidation HEAD, revert the nx2 transport routing so the Bluetooth link uses the serialport-TTY (/dev/rfcommN) path again (proven RX); KEEP all of 2y4. Then run one bounded + abortable + instrumented on-air dial (RADIO-1, operator-keyed) to confirm RX is restored and the rapid double-key is gone.

WHY DEFERRED: operator is fully remote over Tailscale and cannot key the radio or observe on-air results. Potentially-breaking transport changes without on-site follow-up testing do not advance the project and risk confusing future troubleshooting. Execute only when physically present at pandora.

GATE: blocked on tuxlink-9ky (Pi-side Bluetooth Page-Timeout) — no Bluetooth transport of any kind can be tested until the Pi can page the UV-Pro.
design:
Change surface for the transport revert:
- src-tauri/src/winlink/ax25/link.rs — KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth { mac } routing (added by nx2); route Bluetooth back to the serialport-TTY (/dev/rfcommN) open path (commit 4226299 connect_link serial arm).
- src-tauri/src/winlink/ax25/rfcomm.rs — the in-app RfcommSocket (nx2); stop using / remove. NOTE ad79492 (4ef byte trace) also edits rfcomm.rs — handle that overlap.
- connect_link dispatch + the TS DTO btMac (e123da9) + the UI USB/BT device picker (must surface /dev/rfcommN again; tuxlink-sox notes /dev/rfcomm0 did not appear in the picker — discovery gap to fix alongside).
nx2 commits to reverse: a511c69, e123da9 (+ the trace bits in ad79492 touching the socket). Use additive reverts, not history rewinds.
KEEP entirely: ea8ac94 (2y4). Its fixes live at the datalink/AbortableByteLink layer and are transport-agnostic, so they apply equally to the serialport-TTY path.
Mechanism constraint: history-rewind is banned (CLAUDE.md destructive-git ban) and the main checkout is operator state — do this in a worktree on a new branch (bd-ID/slug), never by rewinding a branch.
RELATED: candidate resolution for tuxlink-4ef (RX broken); relates to tuxlink-uhc (double-key), tuxlink-nx2 (the socket), tuxlink-sox (transport persistence + picker gap).
acceptance_criteria: Serialport-TTY (/dev/rfcommN) restored as the active Bluetooth transport; all of 2y4 retained; lib + FE test suites green. On-site bounded+abortable on-air dial (operator-keyed, RADIO-1) shows: two-way exchange (RX restored), no rapid double-key on connect, and a working Cancel that halts TX.
notes: Confirmed this session (2026-05-24): Dire Wolf 1.7 (/usr/bin/direwolf) provides a WORKING TCP/IP software TNC — AGW protocol on :8000 and KISS-over-TCP on :8001, both verified accepting + attaching a TCP client using ADEVICE null (no soundcard, no radio). Bluetooth-free transport option for transport-layer testing (tuxlink -> TCP KISS/AGW -> Dire Wolf -> soundcard -> radio), independent of the BT Page-Timeout. soundmodem (Sailer Linux build) NOT installed; Dire Wolf covers the role. Test config: dev/scratch/dw-tcp-test.conf.

title: Register the tuxlink client SID with Winlink (production CMS rejects unregistered clients)

Confirmed 2026-05-22 over both Telnet+TLS. cms-z (dev, 52.37.245.31, plaintext-only) accepts unregistered clients for dev. Production use AND any legitimate prod-TLS smoke are BLOCKED until the tuxlink client SID is registered with Winlink. See memory project-cms-rejects-unknown-clients.

title: Config-read resilience: a divergent packet.link variant (or unknown field) bricks app-open instead of degrading
description: A parallel branch (tuxlink-nx2, uhc/ax25-tx-timing) added KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth{mac}; its build wrote packet.link={"Bluetooth":...} to the SHARED ~/.config/tuxlink/config.json. Builds without that variant (main, #124/tuxlink-3pb) hard-fail on open: KissLinkConfig is externally-tagged (unknown variant rejected, no serde(other) fallback) and Config is deny_unknown_fields. read_config() returns Err and the app bricks. NOTE: read_config's documented wizard consumers (.ok() -> fresh wizard) degrade gracefully, but SOME startup path propagates the error as fatal — that propagating consumer is the fix locus (degrade to None/wizard, don't brick). OWNER: the nx2/Bluetooth work should ship read-tolerance so merging it + version-skew don't brick configs; coordinate (bd dep) rather than two sessions editing config.rs/link.rs at once. DEV HAZARD (generalizable): concurrent branch builds sharing one config + deny_unknown_fields = cross-contamination bricking (config-file cousin of the worktree :1420 collision). Mitigations: graceful read degradation on schema divergence AND/OR per-worktree TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR for in-dev builds. Found during #124 (tuxlink-3pb) browser smoke 2026-05-22.
close_reason: Landed on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate (PR pending): ka7+p5u via live-config refresh on config_set_*; efo via lenient packet.link deserialize + TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR; 2y4 RADIO-1 bundle (bounded airtime + abort-before-write + no pre-connect DISC + T1-floor revert + KISS nibble), Codex-cleared (4 invariants), TOCTOU refinement tracked in tuxlink-0ja. 338 lib + 453 vitest green.

title: AX.25 Path::encode hardcodes C/R=command for ALL frames — responses (UA/RR-as-response) mis-framed per v2.2
description: frame.rs Path::encode (~line 224) always sets dest C-bit=1 / src C-bit=0 with comment 'cr=true for a command frame; refined in P2' — never refined. CORRECT for commands (SABM/DISC/I-frames), but WRONG for RESPONSES: a UA sent when ANSWERING an inbound call, and RR/RNR/REJ sent as responses to a poll, must per AX.25 v2.2 be dest C-bit=0 / src C-bit=1. A strict v2.2 decoder (RMS gateway, BPQ, Direwolf) may reject/mis-handle our responses. This is the independent-decoder-visible class of bug that peer-to-peer + TCP-relay testing (shared codec) could never catch — surfaced now that we're on-air against a real gateway. NOT the cause of the 2026-05-22 SABM-dial DM (a SABM IS a command and is correctly framed; address shift, SSID reserved/ext bits, control 0x3F, KISS port-0 wrap, and no-FCS-for-KISS all verified spec-correct). Fix: thread command/response through Path::encode (or Frame::encode) so response frames set the C/R bits correctly. Most impactful on the LISTEN/answer path (tuxlink-as-master sends UA) + sustained exchanges (RR acks).
notes: Reopened 2026-06-02 (taiga-arroyo-clover): PR #228 closed by operator — AX.25 C/R bit changes touch on-air TX bytes and need verification against a real v2.2 gateway. Out of scope for this session (focus was telnet/UI). Re-land after on-air verification.

title: Packet panel: transport segment resets to USB Serial instead of persisting last-selected (Bluetooth)
description: On-air 2026-05-22: the packet connect panel's transport segment 'constantly resets to USB Serial' instead of staying on Bluetooth (the last-selected mode). Likely cause (verify, do not assume): USB and Bluetooth BOTH map to linkKind:'Serial' (per spec/code 'USB or Bluetooth-RFCOMM both use kind:serial'), so when the panel re-derives the segment from config on reload/poll it can't distinguish BT from USB and defaults to USB. Fix: persist/derive the BT-vs-USB segment distinctly (stored sub-kind, or infer from device path /dev/rfcommN vs /dev/ttyUSB*). Frontend src/packet/PacketConnectionPanel.tsx. Related: the bound /dev/rfcomm0 also did NOT appear in the BT device picker (packet_list_serial_devices discovery gap) — operator had to type the path.

title: Packet AX.25: radio double-keys on connect; configured T1/TXdelay/KISS params not respected
description: On-air 2026-05-22 (UV-Pro, ch5, dial W7MOT-6): the connect caused the radio to key TWICE in very fast succession (operator: 'not good'), and the operator (RF-expert) reports the default AX.25 parameters (T1/TXdelay/persistence/slot) are NOT being honored. INVESTIGATE (do not assume a cause): (1) config->Ax25Params mapping (PacketConfig into_params) — are the configured T1/timing values actually used, or defaults? (2) kiss_param frame correctness — the KISS command nibble MUST be 1-6 (config: TXDELAY=1/P=2/SlotTime=3/...); if any is 0 (data) it would TX the payload and cause spurious keying. (3) connect() T1 retransmit loop (datalink.rs) — a too-short T1 vs gateway RF latency fires a 2nd SABM before the 1st response arrives. The connect reached the gateway (DM back) so the transport works; this is RF-politeness/timing. Needs careful look, not a session-end guess. Evidence: session log 01:41:52 connect -> 01:41:59 DM.
notes: SUPERSEDED-IN-PART by tuxlink-2y4: the T1 floor (PR #123) is to be REVERTED; the real fix (bounded airtime + abort + no pre-connect DISC) is 2y4. The double-key was one symptom of a broader unsafe connect envelope.

title: Validate packet B2F against an INDEPENDENT KISS decoder (Dire Wolf / kernel AX.25), no RF
description: The tuxlink-3wh end-to-end proof wires two tuxlink peers together, which exercises the whole stack but SHARES the codec — a symmetric framing/FCS bug would be invisible. Validate KISS+AX.25 interop against an independent implementation with no RF: e.g. Dire Wolf as a software KISS TNC + the Linux kernel AX.25 stack (kissattach) as the connected-mode peer, on localhost/audio-loopback. Needs operator OK for 'sudo apt install direwolf ax25-tools' (per no-unilateral-apt rule). Catches codec bugs the peer-to-peer test cannot. Ride on tuxlink-7fr.

title: Packet: real in-app Bluetooth/USB TNC connect flow (pairing/binding support)
description: tuxlink does NOT meaningfully connect a Bluetooth TNC: the hard part (bluetoothctl pair + rfcomm bind /dev/rfcommN, needs sudo, deprecated on modern BlueZ) is unsupported manual OS work; the app only scans /dev for an already-bound node + a one-line hint (spec 4.1 punts pairing to the OS). serialBaud is meaningless for RFCOMM but still passed to serialport::open() (untested). Decide + build: either in-app BT discover/pair/bind, or at minimum a guided setup flow; verify the serial open path on real USB + BT hardware. Same applies to USB device-open (never run on hardware).

title: Packet: live connect/listen status feed (backend events -> ribbon/panel)
description: The ribbon/status bar + panel show only static state ('Packet 1200 - not connected') and the Listen control's 'armed' is purely frontend-local, because there is no backend->frontend feed of real connect/listen state. Wire backend status/events (Connecting/Connected/Listening-armed/Answering <call>/Disconnected) so the UI reflects what the link is actually doing. Honest-static was the interim fix; this is the real version.
notes: BUILT + GATED (chasm-sorrel-glade, 2026-05-22, commit 0aa1002 on bd-tuxlink-7fr/ax25-packet): honest live status feed landed. BackendStatus::Listening variant (armed-but-idle, distinct from Connecting); initial_packet_status maps Listen->Listening / DialTo->Connecting (pure, unit-tested); frontend StatusDto union + formatConnectionState + formatStatusState gain Listening, normalizeTransportLabel renders 'Packet 1200'; derivePacketUiState wires the live backend_status poll into the packet indicator, replacing the hard-coded 'not connected' placeholder (honest by construction). Gates: cargo --lib 306 ok, vitest 419 ok, tsc clean. Left IN_PROGRESS pending operator app-smoke of the live indicator (UI-visible feed; real proof is seeing it in the running app + on-air) — same posture as tuxlink-3wh. Real-peer-in-Connected + 'Answering <call>' transient split to a new on-air-only follow-up (status sink in native_packet_connect).

title: ax25: clean Stop for a serial/Bluetooth packet listen (abort over serialport)
description: packet Listen Stop works over TCP (cms_abort shuts the cloned TcpStream -> recv_frame ConnectionAborted -> answer() unwinds). For a USB/BT serial KISS TNC, connect_link_with_abort returns None (no try_clone equivalent for a serialport handle), so abort() is a no-op on the link and a waiting answer() can't be stopped except by disconnecting the TNC. Add a serial abort mechanism: e.g. a stop-flag the answer poll loop checks, or a serialport break/close handle. Built 2026-05-22 on bd-tuxlink-7fr/ax25-packet.
notes: BUILT + GATED (chasm-sorrel-glade, 2026-05-22, commit 12486ff on bd-tuxlink-nj1/serial-stop, PR #119). Serial/BT packet Listen Stop now works: AbortableByteLink (link.rs) wraps the serial link and returns ConnectionAborted once the backend's existing 'aborting' flag is set; recv_frame already maps that to an abort, so answer()/connect() unwind on the next poll (bounded by the 200ms serial read timeout). connect_link_with_abort takes the abort Arc<AtomicBool>; TCP keeps its immediate socket-shutdown fast path (flag unused there). native_packet_connect threads the existing aborting flag in (no new state). Hardware-free tests: AbortableByteLink flag->ConnectionAborted read; answer() unwinds with ConnectionAborted on an aborting link. cargo --lib 311 ok. Left IN_PROGRESS pending OPERATOR on-air verification (a real Stop against a serial/BT TNC e.g. UV-Pro /dev/rfcomm0 — RADIO-1).

title: ax25: resolve Ax25Stream::read() Ok(0)-vs-EOF contract when P4 wires run_exchange
description: P2 review (opus EXTRA-1 + Codex #9): Ax25Stream::read returns Ok(0) for 'no data yet' (link open), which collides with the Read EOF convention. When P4 wires the B2F run_exchange driver to Ax25Stream, the driver MUST NOT treat Ok(0) as EOF — loop/retry until session completes, OR change read() to block-until-data-or-closed. Doc comment already on read(). Decide at P4 wiring.

title: [bug] CMS locator transmitted at full precision — ignores position_precision (privacy)
description: Found during the tuxlink-686 position-subsystem brainstorm (2026-05-22). position_precision (default FourCharGrid) is honored for ribbon DISPLAY (formatGridForDisplay) but NOT for the CMS locator transmitted on-air: winlink_backend.rs ~L627 builds locator = config.identity.grid.clone() (full precision), passed to ExchangeConfig.locator and transmitted verbatim by handshake.rs:52 as '; <target> DE <mycall> (<locator>)'. A 6-char stored grid (e.g. a 6-char wizard entry) broadcasts at full precision regardless of the privacy setting. Affects shipping v0.0.1. Fix: reduce the locator to position_precision at construction (a single broadcast_grid() helper). The tuxlink-686 arbiter closes this via broadcast_grid(); decide whether to also patch v0.0.1 standalone before release.

description: v0.1 position subsystem (absorbs tuxlink-2y5 manual-grid + tuxlink-2ob GPS). Brainstormed 2026-05-22 (basin-arroyo-osprey + operator). Single position arbiter holds {source: Manual|Gps, grid, last_fix}. Manual entry via inline-edit in the ribbon (Approach A) + amber MANUAL/GPS source chip. GPS via gpsd client (LC29C already on /dev/ttyAMA0+pps0, gpsd serving). Source contract: explicit source, Manual sticky (GPS never auto-overrides), operator-only switching, precision enforced independent of source (4-char broadcast default), source always visible. Wizard grid = initial value (source defaults Gps); deliberate inline-edit pins Manual. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-22-position-subsystem-design.md.

Each task got per-task two-stage review (spec+quality) + fixes. Cross-provider Codex gate found 2 P1s, BOTH FIXED in 48187b3: (1) on-air locator now honors gps_state privacy (GPS broadcast only when BroadcastAtPrecision; Off/LocalUiOnly fall back to config grid; gpsd spawn gated on !=Off); (2) position_status now surfaces the live broadcast_grid so the ribbon shows what's transmitted (shared effective_broadcast_locator used by both native_connect + position_status). 212 lib + 41 vitest green; gpsfake e2e validated (JN58td->JN58). All pushed to origin/bd-tuxlink-686/position-subsystem. NOT closed pending operator smokes.

title: Connect-path hardening: bounded DNS, total connect deadline, abort-label accuracy (Codex adrev follow-ups)
description:
Deferred Medium/Low findings from the 2026-05-21 Codex adversarial review of the connect cluster (gqo+9z2). The two High findings (single-flight #1, abort TOCTOU #2) + cleanup #7 were fixed in the cluster before merge; these remain:

#3 (Med) winlink/telnet.rs: to_socket_addrs() DNS resolution is UNBOUNDED before the bounded connect_timeout. A blackholed/hung resolver reintroduces the stall gqo fixed (no socket registered yet, so cms_abort can't unblock it). Fix needs a DNS-with-timeout (threaded resolve + timeout, or async resolver). Low real-world impact now: cms-z + server.winlink.org are single-A and resolve in ~0.07s.

#4 (Med) winlink/telnet.rs connect_with_timeout: CONNECT_TIMEOUT is per-address, not a total deadline. A host with N blackholed A/AAAA records can block up to N*15s. Fix: pass a total deadline; per-addr remaining = deadline - elapsed. Low impact now (current hosts single-address), but IPv6 AAAA could add addresses.

#5 (Med) winlink_backend.rs abort_aware_outcome: maps ANY error to Cancelled when aborting is set. If a real (pre-abort) failure surfaces just as the operator clicks Abort, it's mislabeled 'CMS connection aborted.' instead of the true reason. Cosmetic but can mask a real error. Fix: track whether abort actually shut down THIS connect's socket (epoch), or only map to Cancelled when a socket was actually torn down.

#6 (Low) winlink/telnet.rs connect_with_timeout: returns only the LAST address error. If addr1 = ConnectionRefused and addr2 = TimedOut, the user sees only TimedOut, losing the more actionable earlier failure. Diagnostic-only.

Raw transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-05-21-connect-cluster-codex.md (local-only, gitignored).
notes: Implemented #3/#4/#6 in telnet.rs (commit c612a48 on bd-tuxlink-lbg/connect-hardening). #5 implemented then REVERTED: Codex re-review (codex review --base main, gpt-5.5) flagged P2 — the only-Cancelled-on-teardown fix regresses the abort contract for pre-socket aborts (Abort during DNS/connect would surface as Error not Cancelled) and didn't fix the socket-exists race anyway. winlink_backend.rs left == main (aborting-keyed mapping is contract-correct). 166 lib green; full suite re-running pre-push. PR pending.

Operators need to set their Maidenhead grid manually (Settings/edit-grid UI) so position is correct without a GPS device. Honors the 4-char default precision (project GPS convention). Distinct from tuxlink-2ob (GPS device integration).

Scoping done: NO runtime Settings screen exists yet (only the wizard sets grid); 2y5 needs (a) a backend config_set_grid command (validate Maidenhead 4/6-char, write_config_atomic, refresh ribbon), (b) a frontend edit-grid surface — UX shape undecided (inline-editable ribbon Grid field vs a new Settings panel), which warrants a brief brainstorm per the project's UI norms. Independent of the connect cluster; clean to do as its own bd-<id> branch next session.

title: Compose window slow to open (second WebKitGTK webview parses full eager bundle)
description: Opening New Message spawns a separate Tauri WebviewWindow = a new WebKitGTK web context that reloads + re-parses the ENTIRE app bundle (App.tsx eagerly imports AppShell/Wizard/mailbox) before routing to <Compose>. On a Pi this is the observed slowness. Operator chose to keep the separate-window architecture (ng3 smoke #8 option a). Fix options: (a) code-split so the /compose route lazy-loads a lean chunk (React.lazy + Suspense on AppShell/Wizard in App.tsx) — keeps option a, cuts the compose webview's parse cost; (b) move compose to an in-window docked panel (no second webview) — fastest, but reverses AMD-6 + compose-window machinery (own brainstorm). Recommend (a) first.

title: Top menu bar + titlebar are native gray; Mock B spec is dark blue (custom chrome)
description: The File/Message/Session menu bar and the window titlebar render in the system gray theme; the approved Mock B design shows a dark-blue top. They are NATIVE (tauri.conf has no decorations:false; menu.rs builds a native Menu via app.set_menu). Matching the spec requires custom window chrome: set decorations:false and build a dark-blue HTML titlebar + menu bar (re-wiring the menu events as HTML, window drag region + min/max/close controls). Reported by operator 2026-05-21.
notes: In progress on bd-tuxlink-0ic/native-winlink-client (worktrees/bd-tuxlink-0ic-native-winlink-client). Spec fd5a5c8 / plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-21-window-chrome-ng3.md. Folds in tuxlink-msr (compose minimal bar) + operator-approved option b (Reply accelerators open a reply window). Subagent-driven execution under build-robust-features.

title: Native mailbox has no read/unread state — messages never mark read on open
description: Clicking a message in the inbox never switches it from unread to read. native_mailbox.rs hardcodes MessageMeta.unread=false (everything appears read) and there is no read-state persistence or mark-read path. Implement: track unread per message in the native store (e.g. a read-marker / per-folder read set), mark read when message_read opens it, and reflect it in mailbox_list so the list + sidebar unread badge update. Reported by operator 2026-05-21.
close_reason: Implemented read/unread state end-to-end: per-message .read sidecars in the native store (inbox-scoped unread), mark_read trait method + NativeBackend override, message_read marks-on-open best-effort, useMessage invalidates mailbox queries for prompt badge refresh. TDD at every layer; full suite green; grim-smoked. Commit b840b4d.

title: Compose opens a separate window with duplicate top-of-pane controls (spec)

Decide the intended model (inline compose pane/modal in the main window, OR a compose window WITHOUT the duplicated main-window chrome) against docs/design and rework. Reported by operator 2026-05-21 during the native-client UI review.

title: Project dashboard: pure-derived bd-first, tailnet-served, single-screen, multi-track + live-session
description: Local-only project dashboard for monitoring tuxlink while out (esp. during long autonomous/parallel agent sessions). Design brainstormed 2026-05-21 (alder-arroyo-bluff); spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-21-project-dashboard-design.md. Approach A: small Python collector + tiny HTTP server, server-rendered HTML, ~15s recompute cache, served on the tailnet (pandora 100.110.97.120, free port). PURE-DERIVED bd-first: zero hand-maintained progress config — state/blockers/velocity/ETAs computed live from bd + git + gh + worktree mtimes; tracks grouped by bd LABEL (one-time labeling pass; untracked issues bucket, fix-in-bd not in-config). Anti-goal = LFST dashboard drift (config + plan-markdown slug-inference). Trust model: one source per fact, show-your-work drilldown, honest uncertainty (ETA ranges + 'insufficient data'), DRIFT-detection card (merged-not-closed, stalled, unclaimed worktrees), minimal human surface. Scope: 3 tracks (v0.0.1 / native-client / RF-modem) + overall-progress timeline (real date axis, future=range bands) + live-session panel. Read-only; never transmits; systemd user service.
design: Components: collectors/{bd,git,gh,worktrees}.py -> model.py (pure, fixture-testable) -> render.py (Jinja2 self-contained HTML) + server.py (http.server, /api/state.json, cache) + settings.toml (bind/port/repo/refresh only). No DB. Timeline fill=elapsed-time (neutral); work-done shown via pip state + per-track bars. Future milestones=range bands. ETA separates throughput-bound from decision-gated work. Bind tailnet IP not 0.0.0.0. systemctl --user (no sudo). v1 lives in tools/dashboard/ in-repo; extractable later (transfer goal).

The fix: thread the configured/active transport into the ribbon via formatConnectionState (StatusBarData.connection); drop the hardcoded 'telnet ready'.

title: Bootstrap: supervise/reap in-flight Pat child on mid-spawn app quit
description: Surfaced by Codex R2 on tuxlink-22l (impl review, 2026-05-20, finding #5). The app-start bootstrap runs on a detached std::thread; during the PatBackend::spawn window the Pat child is not yet owned by managed state (BackendState). If the app quits mid-spawn (the ~1s announce window), there is no cancellation/supervisor path to reap the in-flight Pat child, so it can orphan (adrev #8 only partially addressed by PatProcess Drop, which only fires once PatProcess is constructed+dropped). Fix: store an in-flight supervisor/cancel handle in managed state and wire app-exit teardown (on_window_event / RunEvent::ExitRequested) to kill+reap any child started before backend installation. Narrow edge (recoverable via pid file), deferred from the 22l PR.

title: Reconcile wizard test-send Pat URL (hardcoded :8080) with bootstrap's ephemeral Pat port
description: Surfaced by Codex adrev R1 on tuxlink-22l (2026-05-20). wizard.rs::resolve_pat_base_url() hardcodes http://127.0.0.1:8080 (or PAT_URL), but tuxlink-22l's app-start bootstrap spawns Pat on an EPHEMERAL OS-assigned port held in the backend state. The wizard test-send runs first-run (pre-config, before the bootstrap spawns anything) so today they don't collide, but two notions of 'the Pat URL' is a latent bug + a double-spawn / mbox-dir / pid-file contention risk once both exist (adrev #13). Reconcile: route the wizard test-send through a shared spawn helper / the same backend, or have it spawn its own ephemeral Pat, and add an advisory single-instance guard (file lock on mbox_dir). Decide v0.0.1 scope.
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #95 OPEN (ready-for-review, base feat/v0.0.1). Wizard test-send now spawns its own ISOLATED tempfile Pat + /api/connect + poll + graceful shutdown; sender-only reply match; cfg!(test)+CI fail-closed gate. Built via TDD (is_autoresponder_reply) + 2 Codex rounds (R1 7 findings, R2 verification) — all dispositioned; transcripts in dev/adversarial/. Gates green (cargo test/vitest 304/build, no warnings). Live path operator-verified like live_cms_smoke (#92). Closes on #95 merge.

title: Selectable color schemes + night/tactical modes (all-red full-screen, grayscale)
description:
Operator request (2026-05-20, v0.5 target). The mock-d 'mailapp-minimal' amber-on-warm-dark scheme is the right v0.0.1 default, but operators have varied preferences and field needs. Add USER-SELECTABLE color schemes.

Two night/tactical flavors are first-class requirements (not just nice-to-have):
1. ALL-RED night/tactical mode — optimized for full-screen use, designed to PRESERVE NIGHT VISION while remaining readable. NOT a flat monochrome dump: tasteful red-channel-dominant design with deliberate luminance hierarchy (deep red surfaces, brighter red text/accents) so structure stays legible at low light.
2. GRAYSCALE mode — for systems already using a hardware/software night-vision-preservation screen overlay (red gel / NVG-safe filter), where the app should be neutral grayscale so the external overlay does the color work.

ENABLING FOUNDATION: tuxlink-cbz centralized all component colors onto the --tux-* design tokens on :root. A selectable scheme is therefore an alternate token set (e.g. [data-theme='night-red'] :root-scope overrides). No per-component CSS rewrite needed — this is the payoff of the cbz token discipline.
design: APPROVED 2026-05-21 (sandbar-heron-bayou; operator approved the visual mock dev/scratch/color-schemes-mock.html). Scope: selectable PRESETS only (custom editor = later issue). Three schemes as [data-theme] token overrides of the primitive tokens in App.css :root (semantic --tux-* layer remaps automatically). DEFAULT = unchanged (no data-theme / 'default'). NIGHT-RED (data-theme=night-red): bg#0a0000 surface#160000 surface-2#210000 elevated#2b0000 border#3d0000 border-strong#5c0000 border-soft#1c0000 text#ff5b5b text-dim#b34a4a text-faint#6e2e2e accent#ff2d2d accent-2#ff6b6b accent-soft rgba(255,45,45,.14) unread-dot#ff7a7a success#ff9a9a error#ff2020 info#cc5a5a form-tag#d96666; --tux-accent-fg#160000 --tux-danger-fg#160000 --tux-danger-surface rgba(255,32,32,.16) --tux-focus-ring rgba(255,45,45,.55). GRAYSCALE (data-theme=grayscale): bg#0d0d0d surface#161616 surface-2#1f1f1f elevated#262626 border#2e2e2e border-strong#404040 border-soft#1a1a1a text#e8e8e8 text-dim#9a9a9a text-faint#5f5f5f accent#cfcfcf accent-2#e8e8e8 accent-soft rgba(207,207,207,.12) unread-dot#f0f0f0 success#c4c4c4 error#9e9e9e info#b4b4b4 form-tag#bcbcbc; --tux-accent-fg#0d0d0d --tux-danger-fg#0d0d0d --tux-danger-surface rgba(158,158,158,.18) --tux-focus-ring rgba(207,207,207,.45). DECISIONS: (1) switcher = native View>Color scheme submenu emitting menu:view:scheme:<id>; (2) persistence = localStorage 'tuxlink.colorScheme', applied pre-paint in main.tsx to avoid FOUC; (3) monochrome schemes collapse semantic hue -> state via luminance + icons, not color (kept success lighter / error vivid-darker for separation).
acceptance_criteria: Default scheme = current mock-d amber/warm-dark, unchanged. A settings control switches schemes at runtime + persists. At least: default, all-red night/tactical (full-screen, night-vision-preserving, readable, tasteful), grayscale (for external NVG-overlay use). All schemes implemented as --tux-* token overrides (no per-component color literals reintroduced). Each scheme passes the visual-companion smoke against the main surfaces.
close_reason: Implemented selectable color schemes (presets) as design-token overrides: default (unchanged) + night/tactical all-red (night-vision-preserving luminance ladder) + grayscale (for external NVG overlay). colorScheme.ts model (localStorage, pre-paint apply), App.css [data-theme] blocks, native View>Color scheme submenu + AppShell handler. TDD (8 model tests + menu manifest); gates green (frontend 321, tsc, Rust all pass); both schemes grim-smoked in real WebKitGTK — all components recolor via tokens. Custom-scheme editor deferred (later issue). Commit 5af09aa.

title: CI build-linux path filter excludes frontend (src/**) — frontend-only PRs run NO CI
description: release.yml build-linux pull_request paths = [src-tauri/**, external/tuxlink-pat, .gitmodules, .github/workflows/release.yml]. Frontend-only changes (src/**, index.html, package.json) trigger NO CI at all — discovered when PR #85 (CSS+HTML) ran zero checks (merged on local gates + operator smoke). A frontend change that breaks tsc/vitest/the vite build would NOT be caught by CI. Add a frontend CI job (tsc --noEmit + vitest run, and optionally the vite build) triggered on src/**, index.html, package.json, vite/ts config. Connects to the tuxlink-cbz theme (no automated frontend/visual gate). Found 2026-05-20 (grouse-esker-juniper).
notes:
Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-b2s-ci-frontend-job. Branch: bd-tuxlink-b2s/ci-frontend-job. Created 2026-05-20 21:24:42 UTC by fen-sycamore-falcon.
DESIGNED 2026-05-20 (fen-sycamore-falcon) but DEFERRED to operator: the fix is a separate workflow .github/workflows/frontend-ci.yml (a paths filter is workflow-level, not per-job, so the frontend gate needs its own workflow). Creating it tripped the security_reminder_hook (workflow edits run with repo secrets → warrant human review); I declined to end-run a security guard while unsupervised. The workflow is injection-safe (no github.event.* in run: steps). Operator: add .github/workflows/frontend-ci.yml triggered on pull_request paths [src/**, index.html, package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, tsconfig*.json, vite.config.*, the workflow itself] + workflow_dispatch; one ubuntu job: checkout, pnpm/action-setup@v4 version 10, setup-node@v4 node 20 cache pnpm, pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, pnpm tsc --noEmit, pnpm vitest run, pnpm build. (pnpm 10.33.3 / node 20 / lockfileVersion 9.0 locally.) Verifies by triggering on its own PR (it changes the workflow file). No app smoke needed.

title: Session-log history ring buffer for session_log_snapshot replay (Task 15 follow-up)
description: Surfaced during integration-commit fix b6331de. session_log_snapshot is now implemented + registered + projects LogLineDto correctly, but returns an EMPTY Vec because no history exists to snapshot: the WinlinkBackend trait exposes only stream_log() (live BoxStream, no replay) and PatBackend uses a tokio::broadcast channel with no snapshot-readable history. Result: a late-mounting SessionLog seeds with [] and only sees live tail from mount onward; spec ~§198 wants the snapshot to seed prior session history. Fix: add a bounded ring buffer to the log mechanism (PatBackend / AppBackend) that session_log_snapshot reads. The command + registration + DTO projection are already in place (b6331de); only the buffer is missing. Only matters once a live session produces history (post tuxlink-22l).
notes:
ADREV INVERSION (Codex R1 on tuxlink-22l, 2026-05-20): the ring buffer is NOT a post-22l nicety — it is a PREREQUISITE for 22l's session-log delivery. tokio::broadcast only serves current subscribers, so Pat's startup lines drop before the drain subscribes; a durable last-N ring buffer (monotonic seq) is required so session_log_snapshot seeds + snapshot-then-tail loses nothing. Bumped P3->P2. See spec 2026-05-20-pat-spawn-bootstrap-design.md §11.1.
IMPLEMENTED as Task A of PR #88 (SessionLogState ring buffer + seq). Closes on PR #88 merge.
close_reason: Delivered by PR #88 (merged 2026-05-21T12:59Z). 22l: PatBackend::spawn + app-start bootstrap. xx3: SessionLogState ring buffer (Task A). xyd: spawn hardening verified by peregrine-spruce-knoll — recv_timeout announce deadline (real, not in-loop Instant), Stdio::null stdout drain, Child kill+wait on every error path (L193/207/224) + impl Drop (L284), no bare ? between spawn and success return.

title: Wizard keyring UX: locked login keyring on auto-login Linux triggers a confusing gnome-keyring prompt (not in-app guidance)
description: Found during M1 smoke 2026-05-20 on the dev Pi (auto-login enabled = Raspberry Pi OS default; login Secret Service collection Locked=true). pam_gnome_keyring is configured but cannot unlock the login keyring because auto-login enters no password. The wizard's first keyring write (wizard_persist_cms) then triggers gnome-keyring's OWN unlock dialog, which asks for the keyring password — operators confuse this with the sudo/login password and get rejected. The Rust side is correct (map_keyring_error -> WizardError::Locked; snapshot read before destructive set_password). Gap is operator-experience: (1) the wizard should pre-flight the Secret Service lock state and surface clear in-app guidance + a recovery path (unlock, or set the login keyring password-less for auto-unlock) instead of letting the raw gnome prompt confuse the operator; (2) docs/install.md should document the auto-login keyring caveat for Pi deployments. Bites the common emcomm deployment (auto-login Pi). Not an M1 code blocker; the keyring write itself works once the collection is unlocked.
notes: ROOT CAUSE of N7CPZ live-CMS auth failures (2026-05-21, gully-sage-heron, verified from source): Pat's reader go-keyring v0.2.8 searches ONLY the 'login' Secret Service collection (keyring_unix.go GetLoginCollection()), while the Rust keyring crate (wizard writer) creates items in the DEFAULT collection (keyring-3.6.3 secret_service.rs). On a normal desktop login==default so they interoperate (PR #75 verified there). On pandora post-keyring-incident they DIVERGED: login keyring moved aside (login.keyring.broken-20260520.bak), active default = Default_keyring. So wizard/secret-tool writes land in Default_keyring but Pat reads 'login' (absent) -> not found -> interactive prompt -> non-interactive live_cms_smoke stalls (context deadline). FIX: restore the standard setup where the login keyring IS the active unlocked default. Cred-handling implication (8zt/ADR 0011): tuxlink's CMS auth requires the cred in the LOGIN collection; the spec should state this collection constraint, and the wizard should detect/repair a broken-login / default!=login environment (this is the qn8 UX). NOT a tuxlink code bug.

title: Wizard test-send: Busy from a non-test-send mutex holder leaves window stuck in sending
description: Codex Part-97 re-round of b45f592 (P1 liveness, NOT transmission-safety — no double-TX path). The single-flight tokio::Mutex is shared by wizard_persist_cms, wizard_persist_offline, wizard_run_test_send (wizard.rs). The frontend treats ANY WizardError::Busy as a strict no-op that stays in 'sending'. If the contention came from a persist command or another window (not a concurrent test-send), no TEST_SEND_RESULT ever arrives and the window stays in 'sending' indefinitely. Mitigations: unreachable in the single-window first-run flow (persist completes before test-send), and recoverable via the always-present Skip control during sending. Fix options: track mutex holder/source so Busy-from-test-send vs Busy-from-other is distinguishable, OR add a 'sending' watchdog timeout -> failed. Deferred from M1 by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-9w8-wizard-sending-watchdog. Branch: bd-tuxlink-9w8/wizard-sending-watchdog. Created 2026-05-21 01:39:40 UTC by fen-sycamore-falcon.

title: Fix File → Quit native handling (post-PR-70 smoke gap)
description:
Smoke test of PR #70 (Task 7 menu bar) on 2026-05-19 surfaced one failure: clicking File → Quit (or pressing Ctrl+Q) does nothing. Root cause: wire_menu_events in src-tauri/src/menu.rs emits the menu:file:quit event to the 'menu' Tauri channel and stops. The React frontend (src/App.tsx — still Tauri-React boilerplate) has no listen('menu', ...) handler, so the event fires into dead air.

Every menu item has the same shape, but Quit is the one item users have a hard expectation about ('Quit must quit').

Fix: special-case Quit in wire_menu_events so it works natively without waiting for Tasks 9+ to wire the frontend listener. ~4 lines:

  if id == 'menu:file:quit' { app_for_handler.exit(0); }

The event still emits, so future frontend listeners (e.g., 'discard unsaved draft?' dialog in Task 14 Compose) can still observe + intercept later.

Discipline: plumbing-class per feedback_discipline_triage_rule — spec is this issue body, TDD-direct, no Codex round.
acceptance_criteria: File → Quit closes the app cleanly. Ctrl+Q closes the app cleanly. menu_test still passes (event manifest unchanged). cargo build --lib still warning-free. Operator-smoke-confirmed before merge.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-r21-fix-quit-native. Branch: bd-tuxlink-r21/fix-quit-native. Created 2026-05-19 19:27:30 UTC by willow-cypress-heron.

title: Airtime-budget helper for the inbound selector (fast-follow)
description: Fast-follow to the RF-first inbound-selector redesign (2026-06-09, gulch-ivy-kite). Operator chose 'manual triage now, budget helper as fast-follow'. After the manual airtime-triage table ships (per-message Est. Time + running airtime/size total, sort by Est. Time desc), add a budget-target helper: operator sets a target (time e.g. '8 min' tied to a propagation/battery window, or size), the panel highlights messages that bust the budget and offers a one-click 'fit to budget' selection (e.g. greedy smallest-first or knapsack). Most valuable on bandwidth-budgeted RF (ARDOP/VARA HF). Depends on the core selector redesign landing + on-air validation of the manual experience first. Part of the converged fzek+nwwv selector feature.

title: MessageList multi-select a11y: aria-selected should reflect bulk selection + aria-multiselectable/grid nav
description: From tuxlink-etxt Task 8 review. The message list now has two selection notions: open (isOpen, drives reading pane) and bulk (inSelection, the Set). aria-selected on each role=row currently reflects only isOpen, so a bulk-selected-but-not-open row announces aria-selected=false (wrong for SR). Full fix needs: aria-selected={isOpen || inSelection}; container role=grid + aria-multiselectable=true; and the grid keyboard-navigation pattern (arrow roving, Home/End) to make the multiselectable announcement honest. Deferred as a dedicated a11y pass (half-wiring multiselectable without the nav pattern is worse than none). Tuxlink-etxt ships Ctrl/Shift+click + Space/Enter; this is the SR-completeness follow-up.

title: GRIB region map picker: control/interaction polish (post-#481 smoke)
description: Operator smoke 2026-06-09: the GRIB region map (Message -> GRIB File Request... -> Region section) renders and works (box-drag -> region fields populate), but the controls are 'not an amazing implementation' and want polish. Component: src/map/GridMapPicker.tsx (box mode) + how it sits in src/grib/GribRequestPanel.tsx. Specifics TBD via operator walkthrough at follow-up start; pair with the design pass for tuxlink's map-picker UX. Lower severity than the Position-form rework (this one is usable, just rough).

title: store() seeds search-index unread with Inbox-only predicate (inconsistent with list() after tuxlink-etxt)
description: native_mailbox.rs store() (~line 66) seeds the search-index 'unread' column as 'folder == MailboxFolder::Inbox'. After tuxlink-etxt Task 2, list()/list_user() surface unread for Inbox + Archive + user folders from the .read sidecar. So a message stored DIRECTLY to Archive (or a user folder) gets index unread=false while list() reports unread=true. Orthogonal to the read/unread FEATURE (which reads the sidecar, not the index); affects only search-filtered-by-unread. Fix: align store()'s index seed with list()'s predicate (matches!(folder, Inbox | Archive)) and consider user-folder store paths. Found in tuxlink-etxt Task 2 code review (2026-06-08).

title: De-dup haversine: catalog has its own src/catalog/distance.ts
description: At the #472 merge, #465 (catalog-builder) had shipped its own src/catalog/distance.ts (haversine + grid distance) rather than consuming the shared src/forms/position/distance.ts created in tuxlink-egmp B4. Two parallel distance utils now exist. Consolidate onto the shared src/forms/position/distance.ts (export haversineKm + distanceBetweenGrids) and update the catalog imports; delete src/catalog/distance.ts. Pure cleanup, no behavior change. Surfaced during the contacts-favorites merge (osprey-mink-magpie).

title: Opt-in permitted tile server + Rust tile-gatekeeper + Settings URL field (finer-than-4-char map precision)
description: Split from tuxlink-z9u4 per the 2026-06-08 offline-map adversarial review (C10). The bundled-only foundation (z9u4) ships first with ZERO network tile fetching. THIS issue adds the opt-in capability the locked spec (docs/design/2026-06-07-map-pin-grid-design.md §3) describes: a Settings 'Map tile server URL (permitted source only)' field, a Rust backend tile-gatekeeper (the webview NEVER fetches tiles directly; the backend fetches only from the operator-configured permitted host and serves to the picker so CSP stays 'self'), an on-disk tile cache, and fallback-to-bundled on failure. This is what enables finer-than-4-char precision (the bundled static world image physically resolves only to ~4-char). Public OSM is NOT a permitted option. Open design surface (which hosts are 'permitted', Geographica integration, auth) — needs its own brainstorm/plan before build. DEPENDS-ON tuxlink-z9u4 (the BaseMap + offline source chain).

title: Packet relay-chain support for Favorites (additive)
description: Forward gap deferred from the Contacts+Favorites delivery (tuxlink-egmp). The Favorite schema (src-tauri/src/favorites/store.rs) has NO relay/digipeater-path field, so a packet favorite pre-fills only the target callsign — not a 0-2 hop relay chain (the PacketRadioPanel Connect supports relays, but favorites cannot store them). Deliberately out of v1 per the adrev-hardened plan. Additive later: add an Option<Vec<String>> relay field to Favorite (serde default-tolerant, no schema bump needed), have the packet onPrefill set the relays state, and carry it into the FavoriteDial. Not data-loss; purely a convenience gap.

title: Scrub dead Pat references (comments, deprecated field) — keep the 2 live Pat-named paths
description:
Pat sidecar is fully stripped (PR #175, merged 2026-05-31; NativeBackend sole backend). Residual 'Pat' strings cause grep/onboarding confusion (a 2026-06-08 session was misled). Scrub the DEAD ones in a dedicated PR.

SCRUB (dead, safe):
- Stale/misleading comment src/session/logProjection.ts:151-160 (describes a 'pat' LogSource value no longer in the union at L30 — 'backend'|'transport'|'wire').
- Deprecated config field src-tauri/src/config.rs:28 pat_mbo_address (#[deprecated], never read). VERIFY first that the Config serde ignores unknown fields (no deny_unknown_fields) so old config.json still deserializes after removal; remove the #[allow(deprecated)] pat_mbo_address: None literals in tests too.
- History/parity breadcrumb comments mentioning Pat: winlink_backend.rs:31,861; winlink/mod.rs:1,4; bootstrap.rs:69,137; app_backend.rs:3,11,37,40; native_mailbox.rs:1-11; wizard.rs:23-26,384,444; Cargo.toml:23,34-37; Compose.tsx:10-14,410-411; useStatus.ts:499; mailbox/types.ts:14,24; wizard/types.ts:12; Step3TestSend.tsx:5. Reword to drop Pat naming (keep any genuinely-useful 'native replacement' context).
- Test-fixture strings ('Pat HTTP server ready', 'Pat process started', etc.) where they don't assert real behavior.

KEEP — DO NOT scrub (live, functional, NOT the sidecar; deleting breaks things):
- X-Pat-Transport incoming MIME header (ui_commands.rs:1039) — wire-compat for messages from peers running Pat (common across Winlink). tuxlink never emits it.
- 'tuxlink-pat' keyring service key (credentials.rs:29 + winlink_backend.rs:2377,2703) — one-time credential-MIGRATION fallback. Confirm with operator that creds are already migrated before any future removal.

NOT Pat (leave): JPATS Winlink forms, getpat.io Forms CDN, GitHub PAT token, dev/scratch/tuxlink-pat (gitignored reference copy).

SEQUENCING: run AFTER PR #465 (catalog-builder) and tuxlink-raez (contacts-favorites) merge — the breadcrumb comments live in winlink_backend.rs/bootstrap.rs/app_backend.rs/wizard.rs which those branches may touch; a concurrent scrub PR would conflict. Verify build + clippy --all-targets -D warnings after.
notes: UPDATE 2026-06-08: the 'tuxlink-pat' keyring service is being REMOVED in tuxlink-kc3q (operator decision), not kept — it was found to be a LIVE (mis-pointed) password source for ARDOP/VARA-HF CMS, not a dead failsafe. So the KEEP list for this scrub is now ONLY: X-Pat-Transport incoming MIME header (peer wire-compat, ui_commands.rs). Everything else (deprecated pat_mbo_address field, comments, test fixtures) remains scrub-able. Sequence after PR #465 + tuxlink-raez + tuxlink-kc3q land.

title: [bug] logging export manifest can report stale outer archive size
description: Holistic bug hunt 2026-06-06 found build_archive writes the pass-1 compressed size into manifest.compression.outer_archive_bytes, then rebuilds the final archive from modified manifest bytes. The embedded manifest can disagree with the final archive size and ExportResult.archive_size_bytes. Evidence: docs/bug-hunts/2026-06-06-logging-reactor-panic-holistic.md.
acceptance_criteria: Export manifest compression.outer_archive_bytes equals the actual final archive byte length and returned ExportResult.archive_size_bytes; regression coverage validates the manifest extracted from a generated archive.

title: Verify CheckInForm wire-format keys match WLE template (if re-registered with simplified schema)
description:
If the operator picks option (b) for tuxlink-4ai0 (keep simplified UI but map to WLE payload at submit), then the simplified field set in src-tauri/src/forms/templates/checkin.rs needs a submit-time mapping layer in src/compose/CheckInForm.tsx::buildPayload() that translates the 7 UI fields into the corresponding WLE field names. Wire-format alignment test must verify the EMITTED payload keys match the WLE template (msgto, organization, etc.) NOT the simplified UI keys.

If the operator picks (a) full WLE schema or (c) ship-as-generic-message, this issue is moot.

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, tuxlink-4ai0, PR #392 follow-up.

title: PositionFormV2 GPS IPC failure: add retry button + error banner
description:
If position_current_fix IPC fails (gpsd not running, no fix), PositionFormV2 currently logs to console and renders with empty grid + no operator-visible error. Add an inline retry button + error banner pattern matching Ics309FormV2's preview-fetch error state.

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: Replace window.prompt with proper modal for FormDraftLibrary slot label entry
description:
All 3 wired forms in PR #392 (PositionFormV2, Ics213Form, BulletinForm) — plus CheckInForm if/when re-registered (see tuxlink-4ai0) — use window.prompt for slot label entry. Limitations: blocking, inaccessible, no validation feedback, accepts whitespace-only labels (no trim).

Replace with a proper modal that: explicit label/input/button layout; trims whitespace and rejects empty; shows duplicate-label warning if proposed label already exists for this form_id; keyboard-accessible (Tab/Enter/Esc).

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: FormDraftLibrary: 'Update slot' in-place affordance
description:
In PR #392, 'Save as slot…' on all 3 wired forms (PositionFormV2, Ics213Form, BulletinForm) always creates a NEW slot — even when a slot is already selected. This is intentional v0.0.1 UX (the always-create comment in each form's saveSlot documents the decision). Operators who want to update an existing slot must delete + recreate.

Future UX: either (a) a second 'Update slot' button visible when a slot is selected, or (b) a modal on Save that disambiguates create-vs-update intent. Either approach needs the saveSlot to switch from setSlots(prev => [...prev, newSlot]) (append) to a replace-in-place pattern (prev.map(s => s.slot_id === updated.slot_id ? updated : s)).

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: Bundle Leaflet offline tile pack for PositionFormV2 (offline-first UX)
description:
PR #392 PositionMapWidget uses Leaflet with OSM online tiles (blocked by current CSP — see tuxlink-bt2q) + blank-canvas + grid-square overlay fallback. A bundled tile pack (e.g. low-res world map at zoom 0-5) would give a richer offline UX independent of the CSP allowlist decision. Operator decision 2026-06-04 deferred this to P3.

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: React.lazy form-registry entries to split Leaflet + printpdf from main bundle
description:
HTML Forms P2 (PR #392) added Leaflet (~143 kB gzip) and printpdf-related JS to the main index chunk. Both load on app launch even when the operator never opens Position Report or ICS-309. React.lazy on the form-registry entries would defer these until the operator picks the relevant form.

The vitest testTimeout was raised from 5s to 15s (Task 2 commit 6487ec2) to accommodate the cumulative form-registry growth — a band-aid for this issue. Once React.lazy lands, the testTimeout can drop back to 5s (or 10s for safety). Also resolves the 5 AppShell vitest-flake class that surfaced in PR #392's full sweep (tests pass in isolation, time out under cumulative module-load pressure).

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: Smart auth diagnostics: thread credential_scope into Re-enter-password affordance (Codex post-impl MAJOR #6)
description: The Mode 3 Re-enter password inline form currently renders unconditionally and writes to the configured primary callsign from config_read. Per spec §4.3 (i), an aux/unknown-scope failure should route to 'Try a different callsign' (the wizard) instead, to avoid overwriting the wrong password. Requires threading CredentialScope into AuthClassified events (Rust → Tauri → React) and consuming it in the banner. Filed as Codex post-impl MAJOR #6 follow-up.

title: Task 11 follow-ups: viewer substitution-in-script + dedupe token mint + OpenViewerResult shared type
description:
Minor + deferred Important items from Task 11 code-quality review (commit 223c93c). None block ship; address if natural during Task 13 polish or as standalone follow-ups.

## I1 (Important) — substitute_var_placeholders runs inside script/style blocks

src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs:357-389. Function walks entire HTML doc and replaces every {var X} with html_escape(value). If a WLE viewer template uses {var X} inside a <script> block (intended as a client-side reference), substitution corrupts JS source. NOT a security issue (html_escape neutralizes <), IS a correctness issue if any WLE catalog viewer relies on unsubstituted {var X} for client-side use. Risk unknown — depends on WLE catalog conventions.

Mitigation: (a) note as known limitation in docstring; (b) skip substitution inside <script>/<style> blocks (more correct, more code). Suggest tracking in docs/pitfalls/implementation-pitfalls.md.

## M1 — dead branch in open_webview_viewer's viewer-filename resolution
src-tauri/src/ui_commands.rs:1360-1362. find_form's BUNDLED_FORMS branch is dead code in practice (all 5 native forms have React Views, so MessageView never mounts WebviewFormViewer for them). Intent is defensive. Comment should acknowledge.

## M2 — OpenViewerResult interface duplicates OpenFormResult shape
WebviewFormViewer.tsx:41-45 vs WebviewFormHost.tsx:76-80. Same 3 fields. Extract to shared module (src/forms/types.ts or new file).

## M3 — fieldValues HashMap collapses multi-value fields
MessageView.tsx:202-205. FormPayload.fields is Array<[k, v]> tuples; same key can appear multiple times. HashMap keeps last. Acceptable for Viewer read-only display; add a comment documenting the intentional collapse.

## M4 — OpenedViewerSession doesn't derive Debug
Symmetric with OpenedSession (also no Debug). Add #[derive(Debug)] to both for easier debugging.

## M5 — mint_session_token retry loop duplicated in open + open_viewer
http_server.rs:780-784 and :812-815. Extract mint_unique_token(&mut guard) helper.

Also dedupe with mint_consent_token from modem_status.rs per tuxlink-7cn1 (separately filed).

## Parent

title: Extract shared 16-hex token-mint utility (dedupe modem_status + forms::http_server)
description:
Both src-tauri/src/modem_status.rs::mint_consent_token (~line 182) and src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs::mint_session_token (~line 523) implement byte-for-byte identical 16-hex random token generators (16-iter loop over rand::random::<u8>() & 0xF -> char::from_digit). The forms version's doc comment explicitly cross-references the modem version.

Extract to a shared util module so a future hardening (CSPRNG, length change) only edits one place. Suggested location: src-tauri/src/util/token.rs or src-tauri/src/security/token.rs. Also introduce a named const for the length (currently '16' is repeated across the mint function, tests, and the FormSessionRegistry doc comment) — e.g., SESSION_TOKEN_HEX_LEN = 16.

Caught by code-quality reviewer on PR for tuxlink-tzr5 (P1 forms Task 8). Non-blocking — flagged as I1 (Important) + M3 (Minor) but both deferred to follow-up.

Effort: small. Touches ~3 files. No behavioral change.

title: useMermaidRender hook doesn't re-run on topic switch
description: Latent bug found during Task 14 code review of tuxlink-ymiv PR #1 (PR #347). The src/help/useMermaidRender.ts useEffect is keyed on [containerRef] which is a stable ref object, so it runs ONCE on ReadingPane mount. Since HelpView renders ReadingPane with no 'key' prop, switching to a topic that contains Mermaid blocks AFTER first mount won't re-trigger the hook. Currently latent because no shipping topic uses Mermaid. Fix: change dep array to [containerRef, html] in src/help/useMermaidRender.ts. Must land BEFORE any docs PR ships a Mermaid-containing topic. Discovered by Task 14 reviewer during PR #1 implementation.

title: Docs Phase 3: Hamexandria-informed conceptual content with 6 attribution disciplines
description: Phase 3 of the tuxlink-s8qu epic (parent). Hamexandria-informed how-it-works content explaining ARDOP / VARA / Packet / Telnet from a conceptual frame — protocol overviews, when-to-use-which-mode, common pitfalls. Operator's six attribution disciplines (VERBATIM): (1) NEVER copy YouTube transcripts verbatim into docs/user-guide/*.md; (2) USE Hamexandria as a research aid to inform tuxlink's own (originally-written) explanations; (3) where a creator's framing is genuinely the right framing to cite, attribute prominently (creator name, video title, link to source video) and quote only what fair use plainly permits; (4) prefer to link OUT to the creator's video rather than embed their words; (5) get explicit permission from creators for any substantial extract; (6) maintain a CREDITS / acknowledgments section listing creators whose explanations shaped the docs. Operator framing: 'creators we are referencing are my people and they work hard for little compensation. We do not want to steal their work.' Hamexandria access: 'uv run ham-search' in dev/scratch/ham-knowledge-store/ (gitignored). Cross-checks against the spec doc docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-html-forms-full-parity-design.md (likely outdated for s8qu specifically; consult original tuxlink-s8qu description as source-of-truth).
notes: Absorbed into tuxlink-ymiv spec. Phase 3 (Hamexandria conceptual content) is now distributed across content PRs #2-#6 per the per-section research note workflow in spec §5.2. The six attribution disciplines are codified in spec §5.1.
close_reason: Closed

title: Docs Phase 2: new topic files for shipped-but-undocumented features

Phase 2 scope: add NEW topic files under docs/user-guide/ for features that exist in the code but lack a dedicated topic. Candidates: (a) the help window itself (meta-topic explaining the in-app Documentation viewer + sidebar + reading-width + font-size + search affordances); (b) Saildocs GRIB request workflow (src/grib/GribRequestPanel.tsx) — the operator-facing 'how do I request a GRIB' walkthrough including the message-format the back-end expects; (c) Theme Designer detail — go deeper than 08-color-schemes' current treatment, covering every token group + the per-token color picker + Save / Cancel semantics; (d) any other shipped feature surfaced during Phase 2 scoping. EACH new topic file needs (i) a number prefix > 10 (or insertion into the existing range with renames), (ii) registration in src/help/topics.ts SECTIONS array, (iii) accuracy probe against the current code, (iv) cross-links to neighbors using bare .md refs (no anchors, no out-of-bundle paths — per Phase 1's hygiene work). Likely also need screenshots — operator's framing in tuxlink-s8qu: 'images either cropped or full screenshots where possible. They're often worth 1,000 words.'
notes: Absorbed into tuxlink-ymiv spec. Phase 2 (new topic files) is now PR #2 onward in the spec's phasing, with per-PR bd issues filed at each PR's start. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-docs-knowledge-base-design.md.

title: @media print stylesheet — hide dashboard/sidebar/statusbar on print
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-j0m3 (Print menu wiring). With no @media print rules, window.print() captures the full app chrome (dashboard ribbon, sidebar, status bar, radio panel if mounted) alongside the MessageView. That's serviceable for "screenshot the message to PDF" but not what an email print should look like.

Wanted behavior:
- @media print hides .dashboard-ribbon, .sidebar, .statusbar, the radio-panel column.
- MessageView prints full-width with the header (from/to/subject/date) + body + form attachments.
- Page-break-inside: avoid on the message header block so it doesn't split across pages.
- Tauri webview honors @media print rules natively — no Tauri-side work needed.

Out of scope:
- Multi-page form rendering (HTML Forms work; deferred until form rendering itself ships).
- Print-only views (a separate route) — start with stylesheet rules.

Files touched (expected): src/shell/AppShell.css OR a new src/print.css imported from main.tsx.
notes:
Smoke walk item 28: current Print behavior produces a vertically formatted screenshot of the visible Tuxlink app, not an intelligent printout of the message contents. Acceptance should verify actual message-focused print output, not merely that Ctrl+P opens a print dialog.

Implemented print stylesheet in bd-tuxlink-zdfj/print-stylesheet: hides shell chrome/list/radio panel for print, lets MessageView print full-width, and avoids splitting the message header. Quality gates passed locally. Closure remains blocked by open dependency tuxlink-j0m3.

title: Docs expansion + polish pass + Hamexandria-sourced content (ethical attribution)
description: The current docs/user-guide/ tree (10 topics from PR #214 by gulch-osprey-bog) is a solid first slice but is incomplete: (a) features shipped since that PR (themes / theme designer, status bar, VARA TCP, ARDOP UI, HTML Forms, search, user folders, MessageView attachments, Saildocs GRIB framework, WLE catalog, message sort) are not documented; (b) the existing topics need a polish pass for voice + accuracy + cross-links; (c) per operator: include explanatory content drawn from Hamexandria (the local-vector-DB built from amateur-radio YouTube transcripts, memory: reference_hamexandria) about how Tuxlink and Winlink work conceptually — protocol overviews, when-to-use-which-mode framing, common pitfalls. **CRITICAL: Hamexandria content is third-party creator material with real copyright; treat it ethically.** Operator's words: 'creators we're referencing are my people and they work hard for little compensation. We don't want to steal their work.' Implications: (1) NEVER copy transcripts verbatim into docs/user-guide/*.md; (2) USE Hamexandria as a research aid to inform tuxlink's own (originally-written) explanations; (3) where a creator's framing is genuinely the right framing to cite, attribute prominently (creator name, video title, link to the source video) and quote only what fair use plainly permits; (4) prefer to link OUT to the creator's video rather than embed their words; (5) get explicit permission from creators for any substantial extract; (6) maintain a CREDITS / acknowledgments section listing creators whose explanations shaped the docs. Pairs with tuxlink-0gsy (help-window redesign) — the redesigned window surfaces this expanded content. Suggest splitting into phases: (P1) polish + accuracy pass on existing 10 topics; (P2) topics for shipped-but-undocumented features; (P3) Hamexandria-informed conceptual / how-it-works topics with attribution discipline.
notes: Phase 1 shipped via PR #336 (willow-yew-esker). Phase 2 + Phase 3 carved out as tuxlink-m38d + tuxlink-v8lw with dep edges.

title: Investigate gstack upstream divergence from April 14 snapshot
description:
On 2026-05-05 03:29:30 the gstack install at ~/.claude/skills/gstack/ was updated; the update emptied the bundled workflow sub-skills (office-hours, investigate, ship, plan-eng-review, design-consultation, design-review, document-release, retro, checkpoint, health, qa, review, plan-ceo-review, plan-design-review, design-html, design-shotgun, devex-review, plan-devex-review, qa-only) while leaving the empty subdirs in place. The tuxlink CLAUDE.md skill routing went silently broken for ~28 days until the 2026-06-02 thistle-swallow-cedar session discovered it.

Recovered by restoring the full April 14 self-export from /home/administrator/claude-code-export/skills/gstack/ to ~/.claude/skills/gstack/, then symlinking each CLAUDE.md-routed sub-skill to the top level of ~/.claude/skills/ (the Claude Code harness does not recurse into sub-skill directories).

This issue tracks the followup decision: WHY did gstack restructure? Is there a current upstream (the SKILL.md says 'Regenerate: bun run gen:skill-docs' — there's a bun-based source) that we should pull from? Were the workflow sub-skills intentionally removed (perhaps refactored into a separate gstack-workflows package) or accidentally dropped during a regen? Until this is decided, we are on a frozen April 14 gstack codebase — fine for now but blocks any future gstack updates.

Not blocking. P3.

title: [bug] ARDOP Capture/Playback dropdowns show all ALSA plugin chains; restrict to hardware-only entries
description: Follow-up to tuxlink-y7x7 (PR #242). The Capture and Playback dropdowns surface every entry arecord -L / aplay -L return — including plugin chains (lavrate, samplerate, speexrate, speex, upmix, vdownmix, jack, oss, null, sysdefault, usbstream HDMI passthroughs) that aren't useful for ARDOP. The backend already classifies plughw:CARD= / hw:CARD= entries as isHardware; the frontend uses that for SORTING but should use it for FILTERING. Restrict the dropdown to isHardware entries only; leave the manual-fallback input for the rare 'I really want pulse' case. PTT dropdown already filters to kind=='usb' so no change needed there. Update 'No devices found' hint to say 'No USB audio interfaces found — plug one in and Refresh, or use Manual.'

title: VARA: extend DashboardRibbon + useStatusData to recognize VARA session state
description:
Operator (2026-06-02): top dashboard ribbon shows 'Telnet' + 'disconnected' even when VARA session is Open. Cause: useStatusData (src/shell/useStatus.ts) + DashboardRibbon (src/shell/DashboardRibbon.tsx) read from a unified pipeline that knows ARDOP, Packet, Telnet — not VARA.

Fix (separate from MYCALL/session_log work):
1. Add VARA polling to useStatusData (or have it subscribe to a vara:status event the backend emits)
2. Extend the connection-tone calculation to map VaraState.{open,connecting,error} → ribbon state
3. Update the mode label so when VARA is active, ribbon reads 'VARA HF' / 'VARA FM' instead of the prior mode
4. Tests on the new tone calculation + label mapping

This is the larger surface — touches DashboardRibbon + useStatus + status pipeline. Defer to land after the immediate MYCALL/session-log polish.

title: [bug] Outbox sidebar entry missing queue-depth badge; status bar shows '1 to send' but sidebar shows no count
description: When a draft is queued in Outbox, the status bar's outbox segment correctly shows '1 to send' (src/shell/AppShell.tsx:589 reads outbox.messages.length directly). But the FolderSidebar Outbox entry does NOT show a count badge. Root cause: the counts object passed to FolderSidebar (src/shell/AppShell.tsx:224-227) only populates inbox + sent — outbox is omitted. FolderSidebar already renders a badge when counts[item.id] is set (FolderSidebar.tsx:94-98), so the fix is to add outbox: outbox.messages.length to the counts literal. The outbox useMailbox handle is already in scope (line 191) for the status bar. Surfaced 2026-06-02 by operator after PR #219 (tuxlink-su2h) enabled the Outbox folder entry but didn't wire its count.

title: Follow-ups from tuxlink-0pnb code reviews (PR #208)
description:
Non-blocking follow-up work surfaced by code reviews during tuxlink-0pnb (PR #208 TCP P2P Telnet client-dial):

1. **DRY: triplicated real_factory closure** in src-tauri/src/winlink/credentials.rs. The three p2p_peer_password_{read,write,delete} public wrappers each define an identical inline factory closure. Same pattern as the pre-existing CMS-side read_password. Refactor option: introduce a private fn p2p_real_factory() -> impl Fn(...) helper used by both the new P2P helpers AND the CMS-side wrapper (avoid asymmetric refactor).

2. **Reverse-direction namespace-isolation test** in src-tauri/tests/winlink_credentials_test.rs. Current test verifies P2P-write then CMS-read returns NoEntry. Add the reverse: CMS-write then P2P-read returns NoEntry. Closes the symmetric coverage gap; a buggy p2p_peer_account that dropped the prefix would currently pass.

3. **connect_stream fragile invariant** in src-tauri/src/winlink/telnet_p2p.rs:112-114. The unreachable() arm (.expect on ToSocketAddrs-non-empty-but-no-loop-progress) is logically unreachable but reads as a hidden panic. Mirror telnet.rs::connect_with_deadline's explicit-empty + error-propagation chain.

4. **CONNECT_TOTAL_DEADLINE for P2P transport** in src-tauri/src/winlink/telnet_p2p.rs. P2P inherits per-address CONNECT_TIMEOUT but lacks telnet.rs's CONNECT_TOTAL_DEADLINE multi-address bounded sweep (tuxlink-lbg #4). For a peer whose hostname resolves to many A records, this stacks. Single-A-record peers (the common LAN case) are unaffected.

5. **Abort button** in src/radio/modes/TelnetP2pRadioPanel.tsx. cms+telnet has cms_abort wired; P2P could mirror. Requires a backend telnet_p2p_abort Tauri command. Useful before any long-running connect attempts (which include the 60s socket TIMEOUT path).

6. **Skipped credential round-trip tests in ui_commands.rs**. Plan Task 4 Step 1 called for p2p_peer_password_set_then_status_reports_set + clear-then-not-set tests at the Tauri-command layer. Implementer substituted serialization round-trip tests (DTO shape) to avoid the OS-keyring race that the existing CMS commands also avoid. If the project adds _with_factory test variants at the command layer, these should be added.

7. **ExchangeError inner-variant Display gap**: 5 of ExchangeError's variants (BadProposal, BadAnswer, Transfer, Decompress, Parse) use {e:?} (Debug) in their Display impl because the inner error types don't implement Display. Now that ExchangeError has Display + std::error::Error (added in this PR for the new Tauri command's error path), this gap is observable at the UiError layer. Adding Display to those inner types is a separate cross-cutting refactor.

All items are quality / defense-in-depth, not correctness bugs. None block the v0.1 PR 1 ship.
notes: Item 8: session log not wired to operator-visible pane (final branch review flagged). Progress/wire-log callbacks eprintln! to stderr only; SessionLogSection component is rendered but receives no events during dial. Plan-faithful behavior (plan pseudocode also used eprintln!) but the operator gets no real-time feedback. Wire these through to emit_session_line when outbox-integration follow-on lands.

title: [convergence-discipline] Test fixtures for the 7 known convergence failure modes
description:
Codex P2 #18. Shell/Python tests against fake-repo fixtures covering: (1) post-merge commits to dead branch, (2) stale operator branch 800+ commits behind, (3) missing pnpm symlink with up-to-date lockfile, (4) staged .beads/issues.jsonl blocking rebase, (5) untracked-vs-tracked identical-content collision, (6) stale cargo target/ from old binary, (7) port 1420 ownership / strictPort collision. Each test scripts the failure setup then asserts converge-build either prevents it or handles it gracefully.

Parent: tuxlink-edvb (the umbrella convergence-discipline issue)
