title: VARA codec: model BUSY ON/OFF, OK ack, REGISTERED as typed InboundCommand variants
description:
VARA TCP smoke probe (2026-06-01, larch-clover-delta session) surfaced four VARA event classes the Phase 1 codec hadn't modeled, currently falling through to Unknown(verbatim):

- BUSY ON / BUSY OFF — VARA channel-busy detector
- OK — command-ack response (echoes after MYCALL/BW2300/LISTEN OFF)
- REGISTERED — VARA registration-state report

The Unknown(verbatim) fallback preserves the line so this is non-blocking for Phase 2 (ModemTransport impl). Promoting these to typed variants is a Phase 1.5 task: lets consumers pattern-match cleanly and unlocks busy-channel adaptive scheduling logic later (#8 link adaptation territory).

Full probe transcript: /tmp/vara_tcp_probe_*.log on pandora (also captured in this session's bash transcript).

Acceptance:
- BusyDetected(bool), CommandAck, Registered variants added to InboundCommand
- Parser tests cover each (round-trip Display where applicable)
- Probe re-run shows them as typed events, not Unknown
notes:
Re-opened 2026-06-01 (larch-clover-delta). Filed earlier this session when the VARA TCP smoke probe surfaced 4 unmodeled event classes; implemented as commit 77d18da on bd-tuxlink-hblz/vara-tcp; folded into PR #192 ready-mark. Operator dropped this commit during rebase to PR #199 (the version that landed on main) — consistent with the e2e smoke methodology call: the codec extension was component-smoked only.

The work itself remains useful (it does correctly extend the parser to typed events from real VARA output), but it needs an e2e smoke path before re-landing — i.e. ships under Phase 2's umbrella (tuxlink-hblz) when there's a real VARA session driving the typed-event consumers.

Code preserved on the bd-tuxlink-hblz/vara-tcp branch (not deleted); reachable via commit hash 77d18da on origin.

title: Compose.tsx setFormMode-during-render in defensive guard at lines ~610-617 is a React 18 anti-pattern
Sweep anachronistic 'v0.0.1' / 'v0.1' / 'v0.5' UI strings — current version is 0.10.0 as of 2026-06-01
description:
Multiple UI strings throughout src/ and docs/ reference deferred behavior to 'v0.1' or 'v0.5' as if those are future versions; per recent release commits the project is at 0.9.0 → 0.10.0 in current cycle.

Examples surfaced in the P0 trim session:
- Compose.tsx 'attachments not wired (v0.0.1)' — actually attachments ARE wired now per PR #177 wire-format
- Compose.tsx 'Template selection arrives in v0.1' — template work IS v0.1 (this PR)
- FolderSidebar.tsx 'Outbox + Archive are v0.1' — same now-stale
- Many other call-out tooltips referencing 'v0.1 / v0.5 / future release'

Scope:
1. Grep src/ docs/ for 'v0.0.1', 'v0.1' as exact-version-pinned strings (not file-paths or branch-names)
2. For each: decide whether the strung-out 'deferred to vX' is still accurate or should be updated
3. Update the inaccurate ones to current version pins or remove the qualifier entirely
4. Atomic per-string-class commit (don't bulk-rewrite without per-string judgment)
5. Verify no tests asserting on the string contents break

Out of scope: docs/ files where the version pin is forensically meaningful (e.g., 'first shipped in v0.0.1' historical references).

Surfaced by operator 2026-06-01.
notes: Shipped as PR #193 on branch bd-tuxlink-htx1/version-strings-sweep. Three atomic commits (compose / mailbox / shell+wizard+misc) covering all non-Cc-specific version pins in src/. The Cc-specific v0.x references in Compose.tsx (L13,16,28,244) and draft.test.ts (L279) were left for item C / tuxlink-h1km. All 672 vitest tests pass.

title: Draft-restore corner case: form-mode draft for a no-longer-composable form-id silently falls back to plain mode without operator notice
description:
If an operator had a saved draft with formId set to one of the P0-removed forms (Position_Report, Form-309_Initial, Damage_Assessment_Initial) and reopens that draft after the P0 trim, Compose.tsx restores formMode to {kind:'form', formId, values}, but the defensive guard at lines ~610-617 fires (entry.Form is undefined for these post-P0) and resets formMode to {kind:'plain'}. The operator sees an empty plain compose with the To/Subject restored but no surfacing of their typed form field values. The field values are still in localStorage but no UI surfaces them.

Proposed fix: when the guard fires due to formId-no-longer-composable, surface an inline notice like 'The <form_name> compose form is temporarily unavailable in this version; your draft fields are preserved in localStorage but require the rebuilt native form (P2) to access.' Alternative: serialize the field values into the body text as a preserved-fields block.

Out of scope for P0 trim; flagged by final code reviewer 2026-05-31.

File touched: src/compose/Compose.tsx (draft-restore logic at lines ~141-162; defensive guard at lines ~610-617).

title: Pitfalls doc additions from html-forms session: Vite glob vs Node fs, gitignored-dir discovery, schema-version bump migration pattern
description: Three lessons surfaced during the HTML Forms v0.1 session (PR #177) that warrant docs/pitfalls/implementation-pitfalls.md entries: (1) Use Vite import.meta.glob with eager+raw query for filesystem-scan tests in Tauri frontend (Node fs requires @types/node which isn't a frontend dep; tsc fails). (2) Gitignored-dir discovery: when looking for a file expected to be present, check .gitignore for the path AND check .claude/worktree-archives/ for compressed reference materials — large third-party material (e.g. RMS Express install) lives there compressed rather than on-disk. (3) Schema-version bump migration pattern (from tuxlink-g4dj): when bumping SCHEMA_VERSION, Index::open returns SchemaDrift; operator runs the rebuild command which deletes the index file and walks the source. Mbox stays authoritative; no data loss. Document the pattern for future schema changes.

title: ARDOP: iframe-embed ardopcf WebGUI inside the dock (eliminate external-browser context switch)
description:
Follow-up from PR #164 (tuxlink-60wh). The WebGUI link works but bounces the operator into an external browser, breaking the inline-UI convention (memory: inline_ui_no_window_clutter). ardopcf's WebGUI is plain HTML+JS at http://localhost:<webgui_port>/; embedding via an <iframe> in the ArdopDock would keep Spectrum/Waterfall/level-meters/test-tone all inline.

Open design questions:
- iframe vs Tauri webview-on-webview — does Tauri's WebKitGTK allow iframes to localhost? (Same-origin posture, CSP).
- Layout — dock is currently narrow; the WebGUI likely wants ~600px. Consider a collapsible/expandable section or a separate sub-panel.
- Race — only attach the iframe AFTER ardopcf is Running (cmd_port reachable), otherwise the iframe renders an error page.
- Teardown — clear iframe src when modem returns to Stopped to avoid stale render.

Acceptance: WebGUI renders inline in the dock; external-browser button can either be removed or kept as fallback. No regressions in dock layout or state machine.
close_reason: Closed

title: find-messages: ghost filter chips in ChipStrip are not interactive (click-to-add not wired)

The 'No active filter — click + to add' placeholder copy implies they are clickable, but v0.1 filters are only addable via free-text FTS5 syntax (FROM:, TO:, etc.). Adding click handlers requires per-chip default-value decisions (e.g. addr input dialog, date-range picker) that are out of scope for v0.1. v0.2 work: add onClick per filter key with appropriate input affordances. Codex marked this P2. Filed by agent finch-gulch-lichen during adrev (tuxlink-1hu Task 21).

title: ARDOP: Transmit Level Test (TWOTONETEST) pre-flight from Settings or dock
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) noted that ardopcf has a TWOTONETEST host command for transmit-level verification. AE8Q's operator walkthrough describes this as the standard pre-flight: send a tone burst, verify radio keys + audio level is right, BEFORE attempting a real connect.

Current state: no equivalent in tuxlink. Operator's first smoke is 'click Connect → hope the audio's right.' If it isn't, debugging is much harder.

Fix scope (small):
1. Add modem_ardop_two_tone_test Tauri command in modem_commands.rs wrapping cmd.send_line('TWOTONETEST').
2. Button in Settings → ARDOP HF, or in dock running-state. Disabled when not connected to TNC.
3. Test: stub TNC accepts TWOTONETEST and echoes back.

Lower priority if tuxlink-<webgui> ships first — ardopcf's WebGUI has a built-in test-tone button accessible at localhost:8514.
close_reason: Closed

title: ARDOP: surface ardopcf's built-in WebGUI (-G 8514) for spectrum + waterfall + level meters
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) found that ardopcf has a complete built-in WebGUI on http://localhost:8514 (toggled by -G 8514 flag). It exposes: Spectrum view, Waterfall view, audio level meters, TX/RX state, and developer command-entry mode.

This is a massive zero-engineering-cost win for the Spectrum/Waterfall UX gap. Three implementation options:

(a) Zero-cost: add 'Open ardopcf Web UI' link in dock that opens http://localhost:<cmd_port-1> via tauri-plugin-shell::open. ~10 LOC. Operator gets full ardopcf UI in browser.

(b) Iframe embed: <iframe src='http://localhost:8514'> in dock secondary tab or modal. ~30 LOC. Inline UX, no browser tab switch.

(c) Tauri proxy: serve the WebGUI in-app via Tauri's HTTP plugin. ~100 LOC. Most polished but biggest scope.

PREREQ regardless of option: add -G <cmd_port-1> to ardopcf's extra_args at spawn (currently we don't, so the WebGUI doesn't run).

Recommend starting with option (a) — cheapest path, gets the capability to operators immediately. Polish to (b) or (c) later if the external-browser flow feels jarring.
close_reason: Closed

title: ARDOP HF UI: numeric live meters (S/N, VU, throughput, bytes, uptime)
description:
PR #156 wired the structural live-meter path (state/peer/bandwidth/ARQ flags animate from real ardopcf events), but explicitly deferred the numeric fields:

- sn_db (signal-to-noise dB) — requires parsing ardopcf's STATUS strings (free-form).
- vu_dbfs (VU input level) — requires polling a specific monitor command or parsing STATUS.
- throughput_bps — requires a rolling-window accumulator over BUFFER events.
- bytes_rx / bytes_tx — cumulative counters from BUFFER deltas.
- uptime_sec — compute from the Connected event's timestamp.

Today these render their UI slots in the dock but the values never update — they stay at whatever the connect snapshot set (or None).

Work:
1. In apply_ardop_event_to_status (transport.rs), extend the Status string parser to extract S/N + VU when ardopcf emits them. Reference: ardopcf source / wl2k-go transport for the exact STATUS message formats.
2. Add a small struct (e.g. ThroughputAccumulator) to track BUFFER deltas + a 5-10s rolling window for throughput_bps.
3. bytes_rx / bytes_tx: monotonic counters updated on each BUFFER delta (need to track the prior BUFFER reading and accumulate the diff).
4. uptime_sec: stamp the Connected event timestamp; compute on each broadcaster tick.

All deferred to this issue per PR #156's body. Should land before VARA HF / Dire Wolf so those transports can use the same field mapping pattern.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-n2uz-ardop-numeric-meters. Branch: bd-tuxlink-n2uz/ardop-numeric-meters. Created 2026-05-31 01:21:24 UTC by kite-larch-kingfisher.

title: search: TOCTOU on path.exists() in Index::open — concurrent creation race
description:
Reviewer of Task 2 (commit a616226) flagged that `Index::open` pre-flights `path.exists()` to distinguish "fresh file" from "stale-version pre-existing file," but there is a race window between `path.exists() == false` and `Connection::open(&path)` where another writer could create the file. In practice (single-writer, operator-controlled mailbox dir) this is unreachable, but the approach is fragile.

Safer alternative: open with `OpenOptions::new().create_new(true)` semantics — a precise atomic "create-only-if-absent" — and interpret `AlreadyExists` as "file pre-existed." rusqlite's Connection does not expose `create_new` directly; the fix would either use `std::fs` to atomically create then handle through rusqlite, or move to a `Connection::open_with_flags` shape.

Filed as v0.5+ hardening — not a v0.1 blocker. Relates to: tuxlink-1hu (find-messages v0.1).

title: modem_ardop_connect: pre-flight identity check (callsign-present)
description: The Tauri wrapper modem_ardop_connect checks capture/playback devices are non-empty before delegating to _inner, but doesn't check identity (callsign or identifier present). If both are missing in the persisted config, init_config_from_persisted_config produces mycall='' and ARDOP rejects MYCALL '' with a cryptic TNC fault. Add a parallel pre-flight: if identity.callsign and identity.identifier are both empty, return 'Complete the wizard before connecting' (matching the audio-device error style). Surfaced by the Task 3.3 code-quality review of PR #153.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #155 (commit b6da454) — modem_ardop_connect pre-flight identity check via check_identity_present helper. Auto-close didn't fire because issue was a child of tuxlink-qvl in the polish bundle.

description: The v1 ArdopDock has no operator-visible Disconnect button — the only way to stop the modem is to quit the app. The backend modem_ardop_disconnect Tauri command exists (Phase 3 Task 3.2). Add a Disconnect button to the dock's running state. Also: when the consent modal reopens after a connect error, the inline connectError persists in the dock background — should clear when the modal mounts.

title: ArdopDock: clear useConsent.token on modem stop event
description: When the modem disconnects, the backend's ModemSession::reset_to_stopped() clears the session's consent token, but ArdopDock's useConsent hook retains the stale token. The next Connect click takes the 'if (consent.token) doConnect' shortcut, calls modem_ardop_connect with the invalid token, and the operator sees a confusing 'consent token invalid' error instead of being re-prompted. Fix: subscribe to modem:status in ArdopDock (or via a useEffect on useModemStatus) and call useConsent.clear() when status.state becomes 'stopped'. Surfaced by the Task 6.2 code-quality review of PR #153. Gate still holds (backend rejects); UX nit only.
close_reason: Subsumed by 42732dd: ArdopDock.doConnect clears useConsent.token in finally after every connect, so the modem-stop hook is no longer load-bearing.

title: README chrome — add standard badges to project header
description:
Tuxlink's README header is currently logo + title + tagline + hero — no badges. Operator request: replicate Geographica's standard chrome (license / version / CI / language) but WITHOUT the AI-orchestration posturing (no 'built in N days', no 'agents N', no 'how it was built' callout). The project is positioned as a shipped working product with a real userbase, not an agentic development testbed.

Add the badge row immediately under the title/tagline:
  - License: GPL-3.0-or-later (from LICENSE file)
  - Version: latest GitHub release (links to CHANGELOG)
  - Build status: release.yml workflow
  - Rust MSRV (from Cargo.toml rust-version)

Possibly also:
  - About-sidebar polish (repo description / topics) — separate concern via gh; flag for operator if relevant

title: CLAUDE.md codex review syntax stale — flag/prompt no longer combinable
description:
CLAUDE.md (§Extended capabilities → OpenAI Codex CLI) documents:

  npx --yes @openai/codex review --commit <SHA> "<attack-angle prompt>"
  npx --yes @openai/codex review --uncommitted "<prompt>"
  npx --yes @openai/codex review --base main    "<prompt>"

Observed 2026-05-29 (yew-cypress-oak, tuxlink-p3u): Codex v0.128.0 (gpt-5.5) errors on these forms:

  error: the argument '--uncommitted' cannot be used with '[PROMPT]'
  error: the argument '--commit <SHA>' cannot be used with '[PROMPT]'

Working alternatives:
  - 'codex review --commit <SHA>' (no inline prompt — Codex picks a default review prompt; worked for tuxlink-p3u, no blocking findings)
  - 'codex exec "<prompt>"' (general-purpose; can be piped a diff via stdin)
  - Possibly: prepend prompt via a prompts file the CLI reads — needs investigation

Action: update CLAUDE.md's Codex section to reflect the current CLI shape. Worked-around for tuxlink-p3u by dropping the inline prompt, but the docs are misleading for future agents. Touches AGENTS.md parity check if AGENTS.md also documents the syntax.

Coupled with: (a) decide if the Pat sidecar is still shipped at all (else the build.rs Go path can go too), (b) verify nothing in the active code paths (winlink_backend / native_cms_probe) still depends on pat_*, (c) regression-test the wizard + compose + connect flow after strip. Likely a multi-PR effort: first verify-isolate, then strip the Rust files, then strip build.rs Go path + external/tuxlink-pat submodule + libax25 build dep IF Pat AX.25 is no longer used (the AX.25 transport on main is native — verify). Depends on the dev-docs decision (sibling bd issue) on Pat deprecation timing.

title: Implement the togglable right-side radio-dock pane (synthesis design)
description: The synthesis mock (docs/design/mockups/2026-05-17-mocks-v2-synthesis.html + synthesis-dock-on.png) shows a togglable 4th pane on the right of the mailbox showing TX/RX state, audio levels, GPS sats, battery, session timer, etc. — accessible via View → Show Radio Dock. Currently the layout exists only in the mock (HTML+PNG); no React component. Brainstorm scope before building: dock-off remains default (mock-b), dock-on is opt-in. Needs (1) a RadioDock React component, (2) backend data feeds for dashboard values, (3) View-menu toggle wiring, (4) ctrl+shift+M shortcut (synthesis spec mentions Ctrl+Shift+M; collides with ctrl+shift+R Reply All — pick a different shortcut). Filed as P3 because dock-off is fully shippable; the dock is enhancement, not core.

title: ARDOP bind-wait: replace TcpListener EADDRINUSE probe with passive listener-presence check
description: with_managed_modem (tuxlink-6aj, transport.rs) detects that ardopcf has bound its TCP ports by attempting TcpListener::bind(addr) and treating the AddressInUse error as "port in use -> ardopcf up". This has a port-steal race: between the probe binding a fresh socket (when ardopcf is NOT yet up) and that socket dropping, the probe transiently HOLDS the port; if ardopcf calls bind() in that ~microsecond window it gets AddressInUse and may fail to start. Probability is negligible for the MVP (the probe socket drops in <1us, ardopcf binds once at startup, poll interval is 100ms) and the Phase 5 code review judged the current probe safe-enough for MVP, but recommended a passive check as the settled design. REPLACE with a PASSIVE listener-presence check that touches neither ardopcf bind nor its accept queue: parse /proc/net/tcp + /proc/net/tcp6 for a LISTEN socket on the port, or shell to ss -ltnH (consistent with process.rs already shelling to lsof for the device-release check). This pattern will recur for any future managed-spawn modem (Dire Wolf, tuxmodem). Verify at on-site ardopcf bring-up whether the current probe ever races in practice.
notes: Codex review (582bbe1) adds a second facet beyond the port-steal race: the TcpListener::bind probe also treats ANY pre-existing process already listening on the port as "ardopcf is up" (false positive) — tuxlink would then connect to / control a stranger TNC it did not spawn. A passive /proc/net/tcp or ss check that also verifies the listener belongs to the spawned PID (or at least is fresh) avoids both the steal race and this false positive.

title: Session-selector polish: active-only transport dot, TelnetCmsPanel quick-pick test, StubPanel edge test
description: Non-blocking minors from the tuxlink-3pb review (PR #124): (1) the sidebar transport-state dot renders on every packet row in any expanded session type — should show only on the ACTIVE packet row; (2) TelnetCmsPanel's host quick-pick → config_set_connect path isn't directly unit-tested (blur + transport paths are); (3) StubPanel's unknown-id label fallback isn't unit-tested. Surface in PR #124 body.

title: Packet param changes via UI may need an app restart (backend uses startup config snapshot)
description: Discovered while fixing tuxlink-uhc. NativeBackend holds a 'config: Config' field set in NativeBackend::new() at startup (winlink_backend.rs:433/481); the connect path reads 'self.config.clone()' (winlink_backend.rs:595/738). The UI settings-save commands call config::write_config_atomic() (ui_commands.rs) which writes DISK but no refresh of the in-memory backend snapshot was found. If so, changing packet params (or other config) via the UI does not take effect until the app is restarted. VERIFY this is actually stale (vs. refreshed via app_backend reconstruction on save) before fixing. Did NOT affect the tuxlink-uhc retest (rebuild+relaunch loads fresh config at startup). Fix likely = reload config from disk at connect time, or rebuild/refresh the backend's config on save.
close_reason: Landed on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate (PR pending): ka7+p5u via live-config refresh on config_set_*; efo via lenient packet.link deserialize + TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR; 2y4 RADIO-1 bundle (bounded airtime + abort-before-write + no pre-connect DISC + T1-floor revert + KISS nibble), Codex-cleared (4 invariants), TOCTOU refinement tracked in tuxlink-0ja. 338 lib + 453 vitest green.

title: Packet BT: in-app RFCOMM-socket transport (no rfcomm bind / no root / no shell) — mediate the OS setup jank
description: DEFERRED by operator 2026-05-22: do NOT design/build until connected-mode AX.25 (Winlink B2F) is PROVEN working on-air over the UV-Pro on Linux — don't automate setup for a transport that isn't proven. Captured idea + feasibility finding so it's not re-investigated. Problem: the current BT path makes the operator do raw OS shell (bluetoothctl pair + sudo rfcomm bind /dev/rfcommN + channel-hunt + flaky retries) — operator flagged this as 'Pat territory'; a savvy BT/Linux user nearly gave up, so the EmComm target audience would give up entirely. Key feasibility finding (verified on pandora, this session): a NON-root user CAN create an RFCOMM socket (AF_BLUETOOTH/BTPROTO_RFCOMM created OK as uid 1000) — so a native client can open the RFCOMM connection DIRECTLY as a socket (connect to MAC+channel), needing NO root, NO 'rfcomm bind', NO /dev/rfcommN node, NO channel-hunting (read channel from the radio's SDP record). The RFCOMM socket is a Read+Write byte stream that drops into the existing ByteLink trait exactly like TcpStream. This SUPERSEDES the spec section 4.1 serial-/dev/rfcommN decision FOR BLUETOOTH. Scope when un-deferred: (1) new KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth { mac, channel } opening an RFCOMM socket via the bluer crate (or raw socket); (2) in-app pairing via BlueZ D-Bus (scan->pick->pair) WITH AUTO-RETRY on org.bluez.Error.ConnectionAttemptFailed (the transient failure the operator hit); (3) read SPP channel from SDP, fallback to 1; (4) graceful fallback to guided prompts if in-app pairing hits a polkit wall. STILL-UNPROVEN before building: RFCOMM socket CONNECT to the radio (only creation verified) — prove that + the on-air connected-mode dial first. Brainstorm context this session; no spec written (operator deferred). Relates to tuxlink-jvp (current shell help-text) + tuxlink-3wh (on-air proof gate).
notes: Socket transport built (PR #123) + connect+hold proven via Python (channel resolved from SDP, held stable). BUT RX is broken on-air (tuxlink-4ef) and the connect envelope is RADIO-1-unsafe (tuxlink-2y4). NOT air-ready.

So PR-creation works; a later step (publish/tag/asset?) is red. Operator values green CI. Diagnose the failing step. Found 2026-05-22 during consolidation.

title: Packet: live status carries real peer call + transient 'Answering <call>' (status sink)
description: tuxlink-orj landed the honest Listening/Connected/Disconnected feed, but two refinements live INSIDE native_packet_connect's blocking answer()/connect() call: (1) BackendStatus::Connected currently uses a placeholder peer:'packet' rather than the real dialed/answered callsign; (2) the transient 'Answering <call>' state (spec 4.6) is not emitted. Both need a status sink threaded into native_packet_connect (mirroring how the 'progress' callback is threaded) so it can push status mid-flight. Per RADIO-1 + the fn's design, native_packet_connect is exercised only on-air (operator) — so these are agent-buildable but ONLY operator-verifiable. Build the sink + real-peer/Answering; operator confirms on-air. Rides on tuxlink-7fr.

Fix: add a .gps.locked green variant; GridEdit adds 'locked' class when source==='Gps' && gpsReady; keep grey for GPS-selected-no-fix; MANUAL stays amber. ~2-line CSS + className change.

title: RMS node map layer: nearest Winlink RMS node by real GPS position (Tuxlink CMS fetch + Geographica render)
description:
# Feature — RMS node map layer: nearest Winlink RMS node by real GPS position (Tuxlink ⇄ Geographica)

**Captured:** 2026-05-22 by `hemlock-arroyo-mink` · **Status:** captured, not designed. Future feature. **A Tuxlink feature that touches both Tuxlink and Geographica in equal measure** — Tuxlink owns the CMS-side data + the nearest-node logic; Geographica owns the offline-map render. (Operator correction: Geographica does NOT poll the CMS — Tuxlink does.)

## Origin

Operator-relayed ask (quoted):

> "Has anyone figured out how to add Winlink RMS nodes as a map layer to either YAAC or Navit? I know that the pat winlink mode will show distance. But to my understanding, the distance calculation is made from the center of the grid square stored in ET-user. This is totally acceptable for HF modes, but is more important for VHF packet or Vara FM. I also have a GPS unit. So it'd be nice to pull up an offline map and see the node that is actually closest to me."

Operator's note: **"We can already do this if we have access to the WL2K RMS node list, which is a thing you can request from the CMS."**

## Value proposition

Pat computes RMS distance from the **center of the user's own grid square** (the `ET-user` entry). With a GPS unit, the operator knows their **precise** position. Using precise GPS as the distance origin — instead of grid-square-center — is:

- **Acceptable to skip for HF** (long range; grid-center error is negligible relative to skip distance).
- **Materially better for VHF packet / VARA FM** (short, near-line-of-sight range; a grid-square is ~mi across, so grid-center error can flip which node is genuinely closest/reachable).

Deliverable: pull up an **offline** map and see the RMS node **actually closest to me**, by real GPS position.

## Architecture — who owns what

**Tuxlink owns (the data + logic half):**
- Fetch the **WL2K RMS node list from the CMS** (callsign, frequency, mode, grid square / position, possibly hours/service). Operator confirms this list is requestable from the CMS.
- Compute nearest-node distance from the operator's **precise GPS position** (not grid-center) to each node.
- Leverages the **position subsystem just landed in `tuxlink-686` / PR #109** (Maidenhead ↔ lat/lon conversion, distance) — that's the enabling piece. This feature should take a `bd dep` on tuxlink-686.

**Geographica owns (the render half):**
- Offline base map + render the RMS node set as a **map layer / POI overlay**.
- Highlight the nearest node; show the operator's GPS position.

The cross-project boundary: Tuxlink produces a node dataset (+ the operator's position); Geographica consumes it as a layer. When the Geographica half is built, track it in Geographica's own tracker with a dep back to this issue (sibling repos run from their own session roots).

## Correctness / posture notes

- **GPS precision:** the project's precision-reduction default (broadcast position → 4-char Maidenhead by default; opt-in to higher precision) governs **what you transmit**. This nearest-node calc is **local-only** — using **full GPS precision** for it is correct and does NOT conflict with that posture. A future implementer must not down-reduce precision for the local distance calc.
- **Node-position precision:** the win comes from precise *user* position. The *nodes'* positions may themselves only be grid squares in the CMS list — acceptable (nodes are fixed; the dominant error source the operator is complaining about is the user-side grid-center origin).

## Open items to verify when picked up

- **Exact CMS request mechanism** for the RMS node list, and the fields it returns (does it carry precise lat/lon, or grid square only?). Verify against Winlink docs — do not assume (ham-radio specifics are a known-unreliable area).
- **Does `tuxlink-pat` already expose RMS-list retrieval?** Upstream Pat has RMS-list functionality (`pat` gateway/`rmslist`-class features); the fork may already provide the fetch, reducing this to parse + compute + hand-off. Verify in the fork.
- **List freshness / caching** for true offline use (cache last-fetched list on disk; refresh when online).
- **Geographica ingestion format** for a custom POI/node layer (GeoJSON? waypoint set?).
- **Prior art (the quoted open question):** has anyone done RMS-node map layers in **YAAC** or **Navit**? Research when picked up — may inform the layer format and UX.
notes: Durable capture committed as docs/design/rms-node-map-feature.md via PR #112 (branch bd-tuxlink-10o/rms-feature-note-durable -> main). The dev/scratch copy is a local convenience; the tracked doc is canonical. NOTE: bd issues.jsonl is not currently reaching origin (no dolt remote; last jsonl commit ~AMD-1 era) — this issue itself is local-only until a bd-sync lands.

title: Frequency/rig control plane: single source of truth tying station + frequency (research)
description: v0.1 ships packet 'Frequency' as a NOTE-ONLY field (operator tunes the radio manually). Future vision (operator Cameron, 2026-05-22 AX.25 brainstorm): a single source-of-truth control plane that binds the target station + frequency + mode together for an RF session, so the client and radio cannot mismatch. A client/radio frequency mismatch can cause real harmful interference (transmitting on the wrong freq); a unified control surface focuses operator attention and prevents that class of error. Cameron has done freq-coordinated calling manually and wants the tool to own it eventually. SCOPE OF RESEARCH (heavy): rig-control protocols (Hamlib/rigctld, CAT per-radio differences incl. the G90), how to set freq/mode safely from tuxlink, interlocks/confirmation to prevent accidental wrong-freq TX, and how channel definitions (callsign+freq+mode, per the Winlink channel-data model) map to a tuxlink 'channel/control-plane' concept. Deliverable of the research pass: a feasibility + design note and a go/no-go. Depends on the v0.1 packet feature landing first.
notes: LOCKED 2026-05-24 (operator) — narrows this research. Rig control is now committed to be its OWN CRATE from the start (tux-rig: trait Ptt/SetFreq/SetMode/ReadStatus + Hamlib as the FIRST backend), NOT baked into client internals. Confirmed origin/main has NO rig control today (no hamlib/rigctl/ptt; only serialport for KISS). CONSUMERS: ARDOP full single-pane (freq+PTT) and the future clean-sheet first-party modem — which FORCES rig control regardless, since the modem is tuxlinks own code with no external modem to offload PTT to. SPIN-OFF NOTE: if the modem ships as a standalone TCP daemon for non-tuxlink clients, rig control relocates INTO the modem; the shared-crate factoring lets BOTH the modem daemon and the client link it (build-once survives the split). Fits the emerging tuxlink-as-RF-process-manager pattern (ardopcf/direwolf/rigctld all spawn+supervise+SIGINT). RESEARCH NOW NARROWS to the still-open HOW: Hamlib backend form (libhamlib FFI vs managed rigctld subprocess vs minimal own-CAT), wrong-freq-TX interlocks/confirmation, and channel-data (callsign+freq+mode) -> control-plane mapping. Full context: docs/design/ardop-deployment-findings.md (Locked decisions + Forward-looking sections).

title: ADR 0014: clean-sheet v0.5+ modem — no prior-art (VARA) examination, preserve independent-creation defense
description: Record (Nygard ADR) the 2026-05-22 decision to design the v0.5+ HF modem fully clean-sheet WITHOUT examining VARA's internals from any source (decompilation, leaked source, third-party RE write-ups, or black-box on-air characterization), in order to preserve the independent-creation copyright defense rather than trade it for the harder clean-room defense. Reinforces ADR-era posture in project memory (project-v05-modem-design-posture). Deliverable: docs/adr/0014-*.md on feat/v0.0.1 via PR + README index entry. The ADR is itself the contemporaneous 'document non-access' paper trail that makes independent creation defensible. Note: patents are a separate strict-liability axis (independent creation is NOT a defense) — flag an IP-attorney patent check before any production, out of agent scope.

Worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-98g-clean-sheet-modem-adr bound to this issue; dispose per ADR 0009 ritual after PR merges. Close this issue on merge.

title: Attachment shrink-to-fit: operator-controlled source-byte reducer for low-bandwidth sends (post-v0.0.1)
description:
# Feasibility note — Attachment shrink-to-fit / source-byte reducer (post-v0.0.1)

**Captured:** 2026-05-21 by `hemlock-arroyo-mink` · **Status:** research captured, NOT designed. Revisit when attachments are prioritized (clearly post-v0.0.1 — attachments are list-add-only today).

**Origin:** Operator question — sending PDFs/images over 1200-baud packet and VARA HF is bandwidth-limited; would an attachment compressor help, and could a Pi do it? Research pass run; this note is the durable capture so the analysis isn't lost.

---

## The reframe that governs everything

**Winlink already compresses every attachment losslessly in transit.** The B2 Forwarding Protocol (B2F) bundles the body + all attachments (up to 5 messages) and runs them through **LZHUF** (the FBB B1 compressor) before transmission. It deliberately runs the modem in "MODE 0" — no native modem compression — so VARA/Pactor/packet add nothing on top in the Winlink path.

**Consequence:** "beat .zip" is the wrong frame. Zip and LZHUF are both general-purpose lossless archivers. Running one over an already-entropy-maximized JPEG/PNG/PDF buys ~5% or worse and can inflate it with container overhead. Re-zipping before send is near-useless. **The only lever that helps is reducing SOURCE bytes before B2F sees them: resize, lossy re-encode, transcode to a denser codec, or substitute a leaner format.**

## Binding constraint — recipient decodability

The typical Winlink recipient runs **Winlink Express on Windows** (or Pat, or a webmail gateway). The Winlink Express picture editor handles only **jpeg, gif, png, bmp, tiff**. Modern dense codecs (**WebP / AVIF / JPEG XL / HEIC**) are NOT reliably decodable by a stock Windows recipient and are effectively non-deliverable for general recipients.

**Universally-safe delivery formats:** JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, TIFF (images); PDF, plain text / CSV / RTF (documents). JPEG is the safest lossy-photo target.

This is why **jpegli** is the sweet spot for photos: modern-encoder-class savings (~35% smaller than libjpeg at equal quality) while the output is still a *normal JPEG* any ancient decoder opens.

---

## The four questions, answered

### 1. Can we do meaningfully better than .zip?
Yes, by a wide margin — but not as a better archiver. Zip barely helps (the wire already runs LZHUF). The wins come from source reduction:

| Lever | Saving vs. original | Recipient-safe? |
|---|---|---|
| Resize + re-encode JPEG (target-KB) | 5–20× (dominant for photos) | yes (JPEG) |
| jpegli / mozjpeg encoder | extra ~35% at equal quality, output still JPEG | yes |
| WebP / AVIF / JPEG XL | 25–55% smaller than JPEG | mostly NO |
| PDF DPI downsample (Ghostscript `/screen`) | MB → tens of KB for scans | yes (still PDF) |
| Format substitution (XLSX→CSV, DOCX→RTF/TXT) | 5×+ | yes |
| JBIG2 / CCITT-G4 / DjVu (scanned text) | 10–50× | DjVu no; CCITT-G4-in-PDF/TIFF yes |

### 2. Can the Pi do it without falling over?
Yes, with codec discipline. The risk is one specific codec, not the workload.
- **Use libvips** for resize/decode/encode — streams in tiles, ~constant small RAM regardless of image dimensions. Single most important "don't take the Pi down" choice.
- **Avoid libaom AVIF as default** (~47× slower than WebP; ~20 s/MP at slow presets — this is the CPU hog). **Avoid guetzli entirely** (~90 s, ~300 MB RAM for one 1-MP image).
- Fast + safe on ARM: jpegli/mozjpeg (~300 ms/MP), cwebp (near-JPEG speed), Ghostscript for PDFs.
- A single interactive "shrink this attachment" on a Pi 5 with libvips + jpegli finishes sub-second for a typical photo. Cap encoder threads, one attachment at a time.

### 3. How out of scope is auto-".jpg make it fit"?
Not wildly — it's the most-precedented, smallest viable slice. **Winlink Express already ships exactly this**: a Resize dialog that takes a target KB and re-encodes to hit it (documented example: 83.5 KB → 30.2 KB at target → 7.0 KB after tight crop). The algorithm is a bounded **binary search over (scale factor, JPEG quality)** to land just under a target KB, with a live before/after preview. Scope grows only if "fit under the limit" expands beyond photos (see scope tiers).

### 4. Clever methods worth knowing
- **Format substitution is the community's #1 document trick** (EmComm training: "save XLSX→CSV cuts size 5×+, DOCX→RTF/TXT; do NOT attach the original spreadsheet"). An "attachment lint" advisor could beat any codec for office docs.
- **Winlink already does template/delta encoding for forms** — ICS-213/Radiogram are tiny XML field-data attachments rendered against a local template library at the receiver, not rendered images. Generalizable lever for forms-heavy EmComm: send field values + template ID. Biggest conceptual win for the EmComm domain.
- **The best compression is often not sending the image** — OCR a scanned text page to plain text (KB vs. tens-of-KB); send "2-story structure fire, NE corner, ~30% involved" as text rather than a photo. Matches EmComm doctrine.
- Progressive/thumbnail-first transmission (app convention, not protocol — Winlink is store-and-forward). Vector-tracing line art (potrace→SVG, but SVG only recipient-safe wrapped in PDF).

## Scope tiers (for when this is revisited)
1. **Photo shrink-to-fit only** — operator dialog, target-KB, libvips+jpegli binary search, before/after preview. Smallest, highest-precedent, lowest integrity risk. Delivers most day-to-day value.
2. **Photos + documents** — adds Ghostscript PDF DPI-downsample + "leaner format" nudges. Medium. Covers bulk of real EmComm attachment traffic.
3. **Full content-adaptive system** — classifier routes photo / line-art / scanned-doc to the right codec; OCR-to-text; possibly template/delta for forms. Large, research-flavored, biggest payoff and biggest integrity-risk surface.

## Integrity traps to manage
- Lossy JBIG2 symbol-substitution can silently swap digits (the Xerox scanning bug) — for EmComm where numbers matter, use lossless JBIG2 / CCITT-G4 only.
- OCR errors on poor scans; over-aggressive lossy recompression of critical content.
- Keep the operator in the loop (matches the project's operator-controlled framing) — never silently transform an EmComm-critical attachment.

## Provenance / honesty flags
- Q1, Q2, and the prior-art in Q4 are anchored on Winlink **primary docs** read directly (B2F spec, Winlink Express picture-editor slides, EmComm-Training exercise PDFs).
- Soft spots, not overstated: (a) one secondary note questioned whether Winlink also disables VARA's internal Huffman — does not change the LZHUF conclusion; (b) there is NO canonical ham "always 320px / quality 40" rule — the real convention is target-KB-driven, which Winlink Express confirms.
- Operator (Cameron) has operated VARA HF on a G90 firsthand — his sense of real-world throughput floors overrides any specific bps figure from a web source.

## Key sources
- Winlink B2F protocol spec: https://winlink.org/B2F
- LZHUF compression source: https://github.com/ARSFI/Winlink-Compression
- RMS Express picture editing (target-KB resize, supported formats): https://philsherrod.com/Winlink/RMS_Express_Picture_Editing.pdf
- EmComm-Training, file attachments / format substitution: https://emcomm-training.org/Winlink-Thursday__File-Attachments-rev.1.1.pdf
- Winlink FAQ (120 KB cap): https://winlink.org/sites/default/files/download/wl2k_faq_5.pdf
- jpegli (standard-JPEG-compatible, ~35% smaller): https://opensource.googleblog.com/2024/04/introducing-jpegli-new-jpeg-coding-library.html
- AVIF encode-cost benchmarks: https://webp2png.co/blog/avif-encoding-performance-benchmarks
- JBIG2 (ratios + digit-swap caveat): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JBIG2
- MRC compression for PDF: https://www.dynamsoft.com/blog/imaging/mrc-compression-pdf/
- Ghostscript PDF optimization: https://ghostscript.com/blog/optimizing-pdfs.html

title: Revisit compose as an in-window docked panel (Gmail-style) — Wayland blocks separate-window dock
description: ng3 smoke #8: operator wanted compose docked bottom-right like Gmail. A separate Tauri window CANNOT be positioned client-side on the Pi's labwc/Wayland (compositor owns placement), so it lands centered; that same separate webview is also slow to open (re-parses the full bundle). The in-window docked panel (a non-modal React panel pinned bottom-right inside the main window) is the only design that delivers BOTH the dock and fast open — it supersedes AMD-6's separate-window decision and retires compose_window.rs/routing/self-close. Deferred per operator: accept centered for v0.0.1; REVISIT if users complain. Subsumes the compose-open-perf issue. Needs its own brainstorm + plan.

title: Wizard test-send: correlate reply via per-send nonce (eliminate residual SERVICE false-positive)
description: Codex pqg adrev R2 #2 (P2): is_autoresponder_reply matches sender SERVICE@winlink.org only. With the fresh isolated mbox this is unambiguous EXCEPT when a SERVICE message is already pending on the CMS for the callsign (or, on the PAT_URL path, old SERVICE mail in an existing mbox) — that would report success before THIS /test/ reply arrives. Robust fix: embed a per-send nonce in the test subject and require the reply to reference it. BLOCKED on confirming firsthand whether the Winlink SERVICE autoresponder echoes the subject in its Re: reply (do NOT guess Winlink internals — see feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability). Operator can confirm during the pqg live round-trip.

title: Session-log drain: surface dropped lines when the 500-ring evicts between polls
description: Codex R3 on tuxlink-22l (fix review, 2026-05-20, finding #1). The session-log drain (bootstrap.rs start_drain) polls the durable SessionLogState via snapshot_since(last_seq) every 250ms. If Pat emits >500 stderr lines within one poll interval, the bounded 500-line ring evicts the oldest before the drain reads them, so those lines are SILENTLY skipped (the cursor jumps past them; no double-emit, no crash — just silent loss under extreme burst). This is an inherent property of a bounded ring + a polling consumer; Pat startup is a handful of lines and >2000 lines/sec sustained is extreme, so the v0.0.1 practical risk is low. Polish (v0.1): detect a seq gap (first.seq > last_seq+1 after a poll) and emit a synthetic '[N lines dropped]' marker so loss is VISIBLE rather than silent; or raise the ring cap / poll faster; or add a non-evicting notify queue. Visibility improvement, not a correctness fix.

title: Compose-window capability over-privilege: allow-close/allow-destroy are window-class (can target other windows)
description: Codex P3 (integration-fix re-round, b6331de). compose.json grants core:window:allow-close + allow-destroy. Tauri's window JS sends a caller-supplied label for close/destroy, resolved server-side via manager().get_window(label), so these window-class permissions are NOT scoped to 'self' — a compromised/XSS'd compose window could close/destroy OTHER windows (e.g. main). Normal operation is safe (compose only calls getCurrentWindow().close(); the main-only Rust guard on compose_window_open prevents recursive spawn). allow-destroy is genuinely needed (onCloseRequested uses it when default not prevented), so it can't simply be removed. Bounded surface: close/destroy only — no data exfil or transmission. Fix options: (a) route window-close through a custom Rust command that closes ONLY the calling window and drop the window-class grants from the compose capability; (b) accept the bounded surface as a documented v0.0.1 risk. Hardening; requires an XSS to exploit. Deferred from the integration commit by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #98 OPEN. Implemented option (a): compose_close_self command (self-only destroy) + dropped window-class allow-close/allow-destroy from compose.json + routed both close paths through it. Removes the risk rather than accepting it. Gates green. OPERATOR GUI SMOKE is the verification gate (close flows runtime-only). Closes on #98 merge.

title: Harden compose_window_open: validate/encode draft_id before using in window label + route
description: Codex P2 (integration-commit round, 266353a). compose_window_open uses draft_id directly in both the window label (compose-<draftId>) and the route without encoding/validation (compose_window.rs:51). The frontend routing.ts parser handles encoded/missing/trailing-slash/malformed paths safely, and the normal menu path uses safe generated IDs (newDraftId), so this is NOT the normal path — but an IPC caller passing '/', '?', '#', or malformed '%' could create a label/URL that doesn't mount the intended draft. Fix: validate draft_id (charset/length) and/or percent-encode before label+route construction; reject invalid. v0.0.1-safe (generated IDs only), so hardening. Deferred from the integration commit by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #99 OPEN (stacked on #98). Added validate_draft_id (pure, TDD'd) + call in compose_window_open. Gates green. Closes on #99 merge (retarget base to feat/v0.0.1 after #98 merges).

title: Wizard MOCKED banner: absent-while-mocked if is_mocked query errors or races a fast mock return
description: Codex Part-97 re-round of b45f592 (P2 visibility, safe-direction failure — no transmission risk). The frontend fails the wizard_test_send_is_mocked query to false (Step3TestSend.tsx:92), so a query error makes the MOCKED banner ABSENT while actually mocked; a fast mocked send can also return before the query resolves. Spec §3.8 wants the banner visible during 'sending' in mock mode so the operator can confirm zero live transmission. Normal local operation shows the banner fine (M1 smoke unaffected). Fix: treat query error as assume-mocked-in-dev / render a pending state, or drive the banner from the same signal the mocked outcome carries. Deferred from M1 by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #101 OPEN (stacked on #97). Query is_mocked on mount (static signal) instead of per-sending-entry → fixes the fast-mock-return race; idle-reset dropped; error stays fail-safe (live). Frontend-only. Gates green (vitest 307). Closes on #101 merge.

title: Task 11 live-path failure serialization: WizardError::Other{detail:json} should be structured TestSendOutcome::Failed
description: Codex P2 from Task 11 review (carried, not fixed in b45f592). Live test-send failures (and the timeout path) are serialized as WizardError::Other{detail: <JSON string>} in run_test_send_impl / live_path_outcome_to_wizard_error; the frontend does not parse that JSON and renders 'Unexpected error: {json}'. The failed substate still appears, but operator copy is degraded — should be a structured TestSendOutcome::Failed mapped to the spec's failure copy. Live-path only (not exercised in MOCK mode or M1 smoke). Deferred by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20) to keep the consent-state P0 fix tight.
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #97 OPEN (stacked on #95). Live test-send failures return Ok(TestSendOutcome::Failed) instead of Err(Other{json}); deleted the JSON encoder (folds Codex pqg R1 P2#7). No frontend logic change. Gates green. Closes on #97 merge (retarget base to feat/v0.0.1 after #95 merges).

title: Reading pane: enforce read-side byte cap before buffering full HTTP response (Task 13 Codex finding 3)
description: Codex post-subagent review of Task 13 (bd-tuxlink-y5c) found the 2 MiB cap is enforced inside parse_raw_rfc5322 — AFTER message_read awaits backend.read_message_in and PatClient::read buffers the entire HTTP response via resp.bytes().await (pat_client.rs:166). The UI cannot bypass the parser cap, but a buggy/oversized Pat endpoint can force unbounded memory/network work before the cap trips. Pat is trusted localhost so risk is low; this is v0.1 upstream-cap hardening (stream + early-abort on Content-Length / chunk count). Only exercised once live Pat bootstrap (tuxlink-22l) flows real messages. Deferred from the y5c merge per orchestrator triage (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-f1a-read-byte-cap. Branch: bd-tuxlink-f1a/read-byte-cap. Created 2026-05-21 04:06:46 UTC by gully-sage-heron.

title: cred-handling spec: use_native_store(true) is stale for keyring 3.6.3
description: Task 10 keyring fix (PR #75, commit 5f269d9) found keyring 3.6.3 has NO use_native_store() fn — backend selection is compile-time via Cargo features (default-features=false + ['sync-secret-service','crypto-rust']). The cred-handling spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-cred-handling-design.md (line ~80 architecture diagram + line ~209 note) still prescribes keyring::use_native_store(true)?, stale keyring-core terminology. Also: the real Secret Service attribute key is 'username' (not 'account') for the Rust keyring crate, matching Pat zalando go-keyring's read — worth documenting the verified cross-process attribute contract. Doc-only fix.
notes: 2026-05-20 gully-sage-heron: PR #102 OPEN (doc-only, independent). Corrected stale keyring-core/use_native_store framing to keyring 3.6.3 (compile-time Cargo features) across the §3.3 diagram, §3.4 steps 3-4, and the R3 F5 decision-log row; documented the username-vs-account Secret Service attribute contract. Closes on #102 merge.

title: Tauri webview shows TV-static / GPU artifacts on first launch (Pi 5, Wayland, webkit2gtk-4.1)
description:
Symptom: every time `pnpm tauri dev` (and presumably `pnpm tauri build` AppImage) launches the Tauri window on the dev Pi 5, the window appears as TV-static / uninitialized-GPU-memory graphical artifacts. Any click or window manipulation triggers a repaint and the page content appears normally.

This is an upstream webkit2gtk + Wayland + Mesa V3D (Pi 5 VideoCore VII GPU) class of bug — the WebKit GPU compositor allocates the webview surface before the first frame is painted, so the window shows whatever's in uninitialized GPU memory until an event forces a repaint.

NOT introduced by tuxlink code. Has been present since the Tauri scaffold was added (commit 52d4181, 2026-04-22). Just easier to notice now after Task 7's menu work made the operator stare at the window carefully.

Mitigation env vars to test (least invasive first):
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 — disables WebKit's DMA-BUF rendering path, known fix for several Wayland-render bugs
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 — disables GPU compositing entirely, software fallback
- GDK_BACKEND=x11 — falls back to XWayland; nuclear option

If any mitigation works, bake it into:
- The dev experience (env var in package.json scripts or .env.local equivalent)
- The release build (tauri.conf.json's runtime config or AppImage launcher script)

For Task 17 (AppImage packaging, tuxlink-cs7): the release artifact MUST ship with the working mitigation env var pre-set so end users don't see TV-static on their Pi/Linux desktops.

Acceptance:
- Operator verifies one of the env vars eliminates the TV-static symptom on their Pi 5
- Working env var is documented in development.md
- AppImage build (Task 17) bakes the env var into the launcher

Priority P3 (polish — doesn't block functionality; v0.0.1 ships with or without this fix per operator call).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-wfw-fix-webkit-dmabuf. Branch: bd-tuxlink-wfw/fix-webkit-dmabuf. Created 2026-05-19 21:40:23 UTC by willow-cypress-heron.
close_reason: WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 set in-Rust at run() startup via PR #73; operator-verified clean launch, no regression

title: Public integration API (REST or similar) for agent/automation use — nice-to-have
description:
Discussed during early potential-user conversations: operators want to drive Tuxlink from custom integrations / agents over an HTTP API. No such surface exists today — the only HTTP server in the codebase is forms/http_server.rs (lazy 127.0.0.1:0 axum bound to a per-open token, scoped to serving form-webview content). Every other Tauri↔backend call goes through invoke() IPC, in-process only.

Design considerations the spec will need to cover:
- RADIO-1 / Part 97 gate: TX-triggering endpoints (connect, send, abort) cannot bypass per-invocation operator consent. Likely splits into a read-only tier (mailbox listing, message fetch, search, status) that's unconstrained and a write/transport tier with interactive consent gates.
- Auth model: bearer token issued by the desktop UI? OS keyring? Per-session token?
- Binding scope: localhost-only by default; opt-in to LAN with an obvious safety prompt.
- Schema discoverability: OpenAPI doc + auto-generated TypeScript client?
- Lifecycle: lazy-start when first opt-in, teardown on app quit.
- Starting skeleton: the forms/http_server.rs pattern (bind + token + Tauri lifecycle) is a reasonable structural reference, but the routes + auth + scope tiers all need fresh design.

Not a release blocker. Tracked for future planning — file design spec when prioritized.

title: Self-generated weather reports from APRS WX + satellite (GOES) ingest
description:
Idea capture (fen-alder-bog, 2026-05-22). Far-future, exploratory — a wild departure from the core 'simple email client' framing, recorded so it isn't lost.

VISION
Ingest APRS weather-station data and combine it with ingested raw satellite imagery/data (GOES or similar) to generate and distribute Tuxlink's OWN weather reports — rather than only relaying others'. Local AI is a candidate for the synthesis/report-generation step.

ARCHITECTURE SKETCH (operator's, verbatim intent)
- Background service requiring AT MINIMUM two dedicated SDRs:
    * SDR #1 — APRS weather-station data ingest.
    * SDR #2 — satellite receive (GOES or similar).
- A dedicated middleware service ingests + parses both data streams.
- Middleware exposes the parsed/combined data to a Tuxlink daemon at a localhost port.

NOTES
- 'It could be done, though.' — operator. Recorded as feasible-but-unscoped; no commitment, no timeline.
- Not yet through office-hours / brainstorming. Promote to a design exploration before any build work.
notes: Hardware dependency: 2+ dedicated SDRs (one APRS WX, one satellite RX). RF receive-only ingest — distinct from the transmit paths gated by RADIO-1. Distribution step (how reports go out) is unspecified and is where Part 97 / RADIO-1 transmission rules would re-enter scope. Local-AI synthesis ties to the existing 'leverage local AI' thread.

PROVEN ON-AIR 2026-05-22 (Cameron): BTECH UV-Pro KISS-TNC over Bluetooth = classic RFCOMM/SPP (NOT BLE-GATT). Connected-mode AX.25 WORKS over the UV-Pro on Linux: radio keyed, SABM transmitted, gateway W7MOT-6 answered with DM (two-way SABM-out/DM-back). DM = gateway refused at app layer (likely wrong SSID/target), NOT a transport failure — the link carried it. *** SPP RFCOMM CHANNEL IS DYNAMIC: observed 4 then 5 minutes apart on unit 38:D2:00:01:55:5C (DC6AP's VR-N76 was 1). It rotates per connect/registration — MUST read from SDP each time (sdptool records <MAC> | grep -A1 'SPP Dev' -> the Serial Port 0x1101 RFCOMM channel), NEVER hardcode. Binding a stale/wrong channel -> open connects to nothing -> 'closed' -> first write 'Broken pipe'. This makes a manual 'rfcomm bind <fixed N>' fundamentally unreliable for this radio -> strengthens the deferred in-app RFCOMM-socket transport (reads SDP channel + opens socket at connect time; non-root socket creation already confirmed uid 1000). *** Audio/headset was a RED HERRING: input.conf Disable=Headset did NOT matter, PipeWire did not grab audio; the audio-headset class coexists with SPP (operator: on Windows it's an audio device AND a COM port). ConnectionAttemptFailed at pairing was transient (retry fixed it). Setup: bluetoothctl pair+trust (radio menu Pairing) -> read SPP channel from SDP -> sudo rfcomm bind /dev/rfcomm0 <MAC> <SDP-channel> -> dial promptly (channel can rotate) -> sudo rfcomm release 0. Enable on radio: Menu>General Settings>KISS TNC. OPEN: clean message transfer (got a refusal, not yet a successful B2F) + the double-key/param bug below.

CORRECTED 2026-05-22: 52.37.245.31 = cms-z.winlink.org itself (my earlier 'stale server.winlink.org rotation IP / Tailscale MagicDNS' theory was WRONG). Probed: cms-z(52.37.245.31): 8772 plaintext OPEN (dev, accepts unregistered tuxlink SID), 8773 FILTERED (NO TLS). server.winlink.org(32.196.219.66/52.206.142.38): 8773 TLS OPEN w/ valid *.winlink.org cert but REJECTS unregistered tuxlink-0.0.1 SID ('Unknown client types... use cms-z'). NET: there is NO working dev-TLS path (cms-z=plaintext-only; prod-TLS needs the tuxlink client SID registered w/ Winlink first per project-cms-rejects-unknown-clients). 8773 IS the correct TLS port. Earlier 52.37.245.31:8773 timeouts were the app correctly dialing cms-z:8773 (no TLS), not a stale server IP.

Packet B2F end-to-end findings (tuxlink-3wh, 2026-05-22): (1) FBB master/answerer handshake = client handshake + trailing '>' on the DE line (build_master_handshake); the master reads the slave handshake terminating on a peeked 'F' line (its message turn) NOT a '>' prompt (read_slave_handshake) — grounded in wl2k-go fbb/handshake.go. (2) Ax25Stream::read returns Ok(0) for 'no data yet' (defect-J), which BufReader/read_until treat as EOF -> B2F over packet MUST go through BlockingB2fStream (winlink_backend.rs) or it prematurely aborts on RF latency. (3) A localhost TCP relay (full-duplex/lossless) MASKS RF-real bugs (premature-EOF, missing KISS params on answer()) — Codex caught all three; validate against latency + an independent KISS decoder (tuxlink-mbw), not just peer-to-peer.

tuxlink-l55l + tuxlink-su2h: in-progress on worktree /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-0pnb-tcp-p2p-telnet (path retained from prior 0pnb work to avoid worktree churn). Branch: bd-tuxlink-l55l/p2p-outbox-wiring. PR #219. Cherry-picked off origin/main after #208 merged.

Winlink B2F already LZHUF-compresses body+attachments losslessly in transit, modem runs MODE 0 (no native compression) — so 'beat .zip' is the wrong frame; re-zipping already-compressed data is a near-no-op. The only lever is reducing SOURCE bytes (resize / lossy re-encode / transcode / format-substitute) before B2F sees them. Binding constraint: recipients run Winlink Express on Windows — only jpeg/gif/png/bmp/tiff/pdf/text/csv/rtf are universally decodable; WebP/AVIF/JXL/HEIC are non-deliverable. Sweet spot for photos = libvips + jpegli (modern-encoder gains, output is still a normal JPEG). On Pi: avoid libaom-AVIF (CPU hog) and guetzli (RAM hog). Full analysis: tuxlink-pxf + dev/scratch/attachment-compression-feasibility.md (local).

Dire Wolf 1.7 (/usr/bin/direwolf) is the working Linux AGWPE/SoundModem-over-TCP TNC on pandora: AGW protocol on :8000 + KISS-over-TCP on :8001, both verified 2026-05-24 accepting+attaching a TCP client with ADEVICE null (no soundcard/radio needed). Bluetooth-free transport for transport-layer testing: tuxlink -> TCP KISS/AGW -> Dire Wolf -> soundcard -> radio. Sidesteps the UV-Pro BT Page-Timeout (tuxlink-9ky). soundmodem (Sailer) NOT installed; Dire Wolf covers it. Test config dev/scratch/dw-tcp-test.conf. See tuxlink-0mz.

P2#1 = VARA stop-state cleanup (abort_writer/abort_stream/transport_owner); P2#2 = widen modem_vara_b2f_exchange to SessionIntent + TransportKind; P2#3 = thread transport_kind through arm_vara_listener_inner; P2#4 = wire VaraStatus.listener_armed + exchange from real state. tuxlink-6c9y operator decisions (2026-06-08, adrev convergence): (1) 6c9y's connection-determined + send-time-selection routing SUPERSEDES tuxlink-u5hl's deferred compose-time routing_flag model (Pattern A) for Post Office + Network Post Office modes. Narrow the build_outbound_proposals safety gate (winlink_backend.rs:285): remove it for PostOffice/Mesh (send-time selection is the replacement leakage guard), KEEP it for P2P/RadioOnly which 6c9y does not address. u5hl residual concern = P2P/RadioOnly leakage. (2) Full inbound message selection in 6c9y v1 -> Post Office connect builds on the native telnet-exchange path (the decide-seam bsiy hooks), NOT the telnet_p2p_connect template; reuses bsiy's inbound-selection mechanism (dep added). (3) Interop: WLE transmits NO routing header on the B2F wire (X-RMS-Routing is EncodeHeader/.mime only, not B2AssembleMessage); -L login is the sole discriminator; reframe 'byte-identical' -> 'field-compatible'.
